## 2026-06-27 · Deep-research II: calibration decision / exits / crypto / execution (Chat A, owner-invoked)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-06-27_quant_engine_II.md` (new).
**Why:** owner ran /deep-research (all 4). Multi-agent run (money-engine-research-2: 129 agents, ~6.6M
tokens, ~58 min, 3-vote adversarial verify), anchored to the engine's CURRENT state.
**Headline — the parked BETA-vs-ISOTONIC decision → CONDITIONAL ADOPT (not a hard swap):** register
{isotonic, beta-3param, identity} as CANDIDATE conviction→win-prob maps and let the EXISTING purged/
embargoed OOS Brier+log-loss selector pick per refit (beta = bivariate LR on ln(s), -ln(1-s), a,b≥0,
near-unregularized; Wilson stays a separate floor on top). Beta wins on LOG-LOSS (the EV/Kelly-aligned
metric), ~on-par on Brier; the 200–1000 crossover is Platt-vs-isotonic so it's a prior, not proof →
let the selector decide. Still NO autonomous change — owner sign-off.
**Other verdicts:** EXITS — keep the ATR trailing stop but reframe as regime-gated drawdown INSURANCE,
not free alpha (stops only add expected return under momentum/positive autocorrelation; gate on the
ρ≥Sharpe hurdle; widen to Chandelier ATR(22)·3, 4–5× crypto). CRYPTO — favor short-lookback (1–4wk)
absolute-trend/ATR time-series momentum in liquid large-caps; add a live liquidity gate; model crypto
taker ~25–30bps round-trip; conditional US-scoped publication-decay haircut (~50–58%). EXECUTION —
spread+half-spread+slippage dominates at retail; add a linear→√ market-impact term (δ=0.5, recalibrate
only prefactor Y per liquidity tier) that's ~0 at retail size but bites for thin/Saudi/small-cap;
flag orders >10% ADV out-of-model + partial fills.
**17-item prioritized build checklist** in the artifact (top: register beta candidate; reframe stop
reporting; re-weight crypto momentum; reactivate the dormant full DSR for momentum). Honesty floor: all
gross→net erosion / risk-truth, no profit promise. No code changed.

---

## 2026-06-27 · Deep-research: 'make money fast / conviction' reality check (Chat A, owner-invoked)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-06-27_money_fast_conviction.md` (new).
**Why:** owner ran /deep-research 'how to make money fast conviction stocks' (refined to an honest,
failure-mode-first question). deep-research workflow: 105 agents, ~3.9M tokens, 3-vote adversarial verify.
**Verdict — validates the engine's honesty floor + core design, demolishes 'get rich fast':**
- CONCENTRATION: a manager's genuine top-few 'best ideas' DO beat (~2.8–4.5%/yr GROSS, Antón-Cohen-Polk)
  but it's gross, skews high-vol, ordinary investors don't capture it; 'all ideas' alpha ≈ 0. The stronger
  'concentration doubles Sharpe' / 'Active Share predicts net' claims were REFUTED in verification
  (Frazzini 'Deactivating Active Share').
- FELT CONVICTION = mostly overconfidence + self-defeating for retail: bought stocks underperform sold
  3.2pp/yr (8.6pp for speculative); most active traders 11.4% net vs 17.9% market; >80% of day traders
  lose net, <1% persistently skilled. Skill is predicted by PAST REALIZED PERFORMANCE, not stated
  conviction — exactly why the engine calibrates conviction→win-rate from outcomes + validates OOS.
- SIZING: full Kelly maximizes growth but is wild; overbetting ≥2x → ruin (LTCM); fractional-Kelly
  POSITION LIMITS compound without blowing up — validates the half-Kelly allocator + heat cap.
- COST is the binding constraint (gross edge real, destroyed by frictions) — validates the NetEdge cost
  gate. Closet-indexing also loses (fees on no differentiation).
**Engine implication:** no new build mandated — this is a STRONG external validation of the existing
design (measured calibration over felt conviction, fractional Kelly, cost gate, 'not a forecast'
honesty). Best owner-facing message: the edge is small, gross, and cost-/discipline-gated — size small,
trust measured win-rates not gut conviction. No code changed. (The 'ideas card' /deep-research was
dismissed by the owner — not run.)

---

## 2026-06-27 · Idea-generation code AUDIT (StockSageAdvisor.advise) (#3, Chat A, owner-invoked)
**Files audited (no change):** `StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `StockSageIndicators.swift`,
`StockSageStore.swift` (buildIdeas).
**How an idea is generated:** advise(closes,highs,lows,volumes,benchmark) builds a directional additive
score (~−1..+1) → action/conviction/stop/target. Signal weights: TREND (SMA50/200 alignment) ±0.40
(heaviest; ±0.15 above-200 only; ±0.20 50-only <200 bars); 6-mo momentum ±0.15; MACD hist ±0.10
(deliberately lighter — redundant w/ trend); regime-conditional RSI (range: <30 +0.25 ONLY if 12-1
uptrend intact = falling-knife guard, >70 −0.25; trend: >80 −0.10, <20 +0.10); volume-confirm ±0.05
nudge; vol-adjusted-momentum ±0.05; relative-strength vs ^GSPC ±0.08 (equities only). TREND-FAMILY CAP
de-double-counts the correlated trend terms. TSMOM 12-1 own-downtrend VETO −0.20 (crash protection).
Regime = efficiencyRatio≥0.30. Action thresholds ±0.2/±0.5; chop downgrades trend-buys to Avoid.
conviction=min(|score|,1). Stops vol-scaled ATR (1.5/2.0/2.5×), 2:1 target; sizing half-Kelly (prior W,
R from stop/target) capped at 1% risk + vol-targeted.
**Strengths (already evidence-aligned):** trend-heaviest; MACD de-weighted as redundant; the trend-family
cap (uncommon, correct anti-overfit); regime-conditional MR + falling-knife guard; RS = OUTperformance;
TSMOM crash veto; vol-scaled risk; honest gating (Hold/Avoid get no trade plan).
**Gaps / candidates (validate vs the running idea-generation research wm08djlag before building):**
(1) **BREAKOUT/DONCHIAN/52-wk-high is BUILT-BUT-DORMANT** — `isBreakout`/`donchian`/`timeSeriesMomentum`
exist + are unit-tested but are NEVER wired into advise(); a documented momentum edge with a ready
implementation hook (highest-value candidate). (2) 12-1 momentum is used only as a NEGATIVE veto, not a
positive signal. (3) weights are hand-tuned constants (by fixed-rule design; downstream calibration
partly corrects). (4) mean-reversion is RSI-only. (5) cross-sectional ranking is downstream (rankByEV),
not a signal input beyond the ±0.08 RS term.
**Decision:** flagged, NOT changed — a new signal's WEIGHT should come from the pending research and
idea-generation changes are owner-facing (no autonomous swap). Pairs with wm08djlag when it lands.

**2026-06-27** · ITER1: calibrated half-Kelly threaded into `suggestedWeight` via pure `StockSageAdvisor.suggestedWeight(…,calibration:)` helper + `buildIdeas(calibration:)` recompute. Extracted the inline weight math from `advise()` verbatim into the new helper (nil calibration → byte-identical linear prior); `buildIdeas` now accepts an optional calibration and rebuilds `TradeAdvice.suggestedWeight` off the calibrated win-prob when one is available; `refreshIdeas` reads `convictionCalibration` on the main actor before the await hop and passes it in; backtester (`StockSageBacktester.swift`) is untouched. Files: `StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `StockSageStore.swift`, new `Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibratedSizingTests.swift` (6 tests). Why: the conservative linear prior understates edge when real calibration data exists — calibrated sizing closes that gap while keeping the backtester deterministic. Result: typecheck green (exit 0).

**2026-06-27** · ITER1 finalize — code-review fixes applied. (1) `retryFailedIdeas()` now reads `convictionCalibration` on the main actor before the `await` hop and passes both `benchmark:` and `calibration:` to `buildIdeas`, eliminating the mixed-sizing board that occurred when retried symbols were merged back. (2) `testCalibratedWeightDivergesFromPrior` replaced with `testCalibratedWinProbExceedsPrior` — tests the intermediate `winProbEstimate` rather than the cap-dominated final weight (the riskPerTrade cap provably dominates at R=2.0, making the original weight comparison trivially equal). (3) `highWinCalibration()` converted from `fatalError` to `throws` + `#require` so a future `fit` nil surfaces as a legible per-test failure. (4) Test comment corrected: `vol 0.20 ≤ 0.25 → scaler = 1.0` → `max(1, 0.20/0.20) = 1.0 (vol at baseline, floor applies)`. (5) `testNonBuyActionSizesZero` renamed `testNeutralActionsReturnZeroWeight` and expanded with sell-family assertions confirming `.sell`/`.reduce` produce nonzero weights on valid short setups. Files: `StockSageStore.swift`, `StockSageCalibratedSizingTests.swift`. Result: ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (all tests green).

**2026-06-27** · ITER3 test-gate: cleared all synthesis must-fix items and ran `driver.sh test` → ✅ TEST SUCCEEDED (full suite, parallel). Root causes found and fixed:

1. **BLOCKER-1 (ownDowntrendVetoesALongScore):** Rewritten to reflect ITER3's removal of the binary veto string. The test now asserts (a) trendOK==false on vShape is still detected, (b) the old "12-1 downtrend" rationale string is correctly absent, and (c) the action is not sell/avoid on a strong-recovery fixture. (`StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`)

2. **BLOCKER-2 (varianceScalar_momentumCrash_attenuates):** The `momentumCrash(300)` fixture's realized vol is ≈10-13% (below the 20% target) because daily moves of -2/bar on a ~300 base are <1%. The test was asserting a false premise. Replaced with direct unit-level calls at synthetic vol=0.40, 0.30, 0.25 to pin the closed-form scalar math (target/realized). The non-buy assertion on momentumCrash is retained but attributed correctly (structural downtrend signals, not the scalar). (`StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`)

3. **BLOCKER-3 (behavioral contract):** Added `varianceScalar_lowVolDowntrend_scalarIsNoOp` using a gentle monotone decline (200→44 over 260 bars, vol≈4.8%) that confirms trendOK==false AND scalar==1.0 simultaneously, documenting the intentional ITER3 trade-off: low-vol downtrends are not attenuated by the scalar. (`StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`)

4. **ITER3 behavioral regressions in existing tests (discovered during gate run):**
   - `varianceScalar_cleanUptrend_stillStrongBuy`: switched from `SageFix.cleanUptrend(260)` (linear +1/bar → MACD sign-noise ≈ -1.8e-15) to `TrendFixtures.up(260)` (quadratic, genuinely positive MACD). (`StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`)
   - `cleanDowntrendIsASellShortSetup`: `TrendFixtures.down(250)` has vol ≈ 65% (late-stage accelerating returns), causing ITER3 scalar to attenuate the bearish family below the .sell threshold. Replaced with a gentle linear decline (200→50.1, vol≈4.8%), and the assertion now accepts .sell or .reduce. (`StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`)
   - `cleanUptrendProducesWinningTargetTrades` and `adverseGapFillIsWorseThanACleanStop`: `(1...600)` / `(1...202)` fixtures start at price=1, making early log returns enormous (ln(2/1)=69%), inflating annualized vol to ≈75% and causing ITER3 scalar to suppress buy signals entirely. Fixed by starting at price=100 (daily returns ≈1%, vol ≈15% < 20%, scalar=1.0). (`StockSageBacktestTests.swift`)

5. **Minor advisor comment fix:** updated stale comment about "close-only callers are unchanged" to clarify that the variance scalar (ITER3) DOES affect close-only callers when vol > 20%. Replaced `(realizedVol ?? 0)` dead nil-branch in rationale string with `let vol = realizedVol` (non-nil invariant by branch guard). (`StockSageAdvisor.swift`)

Files changed: `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageBacktestTests.swift`. Result: `driver.sh test` → ✅ TEST SUCCEEDED. Commit: see ideas-card/iter3-tsmom-variance-scaling branch.

**2026-06-27 — ITER4 Platt calibration: biased-intercept clamp fix (BLOCKER)**
Fixed a correctness bug in `StockSageConvictionCalibration.fitPlatt`: when Newton converges to A>0 (inverted/anti-correlated sample) and the slope is clamped to A=0, the intercept B was left at the co-fitted Newton value — producing a flat win probability far above the true base rate (e.g. 88.5% vs 50% on a symmetric inverted fixture). This inflated probability flowed into Kelly EV for every conviction band, causing over-sizing across the entire ideas list. Fix: when A>0 clamp fires, also reset B to `ln((N-+1)/(N++1))` (the Platt prior log-odds / B_init) so the flat output equals the smoothed base rate, not a biased constant. Also strengthened `plattInvertedSampleClampsToMonotone` in the test suite: added two `#expect` assertions that pin the flat output to ≈0.5 prior (these would have caught the bug; they fail before the fix, pass after). Files changed: `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`. Result: `driver.sh test` → ✅ TEST SUCCEEDED (all 1110+ cases). Commit: see ideas-card/iter4-platt-calibration branch.

## 2026-06-27 — iter5: continuous 52-week-high proximity term (OUTSIDE trend-family cap) (Chat A)
**What:** Added a continuous anchoring-driven 52-week-high proximity signal (Byun & Jeon 2023) to `advise()`. Three surgical changes: (1) New `highProximity(price:highs:window:)` helper in `StockSageIndicators` — one-sided rolling max over `min(252,count)` bars, returns `(pth, effectiveWindow)`, nil on degenerate inputs. (2) Two new constants in `StockSageAdvisor`: `highProximityWeight = 0.10` (mid of [0.08,0.12] band), `highProximityNeutralAnchor = 0.90`. (3) Regime-gated, long-side-only proximity block inserted AFTER the iter3 reassembly (`score = scaledFamily + nonFamily`) and AFTER `regime` is computed — so it is OUTSIDE the 0.65 trend-family cap, NOT scaled by varScalar, and suppressed in `.bearTrend`. Formula: `score += max(0, 0.10·(pth − 0.90))`, max additive = +0.010 (at pth=1). Guardrails: (1) long-side-only floor — cannot manufacture sell/avoid; (2) max +0.010 cannot re-inflate clean uptrend past Strong Buy threshold; (3) bearTrend suppression; (4) short-history honesty string "<252 bars"; (5) byte-identical when pth ≤ 0.90 or regime is bearTrend. Supersedes the dormant binary `isBreakout` advisory intent. Added MARK: 7 in `StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift` — 8 golden-vector tests covering: unit pth/effectiveWindow pins, closed-form contribution ceiling (0.010), pth→1 fires rationale, mid-range neutral, bearTrend suppressed, cleanUptrend still Strong Buy (cap invariant), fallingKnife byte-identity, short-history honest labeling.
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageIndicators.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ typecheck clean (Swift 6, zero errors). Two pre-existing unused-`isBuy`/`isSell` warnings in `advise()` body — not introduced by this change.

## 2026-06-27 · CI — GitHub Actions macOS build+test workflow (owner lifted never-push)
**Files:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (new)
**What & why:** Owner lifted the standing "commit-locally-never-push" rule (2026-06-27) to enable CI. Added a GitHub Actions workflow: on push/PR, `macos-latest` runner runs the canonical `xcodebuild build` + `xcodebuild test -only-testing:"Salehman AITests"` (CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO), with concurrency-cancel and a 30-min timeout. Pushed the `ideas-card/iter6-net-cost-ev-day` branch (iter1-6 + TASI + research index + CI) to the PRIVATE `origin` (swonyu/Salehman-AI).
**Caveat (logged honestly):** the app targets macOS 27; GitHub-hosted runners lag the SDK, so the hosted build may fail for ENVIRONMENT reasons (not code). Fallback documented in the yml: register a self-hosted macOS runner (`runs-on: [self-hosted, macOS]`).
**⚠️ Swift-Testing gotcha discovered (backtest harness):** `xcodebuild -only-testing:".../method"` WITHOUT a trailing `()` runs ZERO tests but reports `TEST SUCCEEDED` (silent no-op). Method-level filters MUST append `()`. Class/target-level filters (what the CI + gates use) are unaffected.
**Result:** ✅ CI workflow committed + pushed to private origin. CI run result TBD (may need self-hosted runner).

## 2026-06-27 · CI — surface the test VERDICT in the log (robust, no false-green regression)
**Files:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
**What & why:** The test step's `… | tee test.log | tail -60` window landed on unrelated (KnowledgeRAG/Chat) tests, so the CI log never positively showed the parity/DSR/math-invariant verdict — green was enforced only by `pipefail`+exit-code (an observability gap; the audit flagged it). Fixed by wrapping the pipe in `if ! …; then print failing lines + exit 1; fi` then printing the positive verdict line on success. **Deliberately NOT the audit's suggested `grep "TEST SUCCEEDED|FAILED" || tail`** — that `||` lets `grep`'s exit 0 on a "TEST FAILED" line swallow a real failure and RE-CREATE the false-green (the c05ce33 bug). The `if !` form guarantees a failed xcodebuild exits the step non-zero. Verified by reading the next CI run log (verdict line present + conclusion correct).
**Result:** ✅ CI green is now verifiable IN THE LOG, not just by exit code. yml-only change.

## 2026-06-27 · feat(stocksage): pooled portfolio-proxy drawdown alongside per-name worstDrawdownR (honesty fix)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStrategyBacktest.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageStrategyBacktestTests.swift`
**What & why:** `worstDrawdownR` was the per-symbol max drawdown (max over per-symbol results), not a portfolio drawdown — but the UI label "Worst DD" made it easy to misread as the latter. Owner-ratified honesty fix: added `pooledDrawdownR` (the equal-weight pooled-chronological proxy) alongside `worstDrawdownR`, both now honestly labeled. Computation: `aggregate()` gains an optional `tradeEntryDates: [Date]` sidecar (1:1 with `trades`); the store threads entry dates via `h.dates[trade.entryIndex]`; `aggregate()` sorts all trades chronologically and walks the cumulative-R curve for peak-to-trough. UI now shows "Worst-name DD" and "Pooled DD (eq-wt)" with a `.help()` tooltip explaining the proxy's limits. Golden-vector test pins `pooledDrawdownR=8.0` / `worstDrawdownR=4.0` on a deterministic 2-symbol fixture and asserts `pooled > per-name` — the same 27.67-vs-17.28 phenomenon measured in production.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED, 1167 tests passed / 0 failed. All existing math-invariant golden vectors (advise/EV/calibration/DSR/tStat) byte-identical green.

**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageBacktestParityTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`
**What & why:** Owner-ratified parsimony cut. Removed the `if let volumes … { score += dir * (vc.confirmed ? 0.05 : -0.05) }` block and its two rationale strings ("Volume-confirmed …" / "Thin volume …") from `advise()`. Rationale: at DSR=0 (no proven edge) the volume term adds complexity inside the already-capped/variance-scaled trend family; T2 ablation showed it directionally worsened drawdown. RS (±0.08) has stronger literature backing and stays. `let scoreBeforeConfirm = score` retained (family anchor; `score - scoreBeforeConfirm` now equals RS + volAdjMom — correct). The `volumes` parameter stays on `advise()` (callers still pass it; Swift does not warn on unused function params). `StockSageIndicators.volumeConfirmation` retained (has its own direct unit-test in `StockSageIndicatorsTests`). Test updates: renamed `negativeControl_volumeTermIsLive` → `negativeControl_volumeInputIsInert` and flipped assertions (volume is now inert; thin vs full volumes must produce identical conviction within 1e-6). Math-invariant comments at lines 443–444 and 566 corrected (old max raw family 0.83 → new 0.78; "vol confirm" dropped from enumeration).
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED, 1170 tests passed / 0 failed. Parity main + RS control green. All non-volume golden vectors (TSMOM/varScalar/Platt/52wk/net-cost/Kelly) byte-identical unchanged.

## 2026-06-27 · fix(grok-safari): bug-fix + harden the Safari bridge & parallel launcher; add unit tests
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/run_parallel_safari.sh`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py` (new)
**What & why:** Refined the Grok→Safari terminal bridge and its parallel launcher; fixed 5 real bugs:
(1) `run_parallel_safari.sh` printed `$#` (requested count) in the success banner instead of `$N` (actually-launched count) — misleading whenever the RAM auto-cap reduces the agent count.
(2) `run_auto_safari._rate_limit_hits` is a function attribute that persisted **across tasks** in a `--loop` session and was never reset after recovery, so a healthy agent that recovered earlier would skip straight to the long "still limited" wait on its next, unrelated rate-limit. Now reset per-task and after any healthy (command-bearing) turn.
(3) `_safari_page_text()` passed eval-failure sentinels (`err:…` / `(osascript error: …)`) straight to login/error detectors, so an unreachable Safari falsely read as "logged-in, error-free". Now sanitized to "" at the boundary → detectors fail safe (login loop retries instead of proceeding).
(4) `_safari_is_generating()` matched the bare word `Cancel` in page text (cookie banners, chrome) → could wedge in a permanent "generating" state and force every turn onto the 45s no-progress watchdog. Narrowed to the specific "Stop generating" phrase.
(5) `_UI_NOISE` chip-stripping regex required trailing whitespace (`Explore\s`/`Investigate\s`/`Regenerate\s`), so a bare chip line (exactly `Regenerate`) leaked through — unlike `Like`/`Dislike` which use `\s*$`. Switched to `\b`. (Caught by the new test suite.)
Added `tools/test_grok_bridge.py` — 30 unit tests over the bug-prone pure core (parse_commands, is_done, catastrophic_match/looks_risky safety floor, UI-noise stripping, diff-fence collection, and the new fail-safe page-text behavior). Safari/osascript paths aren't headlessly testable, but this locks in the parsing + safety logic that decides what actually runs on the Mac.
**Result:** ✅ `python3 -m py_compile` clean, `bash -n` clean, all 30 unit tests pass. Launcher no-arg guard + bridge `--help` smoke-tested. No behavior change to the manual/agent-browser/autofix modes beyond the shared bug-fixes.

## 2026-06-27 · feat(markets): cancellable ideas refresh + Cancel button (backlog #12)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`
**What & why:** Backlog #12 — give the Ideas board a working Cancel button and prevent the spinner from hanging. Two-part change:
1. **StockSageStore.swift:** Added `private var ideasRefreshTask: Task<Void, Never>?` near `isLoadingIdeas`. Refactored `refreshIdeas()` to delegate its body to a new `private func performRefreshIdeas() async` run as a cancellable child `Task`. The outer `refreshIdeas()` keeps `isLoadingIdeas = true` + `defer { isLoadingIdeas = false }` so the spinner clears on every exit path (feed-failure early-return, cancel, and normal completion — fixes the hung-spinner bug). `await task.value` keeps `refreshIdeas` fully awaitable, so `MarketsView:166` still reads populated `store.ideas` after the await (fixes the fire-and-forget stale-ideas bug). Two `guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }` guards inside `performRefreshIdeas()` — one after `let benchmark = await benchmarkTask`, one after `await Self.buildIdeas(...)` — stop applying stale results mid-flight. New `func cancelIdeasRefresh()` cancels the retained task. `retryFailedIdeas()` untouched.
2. **MarketsView.swift:** Added a Cancel button in `ideasHeader` HStack, gated on `store.isLoadingIdeas`, placed immediately before the "Analyzing…"/"Find ideas" button. Styled with `.white.opacity(0.08)` Capsule + `LuxPressStyle()` to match the adjacent "Copy CSV" secondary control. `.help` + `.accessibilityLabel` added.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED, 1168 tests passed / 0 failed. refreshIdeas stays awaitable; defer clears spinner on all exit paths.

---
**2026-06-27 — grok_terminal_bridge.py / run_parallel_safari.sh / grok_status.sh / apply_grok_diff.sh: deep polish (15 improvements)**
**What & why:** Comprehensive hardening pass — correctness, reliability, UX.
1. **Shebang fix** — stale comment was on line 1, shebang on line 2; swapped so `chmod +x` works.
2. **`_safari_generation_state()`** — split `_safari_is_generating()` into a named state function returning 'streaming'/'thinking'/'idle'; added cursor-blink and per-turn Thinking… tail detection; `_safari_is_generating()` is now a 1-line wrapper.
3. **`_safari_get_last_message()` priority reversal** — DOM role/testid selectors for the last ASSISTANT bubble now fire FIRST; global last-`<pre>` is the fallback. This prevents re-parsing primer code blocks (which were always the "last `<pre>`" until Grok replied) as new commands.
4. **React-native textarea injection** — replaced deprecated `execCommand('insertText')` with `HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype.value` nativeInputValueSetter + synthetic InputEvent + change Event. Fires proper React reconciliation. Clears stale composer content before injecting.
5. **`_safari_new_chat()` project-mode await** — now polls for textarea presence (up to 8 s) after navigating to the project URL before returning; prevents injecting into a half-loaded page.
6. **`_safari_stream_reply()` phase labels** — prints `⟳ thinking` when in Think-mode reasoning phase, transitions to `→ ▸ streaming` when text starts; was always printing `streaming ▸` regardless of state.
7. **Tail truncation** — output cap raised from 3 500 → 6 000 chars, now keeps the LAST N chars (errors are at the end of xcodebuild output, not the start). Omit header printed.
8. **No more chunking** — removed the 4 096-char chunk-send loop; React native setter handles arbitrary string sizes. Grok now sees one coherent message instead of 3 fragmented ones.
9. **Stuck-reply detection** — 3-item ring buffer tracks reply fingerprints; if 3 identical replies arrive consecutively, bridge injects a poke and continues (was: agent got stuck forever with no recovery).
10. **`_SEEN_CMDS` size cap** — evicts full set when >400 entries (safety valve for long sessions).
11. **`grok_status.sh`** — full ANSI color overhaul: state colored green/yellow/red, bold labels, dim PID column, separator line.
12. **`apply_grok_diff.sh`** — added `--whitespace=fix` second-pass and `--reject` diagnostic third-pass; previously a CRLF or trailing-space diff would fail with no actionable output.
13. **`run_parallel_safari.sh`** — banner shows think on/off, project URL if set.
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/run_parallel_safari.sh`, `tools/grok_status.sh`, `tools/apply_grok_diff.sh`
**Result:** `python3 -m py_compile` clean, 30/30 unit tests pass.

## 2026-06-27 · grok_terminal_bridge.py — security hardening + xAI marketing noise (3-layer fix)
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`
**What & why:** Live agent logs showed grok.com injecting xAI CLI marketing content (`Grok Build / Beta / Early access for Heavy subscribers / $ curl -fsSL https://x.ai/cli/install.sh | bash`) into the page text after the session marker. Risk: in zsh, a bare `$` before a command (e.g. `$ curl`) expands to empty → effectively runs the command. In YOLO mode this would execute the installer. Three-layer fix: (1) `_UI_NOISE` extended with `Grok Build`, `Early access for Heavy subscribers`, `$ curl`/`$ bash`/`$ sh` shell-prompt-style lines, and `x.ai/cli/install.sh` regex; (2) `_GROK_NOISE` set extended with `"grok build"`, `"early access for heavy subscribers"`, `"beta"`; (3) `BLOCKED_SUBSTRINGS` gets `"x.ai/cli/install.sh"` as a hard catastrophic backstop.
**Result:** 37/37 tests pass. Running agents not restarted (fix takes effect on next restart).

## 2026-06-27 · grok_terminal_bridge.py — 7 more refinements (chrome pipeline, stuck-ring, empty-file guard, primer rule, dot cap)
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py`
**What & why:** Second refinement pass on the project-mode bridge:
1. **`_strip_grok_chrome` now calls `_clean_ui_noise` first** — "Fast/Attach to message/Connected to computer" chips were leaking into Phase 2 `last_reply` via the page-text path (it called `_strip_grok_chrome`, not `_clean_ui_noise`). Now both layers run in sequence. Also added 7 exact strings to `_GROK_NOISE` as belt-and-suspenders.
2. **Stuck-reply ring fingerprints stripped reply** — raw reply was fingerprinted, so repeated UI noise ("Fast\nAttach...") filled the ring and fired false pokes. Now fingerprints `_strip_grok_chrome(_clean_ui_noise(reply))[:250]`.
3. **Empty staged-file pushback** — agents were staging 0-byte files and calling DONE (fake-DONE guard passed because git DID change). Now: `git diff --cached --numstat` detects staged files with 0 insertions → pushes back with EMPTY_STAGED_FILES message.
4. **Primer rule 6 added** — "VERIFY new files have content (wc -l > 0) before DONE." Old rule 6 → rule 7.
5. **Phase 1 dots cap at 40 per line** — 120s × 1.25 dots/s = up to 150 dots; now wraps to new `⏳` line every 40 dots.
6. **`_GROK_PROGRESS_RE` length guard 200→400** — allows longer progress-text strings to be caught by the no-streak-increment path.
7. **37 tests** (up from 35 → 37 with 2 new: `test_strip_grok_chrome_calls_clean_ui_noise`, `test_progress_re_matches_350char_string`).
**Result:** 37/37 tests pass, import clean, 3 agents relaunched.

## 2026-06-27 · grok_terminal_bridge.py — 4 project-mode bugs fixed (Phase 1/2 chrome detection, poke handling, progress detection)
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py`
**What & why:** Live agent logs from the first 4-agent run revealed three new bugs after the Bug 1/2/3 fixes:
1. **Phase 1 marker check fired on grok.com intermediate progress text** — "Running system info command", "Thinking ab...", "Identifyi..." appeared in the page text after our session marker (grok.com tool-use status labels). `len > 10` threshold was too loose; Phase 1 broke immediately, Phase 2 read the noise as Grok's reply → `parse_commands()` → [] → `no_cmd_streak++`. Fix: require `"CMD:"` or `"[[DONE]]"` to be present in the stripped content after the marker before breaking Phase 1.
2. **Phase 2 exited in 1 iteration in project mode** — `_safari_is_generating()` returns False always (DOM selectors miss the stop button in project chats). The `if not generating: break` path fired immediately, returning intermediate progress text as the "reply". Fix: only break when `"CMD:"` is in `last_reply` OR content has been stable for 5s. Phase 2 keeps polling as long as grok.com shows tool-use progress.
3. **`no_cmd_streak` not reset after stuck-reply poke** — old streak count from noise turns could hit 3 immediately after a poke. Also `is_done()` not checked on poke reply. Fix: reset `no_cmd_streak = 0` on poke; check `is_done(reply)` before `parse_commands()`.
4. **Extended `_UI_NOISE` + added `_GROK_PROGRESS_RE`** — project-mode chrome ("Fast", "Attach to message", "Drop here to add files", "Connected to computer") now stripped. Module-level `_GROK_PROGRESS_RE` detects progress-update-only replies in the `no_cmd_streak` logic, re-reads without penalising the streak.
**Result:** 35/35 unit tests pass, `import` clean. 7 new tests cover project-mode chrome, `_GROK_PROGRESS_RE` matching, and `is_done` on poke reply.

---
**2026-06-27 — grok_terminal_bridge: 7 targeted hardening fixes**
- **Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py`
- **What:** (1) `_strip_grok_chrome` now calls `_clean_ui_noise` first + extended `_GROK_NOISE` with project-mode chips; (2) stuck-reply ring fingerprints stripped reply to avoid false pokes on UI noise; (3) empty-staged-file pushback guard after `[[DONE]]` (git numstat check); (4) Primer rule 6 added (wc -l verify before done), old rule 6 → rule 7; (5) Phase 1 dot output wraps at 40 per line with `⏳` continuation; (6) `_GROK_PROGRESS_RE` length cap raised 200→400; (7) two new unit tests (`test_strip_grok_chrome_calls_clean_ui_noise`, `test_progress_re_matches_350char_string`).
- **Why:** project-mode UI chips leaked into `last_reply` and stuck-ring fingerprints; empty file staging could slip past `[[DONE]]` guards; long thinking sessions produced unreadable dot lines.

## 2026-06-27 · grok_terminal_bridge.py — heredoc body expansion in parse_commands()
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py`
**What & why:** Root cause of MarketsIdeasSection.swift being 0 bytes despite Grok generating correct file content: `parse_commands()` only extracts the `CMD:` line (regex `^CMD:\s*(.+)$` stops at EOL), so a heredoc `cat > file << 'EOF'\n...\nEOF` ran with stdin=/dev/null → empty file. Fixed by adding `_expand_heredoc(cmd, full_text)` that finds the heredoc delimiter, scans subsequent lines until the closing marker, and returns the complete multi-line command. Added 3 new tests (`test_heredoc_body_included`, `test_heredoc_no_closing_marker_not_expanded`, `test_heredoc_non_heredoc_unchanged`).
**Result:** 40/40 tests pass. All future agent heredoc file writes will include the body.

## 2026-06-27 · grok_terminal_bridge.py — fake-DONE guard fix (git diff HEAD vs porcelain)
**Files:** `tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py`, `tools/test_grok_bridge.py`
**What & why:** Fake-DONE guard compared `git status --porcelain` snapshots — only lists WHICH files are modified, not HOW. When an agent edits a file already `M` at session start, the status text looks identical → guard incorrectly fires → agent loops indefinitely. Fixed by snapshotting `git diff HEAD` (full content diff) instead: any change to an already-modified file produces a different diff → guard correctly passes. Added Fix 7 comment to test file.
**Result:** safari-1 fake-DONE loop resolved; safari-2/3 will now declare DONE correctly after real edits to MarketsView.swift.

## 2026-06-27 · feat(stocksage): downside-skew / left-tail read (EDGE_RESEARCH #4)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageReturnShape.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageReturnShapeTests.swift` (new)
**What & why:** EDGE_RESEARCH.md item #4 — per-symbol return-distribution honesty check. `StockSageReturnShape.returnShape(closes:)` computes skewness (population 3rd standardized moment), `downside95` (max(0, −percentile(returns, 0.05)) as a fraction), and `worstDay` over `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.dailyReturns`; nil for <30 returns or zero-dispersion series. `ReturnShape: Sendable, Equatable` struct with `isLeftTailed (skew < −0.5)`, `note`, `caveat`. Pure + deterministic; reuses existing `dailyReturns`/`percentile` helpers. RiskFlags wiring (deliverable #3) SKIPPED — `StockSageRiskFlags.flags(...)` takes only pre-computed scalars, not closes; its sole caller is `MarketsView.swift` (the forbidden UI lane). Surfacing "negative skew — left-tailed" in the UI is a one-line owner decision at the call site; the engine output is ready. No sizing change: `ReturnShape` is read-only honesty output, never multiplied into any weight or scalar. No UI/Views/ file touched. Tests: 4 Swift Testing cases — symmetric returns→skew≈0/not left-tailed; large-negative-jumps→skew<0/isLeftTailed/downside95≥|medianDay|; <30 returns→nil; downside-invariants (non-negative, worstDay≤−downside95); caveat-presence on all.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED. ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (all 4 new ReturnShape tests pass; full suite green; isolated `.dd-refine` DerivedData; no main-repo `/Users/saleh/ai` or Views/ touch).

## 2026-06-27 · feat(stocksage): vol-of-vol sizing-reliability read (EDGE_RESEARCH #5)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageVolStability.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageVolStabilityTests.swift` (new)
**What & why:** EDGE_RESEARCH.md item #5 — rolling vol-stability honesty check. `StockSageVolStability.volStability(closes:volWindow:historyWindow:)` builds a `historyWindow`-length series of rolling annualized vols (each from a trailing `volWindow`-bar slice via `StockSageIndicators.annualizedVolatility`), computes CoV = populationStdev/mean of that series, maps to `VolStability.Band` {.steady (CoV<0.15), .choppy (<0.35), .erratic (≥0.35)}, and returns `sizingReliability = 1/(1+CoV) ∈ (0,1]` (= 1 at CoV=0, strictly decreasing). Self-contained (no `StockSageVolRegime` dep — never built); orthogonal to `varianceScalar` (level vs dispersion of vol). Nil for <147 bars default, degenerate windows, or all-equal-closes slices. `VolStability: Sendable, Equatable`; `Band` nested inside struct. Honest caveat: backward-looking stability of risk inputs, not trade quality, not a forecast, not an entry/exit signal. No sizing change: read-only, not wired into any weight/fraction/scaler. No UI/Views/ file touched.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED. ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (all 6 new VolStability tests pass: constantVolSteady, alternatingErratic, insufficientBarsNil, sizingReliabilityMonotoneAndClosedForm, caveatNoteNonEmpty, bandThresholdConsistency; full suite green; isolated `.dd-refine` DerivedData; no main-repo `/Users/saleh/ai` or Views/ touch).

## 2026-06-27 · feat(stocksage): iter7 calibration candidate-selector (Beta-3param + OOS-pick, off-by-default)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibrationSelectorTests.swift` (new), `research/INDEX.md`
**What & why:** iter7 quant-engine deliverable — OOS candidate-selector for conviction calibration. Three additions, all behind a default-false flag so existing behavior is byte-identical:
1. `candidateSelectorEnabled = false` flag (mirrors `relativeStrengthEnabled` pattern).
2. `BetaCalibration` type + `fitBeta`: 3-parameter logistic regression on `[1, ln(s), −ln(1−s)]` (Kull et al. AISTATS 2017). Deterministic IRLS, 25-iter cap, 3×3 Cramer inverse, drop-and-refit to enforce `a≥0, b≥0` (monotonicity guarantee). Near-zero ridge `1e-8·I` only as a singularity guard (not L2 shrinkage). Subsumed the planned ridge-logistic Platt plan.
3. `selectCalibration`: leak-free OOS selector. Chronological 70/30 train/test split + 1-row embargo. Fits {identity, Beta, isotonic} on TRAIN only, scores OOS Brier on TEST only, selects the lowest. Identity wins any tie within `1e-9` (conservative floor — never applies calibration that doesn't earn it OOS). Winner refit on full data. `buildIdentity` + `materializeBins` + `brierScore` helpers (all private).
4. `fitPlatt` comment updated to reference `candidateSelectorEnabled` (replacing stale "iter7 planned" note).
- Flag-off: the `if candidateSelectorEnabled { return selectCalibration(...) }` guard fires first in `fit(_:)`; when false control falls through to the unchanged `isotonicMinSamples` seam → byte-identical.
- No change to `fitPlatt`, `fitIsotonic`, `winProb`, `wilsonLowerBound`, `poolAdjacentViolators`, `chronologicalSplit`, `validateOutOfSample`, `Bin` layout, or any consumer.
- 10 new tests: Beta fit on well-conditioned data (monotone, direction correct), Beta monotone on inverted (drop-and-refit), Beta near-identity, selector identity-floor (small-N guard + structural), selector picks non-identity on separable data, selector leak-free, flag-off byte-identical regression-lock (3 fixtures + restore), structural invariants across data shapes, Beta nil on one-sided, Beta in [0,1] everywhere. Research docs updated.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED. ✅ TEST SUCCEEDED (full suite; all 10 new selector tests green; all existing `StockSageConvictionCalibrationTests` + sizing/EV/math-invariant goldens green; isolated `.dd-refine` DerivedData; no main-repo `/Users/saleh/ai` or Views/ touch; no advise/suggestedWeight/sizing change).

**2026-06-27 · StockSageExpectedValue.summary: earnings-aware "Fastest" badge**
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift:516`
**What & why:** The velocity card's "Fastest" headline badge was computed via `fastLane().first` which sorts by raw velocity and ignores earnings proximity. The board's `.velocity` tab uses `rankByVelocity(earnings:)` which demotes earnings-risky ideas. The mismatch meant the card could crown a near-earnings idea as "Fastest" while the board ranked it lower. Fixed: replaced `fastLane(...).first` with `rankByVelocity(...earnings:...)).first(where: evR > 0)` — same positive-EV filter, now earnings-penalized. One-line change, no new symbols, no sizing/logic change.
**Result:** ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED.

**2026-06-27 · StockSageStore.refreshIdeas: 120s auto-cancel watchdog**
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift:268-277`
**What & why:** `refreshIdeas()` had a manual cancel path (`cancelIdeasRefresh()`) but no automatic timeout — a hung network fetch could leave `isLoadingIdeas = true` forever. Added a 120s watchdog `Task` that cancels the work task if it doesn't complete in time, triggering the existing `isCancelled` guards and the `defer { isLoadingIdeas = false }` spinner clear. Watchdog is disarmed immediately when work finishes normally. Safari-1 HANDOFF prompted this; implementation uses task cancellation (cleaner than direct UI mutation). ✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED (`59a46b6`).

**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageVolRegime.swift` (new), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCapitalAllocator.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageVolRegimeTests.swift` (new, 8 tests)
**What & why:** Implements the last open edge from EDGE_RESEARCH.md: per-symbol realized-vol regime brake using only the symbol's own close history (no VIX → works on Tadawul/FX/crypto). **2026-07-02 · ideas-3 UI findings: 6 fixes in backtestPanel + strategyBacktestPanel + positionSizerPanel**
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`
**What & why:** Adversarial-review findings for section "ideas-3". (1) PSR row label corrected: removed the false claim that PSR >95% is "the honest bar" — added clarification that PSR clears the sample/skew bar only and that the multi-name selection-bias haircut (DSR) lives in the strategy panel. (2) Strategy panel: added the DSR row (Deflated Sharpe, selection-bias haircut for ~12 variants) that was computed by `StrategyBacktest.deflatedSharpe` but never surfaced — the caveat already told users DSR was applied but it was never shown. (3) Win-rate color threshold: both backtest panels changed from `winRate >= 0.5` to `winRate >= 1.0/3` to match the fixed 2:1 exit's actual break-even (~33%); a 40% win-rate system is profitable at 2:1 but was shown in danger red. (4) Decay sentinel: `decayRatio == 0` when `isAvgR <= 0` is an engine sentinel (no in-sample edge to decay from), not a measurement of "kept 0% of edge"; branched display to say "No in-sample edge to decay from" in that case, in both visible text and a11y label. (5) Trail-vs-fixed tie: added a fourth verdict branch "about a wash here" when neither dd nor return margin is cleared in either direction, so a genuine tie isn't mislabeled "fixed 2:1 holds up better here". (6) Sharpe sentinel: `bt.sharpe == 0` when `bt.trades < 2` or zero variance — now shows "n/a" instead of fabricated "0.00". Finding #1 (leverage short-side liquidation price) was already fixed in the prior critical-fix commit — skipped.
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`. Pure UI display fixes; no engine files touched.

**2026-07-02 · Delta-problems batch: 5 confirmed UI findings fixed (MarketsView + BrowseMarketsView)**
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/BrowseMarketsView.swift`
**What & why:** Five confirmed independently-verified findings from delta_problems.json, all in the two Markets view files. (1) Warn-banner attribution: `.warn` case rewritten with gate-neutral wording showing both daily R (-state.dailyRealizedR) and weekly dollars (-state.weeklyRealized); no longer falsely says "down 0.0R today" when the weekly gate fired. a11y label updated to match. (2) Analytics caption stale-case: when `store.analyticsError != nil`, caption now shows `a.holdingsAnalyzed` alone ("N holdings (previous analysis)") without referencing the current live portfolio count, preventing the nonsensical "5 of 2 holdings" copy. Normal (no-error) path unchanged; added `max(0,…)` guard. (3) bestOpportunityCTA onCopy: switched from `Double(sizerAccount)` / `Double(sizerRiskPct)` to `StockSageInput.positiveAmount(sizerAccount)` / `StockSageInput.percent(sizerRiskPct)` so comma-formatted accounts like "10,000" are parsed correctly — matching the fix already applied to bestOpportunityCard. (4) Weekly-timeframe spinner: added `@State private var mtfFetchCompleted: Set<String>` to MarketsView; `.task(id: idea.symbol)` inserts the symbol after `refreshMultiTimeframe` completes (success OR failure); spinner `else-if` now also requires `!mtfFetchCompleted.contains(…)`, so a failed fetch replaces the permanent ProgressView with "Weekly timeframe unavailable." (5) Browse-sheet error bleed: added `@State private var watchlistAddError: String?` to MarketsView; the watchlist box displays `watchlistAddError` (populated by `addWatchSymbol()` from `store.addSymbolError` capture) instead of reading `store.addSymbolError` directly; `.onChange(of: showBrowseMarkets) { watchlistAddError = nil }` clears on open/close; browse-sheet add failures no longer appear in the watchlist box after dismiss. StockSageStore.swift not modified (private(set) constraint honoured; direction (b) fixed at the view layer).
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`. Pure view-layer fixes; no engine or store files touched.

## 2026-07-02 · Ideas-card wave 8+9: detail-sheet decision-first restructure + honesty labels + verified fix pass
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTradePlan.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTradePlanTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`
**What & why:** Wave 8 implemented the 15 triaged proposals from the 4-lens detail-sheet critique (wave 7): the sheet body now follows decision order — header/sparkline/signal-strength meter → risk chips → earnings warning (hoisted above the gate for buy-family; sell-path fallback stays in Context) → gate verdict → position sizer → plan numerics (price/stop/target/R:R/staleness + promoted MTF row) → "Why" rationale → evidence (net-cost, EV, backtest panel) → new labeled "Exit plan" section (regime-size caveat, scale-out ladder, chandelier) → new labeled "Context" section (severity-ordered) → CTA buttons last. Honesty additions: measured/assumed calibration chip under the EV line; conviction relabeled "Signal strength N/100" with an always-visible "How many rules agree — NOT a win probability." caption; "Base size"/"Regime size" stage labels; Copy plan exports the ladder rungs + chandelier level; per-rung bell buttons seed price alerts (level-vs-price direction rule, dedup vs armed alerts). Wave 9 (4 adversarial verify lenses, 18 findings) then fixed: copy-plan prices switched to the sheet's adaptivePrice formatting (rungs/chandelier/stop/target/entry — sub-$1 assets no longer paste "0.01" for a 0.006200 level); plan Action line relabeled "signal strength N/100 … not a win probability" (was "conviction N%", contradicting the sheet); Regime-size tooltips corrected to the real allocator order (regime bias → vol-targeting shrink → vol-regime brake → correlation de-weighting → heat cap — vol-targeting had been omitted); sizer panel restored for sell/reduce ideas (wave 8 had buried it in the buy-guard — 3 verifiers converged on this regression); "Exit plan"/"Context" headers gated on real child conditions (no orphaned headers on Hold/Avoid) + `.isHeader` a11y traits; bell buttons' a11y/help branch on armed state; sheet EV line labeled "(gross)" matching the card; sheet meter tooltip relabeled; duplicate disclaimer deduped; `.uppercased()` call-site inconsistency removed; 3 new hand-derived TradePlan tests (ladder ordering/formatting, sub-dollar chandelier, action-line contract). Rejected as not-regressions: MTF/ladder visibility on Hold/Avoid (pre-existing, descriptive), epsilon alert dedup (would diverge view vs store semantics — documented instead). NOTE: TradePlan nil-param output is intentionally no longer byte-identical to pre-wave-8 (adaptive prices + relabel); doc comment updated to say so.
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` · ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,446 pass, incl. 3 new). Display + export layer only; no ranking/engine behavior change.

## 2026-07-02 · Ideas-engine test hardening: 27 new tests closing the audit's coverage gaps (F05/F06/F17/F40) — test-only
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageBuildIdeasDirectTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMultiBrakeTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageVolRegimeFloorTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageFastLaneThresholdTests.swift` (all new; zero production changes)
**What & why:** The Ideas-board audit (`AUDIT_2026-07-02_ideas_board.md`) found the highest-value engine paths unpinned. Closed: **F05** — first direct `buildIdeas` tests (momentumQuality nil at exactly 20 closes / non-nil at 21; each honesty note actually lands in the rationale when triggered — left-tail ⚠, whippy-volatility ⚠, vol-regime brake, ⏱ execution timing; index symbols excluded; calibration changes ONLY suggestedWeight — action/conviction/stop/target pinned identical and the weight pinned to actually differ via winProb-0.30 bins → Kelly-negative → calibrated weight 0 vs prior > 0). **F06** — first multi-brake composition tests (regime bias 0.25 × vol-target ÷4 × volRegime 0.25 composed: pre-cap `requestedHeat` pinned to hand-derived 0.00271875 ±1e-6 so the heat cap can't mask a deleted brake; heat ≤ cap with 3 braked ideas; edge order preserved; no zero/negative-share lines). **F17** — the vol-regime 0.25 sizing floor pinned exactly (formula-level, ratio sweep 0.5×–100×, and end-to-end via a 293-bar series). **F40** — the fast-lane 1.5× crypto-dominance threshold de-circularized AND genuinely pinned with a straddle pair (BTC target 108.7 → ratio 1.533 TRUE / target 108.5 → ratio 1.455 FALSE against AAPL vel 0.087 — pins the constant to (1.455, 1.533)). A Fable-xhigh adversarial review of the first draft caught 2 blockers before merge — a symbol typo (WHIPPYX/WHIPPYEX) that made the whippy test pass vacuously (all soft `guard else return` patterns replaced with hard `#expect(ideas.count == 1)` + `Issue.record`), and straddle fixtures that were actually at 13.4×/0.40× (threshold unpinned) — plus 2 vacuousness fixes and 2 nits (tautology test deleted, misnamed boundary test renamed to `cryptoRotationDominantFalseWhenCryptoEVNegative`). ## 2026-07-02 — 5 residual adversarial-verify fixes: F11 hardening, vacuous-test fix, F27 copy-drift hardening, F32 comment honesty, dev-log cleanup
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTradeGateTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.
**What & why:**
- **F11 residual (StockSageStore.refreshLaneCorrelation):** The prior fix only stamped the fingerprint on success; on failure the fingerprint stayed unchanged (not nil), so set-oscillation (A ok → B fails → back to A) permanently blocked A's refetch. Fixed: `laneCorrelationFingerprint = (result != nil) ? fingerprint : nil` — failure now CLEARS the fingerprint so every next call retries. Added `guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }` immediately after the `await` so a cancelled stale task cannot clobber a fresh task's published value (mirrors F31 pattern). Updated comment and MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md StockSageStore gotcha to describe the corrected behavior.
- **Vacuous test (StockSageTradeGateTests.correlationBandBoundary05IsModerateNotLow):** Added `#expect(corrCheck != nil)` before the `if let` block, mirroring the 0.79/0.45 sibling tests, so the boundary test fails loudly if the correlation check disappears from the gate.
- **F27 copy-drift hardening (StockSageExpectedValue + MarketsView):** Factored the duplicated financing-note construction (`(finRate > 0 && finDays > 0) ? String(format: " + ~%.0fbps/yr short financing", ...)`) into a new `nonisolated static func financingNoteSuffix(rate:days:) -> String` on `StockSageExpectedValue`, next to `financingCostInputs`. Replaced both MarketsView sites (fullPlanText ~L4352 and sheet evidence ~L4614) with calls to it. Output is byte-identical — the function body is textually the same expression, confirmed via `derive_f27.swift`. +3 unit tests in StockSageExpectedValueTests: (0.03, 12) → contains '300bps/yr short financing'; (0.03, 0) → empty; (0, 12) → empty.
- **F32 comment honesty (StockSageStore.deriveEarningsProximity):** The comment said the derive guard "mirrors" the fetch-time guard. They differ by one instant: fetch uses strict `> -86_400` (evicts at exactly -86400s); derive uses non-strict `>= -86_400` (keeps at exactly -86400s). Updated the comment to state this honestly and note the conservative direction. Updated MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md Gotchas + Invariants sections to match.
- **Dev-log cleanup (this entry's predecessor):** Removed the duplicate '**Updated test pins:** None — …' line from the 2026-07-02 state/cache entry; rewrote the `derive_statecache.swift` scratchpad reference to note derivations are preserved as inline comments in the test files (the scratchpad is ephemeral).
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` · ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,493 pass, 0 failures; +4 new tests: 3 financingNoteSuffix + 1 vacuous-guard; byte-identity confirmed via derive_f27.swift).

## 2026-07-02 · Pinned-bar visual-QA fix: compact button + verdict labels (display-only)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`
**What & why:** Live-app visual QA of the merged waves 11+12 (real 210-name data, sheet at default width) caught the pinned CTA bar wrapping mid-word: "Log this trade" broke onto three lines, "Backtest 5 years" split as "Backtes/t 5 years", and after fixing those with `lineLimit(1)`/`fixedSize()` the verdict chip truncated to "Proceed…" — the one element whose legibility is the bar's whole point. Fix: shortened button labels ("Log trade", "Copy plan" unchanged, "Backtest 5y") with `lineLimit(1).fixedSize()`, added a `.help` to the shortened Backtest button, and gave the verdict chip COMPACT labels ("Clear" / "Caution" / "Do NOT trade") — icon + tint carry severity, the full verdict phrase stays in `.help` ("Proceed with caution — …caveat") and the VoiceOver label ("Pre-trade gate: Proceed with caution"), and the complete gate section above remains the authoritative wording. Re-verified in the running app: Log trade · Copy plan · Backtest 5y · ⚠ Caution all render single-line at the 440pt floor. Also confirmed live during this QA pass: Evidence header, gross·net velocity line, "win% assumed" chip, net-labeled gate line, Today-card CAUTION reasons, fast-lane/Today "R/day gross" tags, and Arm-all-3 on the ladder header all render correctly on real data.
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` · ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,493 pass). Display-only.

Modeled on the run-salehman-ai house style (front-loaded rule, concrete commands, Gotchas).
**Result:** Docs-only; no code touched, no build/test run needed.

## 2026-07-02 · Calc-quality wave — calibration provenance (F01/F02/F43 display half), date-aligned cluster correlation (F14), calibration-fit caching + rank decorate-sort (F12), weekly gross labels (F03/F44)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `StockSageStore.swift`, `StockSageClusterCheck.swift`, `StockSageGlossary.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibrationSelectorTests.swift`, `StockSageClusterCheckTests.swift`, `StockSageBuildIdeasDirectTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`. (Completed across two sessions: the first implemented items 1–4 but died at the session limit mid-test-writing; this session verified the inherited diff hunk-by-hunk against the spec, fixed a type-checker-timeout build break, and wrote the missing tests/docs.)
**What & why:** Owner mandate: improve the ideas-surface calculations within the standing gates — honesty floor intact, ZERO ranking/EV numeric change, owner-held decisions (identity-thin-branch nil/clamp, weekly netting) NOT taken.
- **Item 1 — calibration provenance (F01/F02/F43, display-only):** `StockSageConvictionCalibration` gains `Method` (`isotonicWilson`/`beta`/`platt`/`identity`); every fit path stamps its own (buildIdentity → `.identity`). New `chipTitle`/`chipHelp`/`isMeasuredFromOutcomes` are the single source for UI wording: identity → "win% assumed (identity)" + warning tint, Platt → "win% fitted · n=X" (tooltip: central MLE, NOT conservative), isotonic → "win% measured · n=X (conservative)", Beta → "win% measured · n=X (OOS-validated)" (deliberately NOT "(conservative)" — Beta is a central fit; claiming conservatism would trade one overclaim for another). All six MarketsView calibration displays (calibrationChip + ideasHeader label + list-row conviction-meter .help + detail-sheet conviction-meter .help + strategy-backtest status + the chip's 3 other host cards) key on `.method`, never on `calibration != nil` — the detail-sheet meter (hc#2, ~L4482) was missed by the first pass and still claimed "win-rate mapped from N realized trades" for identity; caught by the adversarial verifier and fixed to the same identity→"ASSUMED"/platt→"fitted"/else→"measured" wording as its list-row twin. `winProbEstimate`'s "MEASURED, conservative" docstring overclaim fixed (F43), and the selector docstring's inverted "strictly more conservative" claim corrected (identity exceeds the 0.35+0.23·c prior for conviction ≳0.45; nil/clamp remains owner-held per AUDIT F01). Zero numeric change.
- **Item 2 — date-aligned cluster correlation (F14):** new `StockSageClusterCheck.checkDated` correlates candidate vs holdings on their COMMON UTC days (`alignByDate`) instead of positionally; `StockSageStore.refreshPrecheck` now stashes `datedReturns` for every symbol it already fetches into `@Published precheckDatedReturns` (no new network); the detail-sheet cluster check consumes those. Honest-unknown contract: <5 overlapping days or zero-variance pair → skipped (tallied), no scorable pair → nil → nothing renders; green affirmation stays suppressed under partial coverage. Displayed correlation for cross-calendar pairs (Tadawul/US, crypto/equity) changes BY DESIGN — the old positional pairing biased them toward 0 (false-green "adds diversification"); ranking/EV untouched (allocator gates still use positional `check` on index-aligned series).
- **Item 3 — calibration-fit caching + rank decorate-sort (F12, zero behavior change):** `convictionCalibration`/`calibrationOOS` now memoized in `JournalCalibrationCache`, keyed on VALUE equality of the journal trades array — ANY mutation invalidates on the very next read (no per-mutator hooks to forget); previously the full fit re-ran on every read (33 reads per MarketsView render). rankByEV/rankByVelocity/rankByVelocityWeighted converted to decorate-sort-undecorate (key computed once per idea, was twice per comparison); comparators + offset tie-breaks byte-identical.
- **Item 4 — weekly rollup labels (F03/F44, label-only; netting owner-held):** "gross, before costs" at the velocity summary card (Est./week, $/week), fast-lane strip (R/week, $/week), playbook weekly line + "net" on the playbook fastest line, `MoneyVelocityCopy.weeklyDollars`, glossary weeklyR/weeklyDollars; F44 caveat ("can include ideas the net-cost floor demotes; the 'Fastest' pick excludes them") in every weekly .help + the weeklyR glossary. NO number changes.
- **Build break found in the inherited diff:** the chained tuple `enumerated().map{...}.sorted{...}.map(\.idea)` expressions blew Swift's type-checker budget ("unable to type-check this expression in reasonable time") — split into explicitly-typed decorate/sort/undecorate statements in all three rank functions, semantics identical.
- **Tests (8 new, all passing):** provenance pins (`identityProvenanceRendersAssumedNeverMeasured` — selector small-N AND journal path carry `.identity` + render "assumed", chip never says "measured"; `realFitsCarryTheirMethodProvenance` — Platt/isotonic stamp + exact chip titles; `chipTitleSaysMeasuredIffProvenanceIsMeasured` — all-methods honesty invariant); F14 pins (`datedDisjointCalendarsAreUnknownNotFabricated` — disjoint/thin-overlap/short/zero-variance → nil; `datedAlignedSeriesMatchesHandDerivedPearson` — hand-derived r=0.8007912959402325, cross-hour UTC-day bucketing; `datedCrossCalendarOverlapUsesOnlyTheIntersection` — hold=2×cand on the 5 common days → r=+1 exactly vs positional ≈0.425; `datedMixedCoverageScoresTheAlignedHoldingAndTalliesTheSkipped`); F12 pin (`journalCalibrationCacheMemoizesAndInvalidates` — compute-once on repeat reads, identical to direct fit, mutation → recompute + changed result). One fixture updated: `StockSageBuildIdeasDirectTests` direct-constructor calibration now passes `method: .isotonicWilson` (display metadata only). MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md sections updated for all touched files.
**Result:** ✅ `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` · ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,506 test-case passes, 0 failures). Honesty floor: no fabricated coefficients (nil = unknown), estimates labeled, "measured" only when genuinely measured. Owner-held gates untouched.

## 2026-07-03 · Math red-team fixes: D-1 Beta drop-refit re-anchor · D-2 allInCost deletion · D-3 MonteCarlo guard
**Files:** Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift; Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibrationSelectorTests.swift; Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageNetEdge.swift; Salehman AITests/StockSageNetEdgeTests.swift; Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageMonteCarloRuin.swift; Salehman AITests/StockSageMonteCarloRuinTests.swift; MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md; plans/PLAN_2026-07-03_d1_beta_reanchor.md
**What & why:** (D-1, HIGH — AUDIT_2026-07-03_math_redteam.md + o1 adversarial re-derivation) fitBeta's dropA/dropB branches clamped a negative surviving slope to 0 but KEPT the intercept co-fitted with that discarded slope, shipping a flat win-prob map above the sample base rate (pinned o1 failing input: n=40, base 0.575 → shipped 0.768; ~4.4% of inverted-sample runs shipped this through the standard selector route — "selector masks it" was disproved). Now a clamped surviving slope triggers an intercept-only refit (honest base-rate MLE, labeled .interceptOnly), mirroring the Platt A-clamp B-re-anchor; comment-only note added on the irls init sign-flip nit (loss-rate log-odds under the σ(+z) convention; harmless at convergence). 2 fixture tests hand-derived via standalone replica (/tmp/derive_d1_beta.swift), shown red pre-fix (0.768285 / 0.231715) and green post-fix (0.575000 / 0.425000); all pre-existing CalibrationSelectorTests pins green UNMODIFIED (test-file diff: 69 insertions, 0 deletions). (D-2, latent — resolves logged "#3 takerFeeBps dead-code unit trap") deleted dead AllInCost/allInCost(): its taker leg charged ×2 per-fill vs evaluate()/roundTripBps' round-trip convention (90 vs the labeled 70bps on crypto); zero live callers (grep-proven), orphan test deleted, stale MARKETS_TAB_MAP consumer claim (MarketsView never called it) corrected; two stale comment lines inside StockSageNetEdge.swift that still referenced the deleted symbol were reworded (plan gap — its own census expectation required zero app references). (D-3, LOW) MonteCarloRuin.simulate guard floors at max(1, minTrades) — minTrades ≤ 0 with an empty log previously reached `% UInt64(0)` (runtime trap, proven red: suite run crashed at the trap test, xcresult "Crash: Salehman AI at … StockSageMonteCarloRuinTests.swift line 41", standalone check printed "Fatal error: Division by zero in remainder operation"); trap test added.
**Result:** ✅ full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 0 failures; @Test count 1502 → 1504 (−1 orphan +2 calibration +1 MonteCarlo); all three new tests named passed in the log (betaClampedDropARefitAnchorsAtHonestBaseRate, betaClampedDropBRefitAnchorsAtHonestBaseRate, degenerateMinTradesZeroReturnsNilInsteadOfTrapping). Branch diff vs fork point 0f32d31 (before this entry): MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md 10±, ConvictionCalibration.swift +27/−2, MonteCarloRuin.swift +3/−1, NetEdge.swift −38/+3, CalibrationSelectorTests +69, MonteCarloRuinTests +9, NetEdgeTests −15, plans/PLAN +661. Honesty floor strengthened: a clamped Beta fit can no longer overstate the sample base rate. [FALSIFIED same day — the guarantee held only in the mid-band the fixtures sat in; see the D-1b entry below for the measurement and the fix.] SOURCE_BUNDLE regen + merge deferred to the shipping-changes pipeline when this branch lands.

## 2026-07-03 · D-1b — the D-1 re-anchor itself diverged and could OVERSTATE at extreme base rates (falsified claim corrected)
**Files:** Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift; Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibrationSelectorTests.swift; MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md
**What & why:** Adversarial re-check of the D-1 fix proved its own honesty claim false. `irls(false,false)` (the intercept-only re-anchor) inits the intercept at ln((nNeg+1)/(nPos+1)) — under fitBeta's p = σ(+z) convention that is the LOSS-rate log-odds — and the undamped 1-D Newton started on the wrong side oscillates past the MLE and diverges under the 25-iter cap. Exact enumeration (the refit depends only on the counts; scratchpad verify_d1_enum.py, re-run this session): 12,948/44,850 (n,nPos) pairs 2≤n≤300 fail to anchor within 1e-6, 6,474 OVERSTATE, worst +0.9565 (n=23,nPos=1: base 0.0435 shipped as flat 1.0). End-to-end in real Swift (byte-matched replica, scratchpad derive_d1b_witness.swift): a 31-row base-28/31=0.903226 sample routes full-fit a0=+1029.9/b0=−333.6 → dropB → a1=−9.07e7≤0 → the NEW re-anchor → divergent c=6.60e8 → flat winProb 1.000000 (+0.096774); the same sample on pre-D-1 code shipped 0.000000 (understated, floor-safe) — i.e. D-1 as first shipped converted floor-safe understatements into floor-breaching overstatements in the extreme-base-rate regime, while the two mid-band fixtures (0.575/0.425) stayed green. Root cause isolated by enumeration: correcting only the init sign anchors ALL 44,850 pairs. Fix (o1-remediation option b): the intercept-only path now inits at the exact MLE ln(nPos/nNeg) (the score nPos−n·σ(c) is 0 there; Newton converges immediately; enumeration-proven exact, worst |dev| 3e-16) — one edit inside `irls` covering all three `irls(false,false)` call sites: both-slopes-negative (pre-existing on main with the SAME latent divergence, wrongly asserted convergent by both the audit and the o1 review — they verified only mid-range base rates), clamped-dropA, clamped-dropB. The falsified "harmless at convergence" comment rewritten to state the measured truth. Full/2-param paths keep the legacy init untouched (converged fits init-independent; existing fixtures pin their non-converged landings; any divergent fit they emit terminates in the fixed re-anchor). 31-row witness fixture added (standalone-derived; red-first).
**Result:** ✅ red-first: pre-fix suite run `** TEST FAILED **` with exactly the new witness test failing (shipped flat 1.000000); post-fix FULL suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 0 failures; @Test 1504 → 1505; both existing D-1 fixtures byte-identical pre/post the init fix (measured by the derive script). NOW the honesty claim holds by enumeration, not just at the fixtures: a clamped/degenerate Beta fit anchors at the exact sample base rate for every (n,nPos) pair, 2≤n≤300.
