## 2026-07-09 · Research: turn-of-month ETF cache probe (token-free interim measurement)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_probe.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Continued the queued turn-of-month candidate by executing a token-free real-data probe directly against `~/Library/Application Support/salehman_history_cache.json`. Verified cache epoch semantics and scope first (global cache 2025-07-07→2026-07-08, ~252 bars by design), then measured a fixed TOM window on 10 liquid ETFs (SPY/VOO/QQQ/DIA/IWM/VTI/XLF/XLK/XLE/XLI): enter last trading day close, exit +3 trading-days close, net of 8bps round-trip ETF cost.
**Result:** All symbols had `n=12` TOM windows (underpowered). Aggregate mean TOM return remained positive in this slice (gross +0.389%/mo, net +0.309%/mo), but TOM net Sharpe exceeded buy-and-hold monthly Sharpe in only 1/10 symbols. Logged as INTERIM/UNDERPOWERED evidence only; not promotion-eligible, no code change/wiring, frontier stays open pending a multi-year split-adjusted ETF panel run with full walk-forward significance + DSR gating.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: stricter TOM walk-forward sweep on same cache (offset sweep + trial accounting)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_sweep.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Ran the requested stricter TOM variant on the exact same cache dataset, adding offset sweep and explicit trial accounting to reduce overfitting risk. Sweep spec: entry offsets {-2,-1} trading days before month-end, exit offsets {+3,+4} trading days after month-start (4 trials). Per symbol, selected best net-Sharpe config only after evaluating all sweep configs, then applied conservative trial-aware significance screen.
**Result:** Best-of-sweep point estimates rose (aggregate best-config net mean +0.560%/mo across 10 ETFs), but evidence remains weak after accounting for trial selection: TOM net Sharpe beat buy-and-hold monthly Sharpe in only 2/10 symbols and 0/10 symbols passed trial-accounted significance. With cache span still 2025-07-07→2026-07-08 (`n=12` windows/symbol), this is explicitly STILL UNDERPOWERED and non-promotional. No code changes/wiring; candidate remains open pending multi-year panel run.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM pooled max-t continuation (same cache, stronger trial control)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_maxt.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Tightened trial accounting again on the same cache by moving from per-symbol best-config heuristics to a pooled max-over-config test with permutation null calibration. Built pooled equal-symbol monthly TOM net return series for all four offset configs and tested the maximum t-statistic under month-level sign-flip permutations (`B=4000`) to account for sweep selection explicitly.
**Result:** Best config `(-2,+3)` produced pooled mean net +0.574%/mo with Sharpe 0.423 and t=1.404, but selection-adjusted max-t p-value was 0.1725 (not significant). Comparator buy-and-hold pooled monthly Sharpe remained higher (0.620). This reinforces the underpowered/no-overclaim disposition: still no promotion case, no wiring changes, candidate remains open pending a multi-year powered panel.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM holdout continuation (train-locked config, no holdout reselection)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_holdout.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Continued strict anti-overfit progression on the same cache by introducing an explicit holdout protocol: select best offset config on TRAIN only (first 6 months), lock it, and evaluate unchanged on HOLDOUT (last 6 months). This removes holdout reselection and tests out-of-split stability under the same 4-config sweep.
**Result:** Locked config from TRAIN was `(-1,+3)`. TRAIN: mean net +0.2839%/mo, Sharpe 0.267, t=0.596. HOLDOUT: mean net +0.3341%/mo, Sharpe 0.810, t=1.811; holdout Sharpe exceeded pooled ETF buy-and-hold holdout Sharpe (0.594). Despite that directional lift, evidence remains underpowered (`n=6` months/split, non-significant t), so disposition stays non-promotional: no wiring/activation change; TOM candidate remains open for multi-year panel validation.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM LOOMO continuation (locked-config month fragility)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_loomo.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Added a leave-one-month-out fragility check on the same cache and locked config `(-1,+3)` to quantify month-level dependence after the holdout step. This tests whether the estimate is robust to single-month perturbations under current sample constraints.
**Result:** Baseline (12 months) remained weak: mean +0.3090%/mo, Sharpe 0.383, t=1.269. LOOMO ranges were wide (mean +0.1219% to +0.4127%/mo, Sharpe 0.226 to 0.541, t 0.714 to 1.710), with strongest influence from dropping 2025-12 (largest deterioration) and 2025-10/2025-07 (largest improvements). This confirms month-level fragility in the 1y cache slice; still underpowered and non-promotional. No wiring/activation changes.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM nonparam continuation (sign-test + bootstrap CI)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_nonparam.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Continued strict TOM validation on the same cache with nonparametric diagnostics to reduce normality reliance in a tiny sample: exact two-sided sign-test plus bootstrap 95% CI for pooled monthly mean returns across all sweep configs and the locked config.
**Result:** Locked config `(-1,+3)` remained non-significant (sign-test `p=0.3877`) with bootstrap 95% CI crossing zero (`-0.1227%` to `+0.8068%`/mo). Best-by-mean sweep config `(-2,+3)` also remained non-significant (`p=0.7744`) with CI crossing zero (`-0.1502%` to `+1.4037%`/mo). Across the full 4-config sweep, every bootstrap CI crossed zero. Disposition unchanged: still underpowered/non-promotional; no wiring/activation changes.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM exact sign-flip continuation (single + family-wise)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_exact_signflip.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Executed exact finite-sample randomization tests on the same pooled TOM month series (`n=12`) to remove asymptotic dependence and tighten trial-aware inference. Added both single-config exact sign-flip tests and an exact family-wise max-|mean| test across the 4 sweep configurations.
**Result:** Locked `(-1,+3)` exact two-sided sign-flip `p=0.243652`; best-by-mean `(-2,+3)` exact `p=0.190430`. Family-wise exact max-|mean| over sweep returned `p=0.324219`. Exact finite-sample inference remains non-significant and consistent with prior max-t/holdout/LOOMO/nonparam outcomes. No wiring/activation changes.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM cost-stress continuation (0 to 24 bps)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_cost_stress.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Added cost-assumption stress testing on the same cache for locked `(-1,+3)` and best-by-mean `(-2,+3)` configs, sweeping round-trip cost `{0,8,16,24}` bps to check whether modest implementation-friction changes alter conclusions.
**Result:** Means and t-stats degraded monotonically with higher costs for both configs; at every cost point sign-tests remained non-significant and bootstrap CIs crossed zero. Cost robustness therefore does not support promotion on this sample. No wiring/activation changes.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM LOSO continuation (leave-one-symbol-out)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_cache_loso.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Added concentration-dependence diagnostics by re-running the locked config `(-1,+3)` while dropping one ETF at a time from the 10-symbol panel (LOSO), to detect single-constituent dominance risk.
**Result:** LOSO mean range stayed modestly positive (`+0.2686%` to `+0.3279%`/mo), with no single drop causing collapse or creating significance. All LOSO variants remained non-significant and bootstrap CIs crossed zero. Conclusion unchanged: underpowered/non-promotional; no wiring/activation changes.

## 2026-07-09 · Wave 7 follow-up: conviction-scaled risk export + a11y parity
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** A 5-lens parallel review of the wave-7 diff converged on two parity gaps: (1) `copyAllText` exported the plan WITHOUT the conviction-scaled risk line the on-screen row shows, so a pasted plan silently reverted to the flat base risk% (EXPORT-01 precedent: exports carry every on-screen risk fact); (2) the row's `accessibilityLabel` never spoke the scaled-risk line or its "scales size, not odds" caveat (TODAY-A11Y-02 precedent: spoken labels mirror visible honesty copy). Fixed both: the export line now appends `| conviction-scaled risk X.XX% (regime ×N.NN) — scales size, not odds` when `scaledRiskFraction` resolves, and the a11y label speaks the same sentence. Display-only additions — no ranking/gate/sizing math changed.
**Result:** Two new tests pin the contract with hand-derived fixtures (from the scaler SPEC, not the implementation: c=0.9 → min(1.5, 0.5+0.9)=1.4 → 0.01·1.4·1.2 = 1.68%; c=0.5 → 1.0 → 1.20%): `copyAllTextCarriesConvictionScaledRiskParityWithTheRow` and `copyAllTextOmitsScaledRiskWhenRiskFractionMissing`. Focused suite verdict: `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (`xcodebuild test -only-testing:"Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests"`), new tests confirmed executed on both parallel runners.

## 2026-07-09 · TOM owner-activation + full ideas-card/StockSage audit (3-lens) — consistency sweep + fairness/disclosure fixes
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTomGateTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `skills/stocksage-flags-and-config/SKILL.md` (+ `.claude` mirror), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Full audit (3 independent read-only lenses: board/card UI honesty, engine correctness, docs/tests integrity) found the tree in a self-contradictory TOM state after the owner-ordered activation ("WIRE ACTIVATE"): code had `turnOfMonthEnabled = true` + full rankByEV/bestOpportunity wiring, while the flag's own doc comment, `StockSageTomGateTests` (FAILING — suite was red), MARKETS_TAB_MAP, research/INDEX, this log, and the flags-catalog skill all still claimed "default OFF / not wired". Fixes, aligned to the owner-activated state (activation is an owner call; the evidence verdict stays underpowered/OPEN): (1) flag doc-comment rewritten to the truth (activated, gates only the rank tilt, no advise()/sizing branch); (2) gate test re-pinned to the ratified state (`turnOfMonthFlagMatchesOwnerActivatedState`) + new flag-off inertness test (`seasonalityBonusIsInertWhenFlagIsOff`, hard `#expect(… == 0)` with a strong reliable month fixture); (3) ENGINE consistency: `bestOpportunity` now adds the seasonal bonus AFTER the netRatio cost-scaling — the same `key + bonus − penalties` placement `rankByEV` uses — so board and card can't diverge in near-ties when netRatio < 1; (4) FAIRNESS: seasonality was a navigation-dependent rank input (fetched only on sheet-open → a ±bonus applied only to symbols the user happened to view); added `refreshSeasonalityForTopIdeas(limit: 15)` mirroring the earnings top-N prefetch, kicked at scan commit, gated on the flag; (5) DISCLOSURE: ideas header now shows a one-line "seasonal month tilt" note when the flag is on and seasonality data exists (invisible ranking factors violate the honesty floor); (6) doc sweep: MARKETS_TAB_MAP advisor entry, research/INDEX dated UPDATE on the status-lock line (never deleted), flags-catalog skill two→three flags + TOM row (repo + `.claude` mirror synced). Audit triage also RECORDED without change: velocity surfaces deliberately exempt from the tilt (monthly tendency has no EV/day unit meaning — doc'd on the helper); "bonus scored before cost gate" lens claim REJECTED (rankVal only runs on already-gated candidates); netRatio nil→1 optimistic fallback HELD (pre-existing, acknowledged in-code); "Est. EV" label drift + rationale lineLimit truncation HELD for a display wave with visual QA.

## 2026-07-09 · Research: TOM multi-year POWERED panel (10 ETFs × 10y) — POWERED NULL, lane's exit clause answered
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-09_tom_etf_multiyear_panel.md`, `research/INDEX.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Executed the exact run every 1y TOM note named as blocking ("multi-year split-adjusted ETF panel with walk-forward significance + DSR gating"), unblocked by the same-day 96daf89 proof that Yahoo v8 multi-year fetches work (paced, minimal UA). Fetched 10y adjusted closes for the 1y chain's exact 10-ETF panel (2,512 bars/symbol, 2016-07-11→2026-07-08, all HTTP 200), giving n=120 pooled month windows vs the chain's n=12. Protocol pre-registered: locked config `(-1,+3)` @ 8bps carried unchanged from the 1y train-lock; secondary 4-config sweep under joint family-wise sign-flip control, cost stress {0,8,16,24}bps, split halves, LOSO; DSR/PSR from the SHIPPED `StockSageDeflatedSharpe` compiled verbatim by swiftc (trials=4, measured varTrialSharpe=0.000260).
**Result:** **NULL, powered and decisive.** Locked: +0.2223%/mo net, t=1.178, sign-flip p=0.2423, bootstrap CI [−0.1412%, +0.5860%]; family-wise p=0.2619; shipped-engine DSR locked 0.8311 / best 0.8678 — all `passes=false`; non-significant even at zero cost (t=1.602, p=0.113). Decisive split: H1 2016–21 +0.4444%/mo (t=1.663) vs **H2 2021–26 +0.0001%/mo (t=0.000)** — the 1y cache positivity was noise from a dead stretch. TOM beat own B&H monthly Sharpe in 1/10 symbols; LOSO t-range 1.03–1.26 (no single-name dependence). Research lane's multi-year exit clause ANSWERED; the owner-activated `turnOfMonthEnabled` tilt untouched (deactivation is equally owner-gated — decision now evidence-sharp: keep as disclosed preference or order deactivation). Docs-only, no code change.

## 2026-07-09 · Review-fleet fix wave on the unpushed VS Code delta (3 Fable-xhigh lenses, 12 verified findings fixed pre-push)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionScaler.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift`, `Salehman AITests/TomFlagTestLock.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageTomGateTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageConvictionScalerTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Before pushing the VS Code session's waves 2-7 + TOM-activation snapshot, a 3-lens adversarial review fleet (engine-math / UI-honesty / tests-docs, all Fable-xhigh) was run on the full unpushed delta; every finding below was verified against the code before fixing. ENGINE: (1) `seasonalityRankBonus` was direction-blind — a SELL idea on a seasonally-rising name was BOOSTED (the inverse of the short's EV); now side-signed (buy +, sell −, hold/avoid 0; `bestOpportunity` was already buy-family-only). (2) `summary()` was a FIFTH `bestOpportunity` call site without seasonality — the money-velocity headline, its copyable playbook, and `velocityHistory` could crown a different best than the tilted board; `summary()` now takes+passes `seasonality:` and both MarketsView call sites thread `store.seasonality`. (3) `StockSageConvictionScaler`'s absolute 0.5% floor, designed for the documented 1% base, UP-scaled a smaller user base (0.1% risk → displayed "conviction-scaled risk 0.50%", 5× the configured budget — dangerous direction); floor is now `min(0.5%, base×0.5)`, byte-identical for base ≥ 1%. (4) `refreshSeasonalityForTopIdeas` prefetched in advisor-score order, not the EV order the tilt affects — now prefetches the top-15 of the UNTILTED EV rank. UI HONESTY: (5) Today-card headers claimed "ranked by growth rate" over the `.equityExecutableFirst` sort (equity→gate→…→velocity); headers + `copyAllText` header (new `mode:` param) now state the true order. (6) `weeklyExecutedVsBlockedMetric` fabricated an ▲/▼ improving/deteriorating verdict from unit-incompatible quantities (avg NET realized R/trade minus summed GROSS velocity R/week); verdict removed, facts kept with gross/net labels. (7) `paperOutcomePanel` was headed "(net-of-cost)" over a GROSS planned leg; legs now labeled. (8) `executionRecommendationPanel` hard-coded "#1" for a row that can render at #2/#3 (first non-blocked pick) — now captions the real rank; its non-urgent order-type default prescribed the "patient limit / uninformed" leg the indexed 2026-07-03 research says SIGN-FLIPS for conviction-directional ideas — now presents the cost trade-off without prescribing the flipped leg. (9) TOM disclosure line rendered under velocity/conviction/R:R sorts where the tilt is NOT in effect (inverse honesty error) — now gated on `ideaSort == .ev`. (10) Row/export/VoiceOver scaled-risk lines state the printed share count uses the BASE risk % (was two contradictory size prescriptions in one line). TESTS: (11) new `TomFlagTestLock` (NSLock, mirrors ToolPolicyTestLock) guards every read-under-assertion/mutation of the cross-suite process-global `turnOfMonthEnabled` (TomGate suite + the EV activation test raced); (12) TIGHT straddle fixtures pin the |t|<1 noise gate at t=0.98974 (gated) / t=1.00896 (passes) — the originals sat at 0.54/4.04, leaving the constant unpinned across (0.54, 4.04) — plus new hand-derived tests: sell-inversion (−0.018 mirror), neutral-action zero, summary-parity, and 3 scaler-floor pins (0.0007 pass-through, 0.0005 = base×0.5 floor, 1%-base behavior unchanged). ALSO records for the tests-docs lens: the 474041d snapshot's |t|<1 noise gate, `MonthStat.stdDev`/`tStat`, and the in-progress-month exclusion in `StockSageSeasonality.compute(now:)` (measured: Yahoo 1mo bars include the partial current month — a +8.3% partial-July MTD move would otherwise be credited as a full July sample in the exact bucket the tilt reads) shipped in that commit without their own log entry — documented here and in the five updated MARKETS_TAB_MAP entries (ExpectedValue/ConvictionScaler/Seasonality/TodayPlan/MarketsTodayActionsCard).
**Result:** Full independent gate re-run after the fixes — verdict pasted in the shipping commit trail; all new tests hand-derived from spec (never from the code under test). The rebase-overlap BLOCKER both lenses raised (waves rewrote the exact `copyAllText`/`build()`/MarketsView regions origin's Round-G/H touched) is handled in the reconcile step by hand-composition — Round-G's `StockSageInput` Kelly seam and Round-H's `priceAsOf`/"PRICE NOT LIVE" plumbing must survive alongside wave-7's scaled-risk line.

(3) **Cost table — RE-RATIFIED with one revision:** `.SR` gets a dedicated Tadawul tier, 60bps RT (spread 20 + slippage 10 + fees 30, assetClass "intl (Tadawul)" so the detail sheet's cost line discloses the tier) per RESEARCH_2026-07-03_current_era_costs.md §2 (Saudi fees ALONE 24–36bps RT, 3/3-verified — the flat 30 was the dangerous-direction understatement on the app's own Saudi-first core); US 13 / liquid-intl 30 / crypto 70 / FX 7 / index 8 ratified as-is (FX/ETF deliberately err SAFE; crypto 70 = the research's own Kraken-class midpoint — venue-aware needs a venue input the app doesn't have, recorded as a feature candidate). Contract tests re-pinned to the NEW spec values from the research doc (the legitimate re-ratification path), plus a companion pin that non-Saudi intl stays 30 (the tier must not leak) and a hand-derived `weeklyRNet` contract (net exists with gross, strictly below it on a costed lane, equals the standalone net function under identical inputs).
**Review round (1 Fable-xhigh adversarial lens on the diff, 4 findings, all fixed pre-push):** (MAJOR) the net Est./week tooltip routed through `weeklyGrossHelp`, whose turnover-note tail calls the annotated number "this gross figure" — false for the net headline; `weeklyGrossHelp` gains `netFigure:` and swaps the tail to "already charged in this net figure" on the net branch only. (MINOR×3) `playbook()`'s copyable weekly line was still gross-only — now net-first with labeled gross beside it and a labeled-gross fallback; the "Since last session"/trend lines display the deliberately-gross history unlabeled under a net headline — both now say "gross weekly-R"/"gross wk-R"; `weeklyDollarsNet` was missing from `MoneyVelocityCopy.all`, silently escaping the structural hedge-word guard — added. Reviewer also PROVED the card-net == strip-net equality arg-by-arg (lane derivation, tradingDays lane-count equivalence, concentration factor) and the `.SR` tier's non-leakage + research-citation fidelity.

## 2026-07-09 · UNIVERSE 210→1,024: §0.5 verification COMPLETE — 1,024-row verified manifest + timing shipped; week-long throttle block root-caused as a process-fingerprint artifact
**Files:** `research/UNIVERSE_VERIFIED_2026-07-03.md` (new — IN-1 manifest + IN-2 timing block), `plans/PLAN_2026-07-03_universe_1024.md` (§0.5 BLOCKED → PRECONDITIONS MET), `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** The owner's queued expansion ("increase the equities from 210 to 1024… make no mistakes", 2026-07-02) has been execution-blocked on its §0.5 precondition since 2026-07-03 — the verify lane hard-aborted on `.SR` 429s. Root cause found by measurement this session: **the throttle is a process/stack fingerprint, not an IP cooldown** — python urllib drew 3×429 on `2222.SR` while a URLSession/CFNetwork request with the IDENTICAL UA and URL (the app's own `StockSageQuoteService` shape) returned HTTP 200 in the same minute. Fix: `universe_fetch.swift` (40-line URLSession CLI in the durable `~/.claude/salehman-universe/` kit) swapped in as the verifier's HTTP layer. The full §0.5 lane then ran clean in resumable 250-request chunks (~2s pacing, core-first, 3×429 hard-abort budget): **209/210 core re-verified + 815 additions = 1,024 verified names, 0 candidate failures, 0×429 across ~1,435 requests.** One ruling under the same-day owner gate-lift: **ROG.SW dropped from the kept core** — persistent HTTP 404 on the app's own path (retried + re-probed 3×), i.e. dead on Yahoo AND already failing every live production scan (it sits in `StockSageQuoteService:355`'s Zurich group) — a bug fix, with the additions quota raised 814→815 to keep the owner's exact-1,024 target. IN-2 timing measured at concurrency 6: N=209, p50=0.314s, p90=0.356s, rate429=0.0%, wall 11.5s; the plan's FILL-W hand-derivation gives watchdog=120s at C=6 (the existing 120s watchdog suffices). Manifest self-checks all passed (1,024 rows, 0 dupes, known groups only, no non-equity additions).
**Result:** IN-1/IN-2 delivered per the plan's §0.6 interface contract; plan §0.5 flipped to PRECONDITIONS MET (original block kept verbatim as historical). Next: the fill-in step (⟦FILL⟧ tokens from the measured numbers) and the implementation phase (worktree `ideas-card/universe-1024`, engine work, MANDATORY visual QA on the 1,024-row board). Docs-only commit — no app code changed.

## 2026-07-09 · Paper-store lost-update fix (autonomy tick 8): cross-process saves no longer clobber closes — found from LIVE store data
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePaperTrader.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSagePaperTraderTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Tick 4's C2 measurement flagged GE/SAN.MC/O39.SI each holding TWO open paper trades — investigated this tick. The pairs are CROSS-scan (07-07 and 07-08 legs) and BOTH open while the store holds ZERO closes anywhere: impossible in one process, because `step()` only re-opens a symbol after a close, and `isOpen` is computed from `closedAt` (a close cannot silently un-flag). The only consistent explanation: **two app instances** (the owner's app and this Mac's QA launches run concurrently) each hold the whole trades array in memory and `save()` wrote it wholesale — last-writer-wins clobbered the other process's closes (resurrected as open) and could drop its opens. Fix: `save()` now reconciles with the on-disk array before writing — per id, a CLOSED record beats an open one (closes are terminal; markToMarket only moves open→closed), and ids this process has never seen are preserved. `remove()`/`reset()` bypass reconciliation (explicit deletions — a merged save would resurrect them; the ponytail ceiling is named in-code: tombstones if removal-under-concurrency ever matters). The three existing dupes self-heal: both legs are real records and will mark-to-market independently on the next scan. Three new two-store tests pin the contract: a stale process's save cannot resurrect a closed trade; foreign opens survive a stale save; reset/remove still delete.
**Result:** Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` 0 failures; all three reconciliation tests executed by name. Engine-store change only — no UI surface, no visual QA owed.

## 2026-07-09 · Journal-store lost-update fix (autonomy tick 9): the MONEY-CRITICAL journal had the paper store's exact clobber defect
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageJournal.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageJournalTests.swift`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Defect-class sweep after tick 8's paper-store fix: every whole-array UserDefaults store was checked for the same cross-process last-writer-wins hazard. `StockSageJournalStore` — the REAL trade journal feeding `convictionCalibration` (win-prob), the drawdown brake, and every analytics panel — had the identical pattern (`save()` = wholesale array write to `.standard`), meaning a second app instance could silently resurrect a closed real trade as open and lose its realized outcome from calibration. Same fix as the paper store: `save(reconciling:)` merges with disk first (per id a CLOSED record beats an open one; foreign ids preserved), `remove()` bypasses (explicit deletion, ponytail ceiling noted), `qaSeed` still never touches disk. Store gains the paper store's injectable `defaults:`/`key:` seam (production singleton unchanged — only the `.shared` constructor exists). Three mirrored two-store tests pin the contract. SWEEP DISPOSITION for the remaining stores: `StockSageScanDelta` (:97) is a single latest-snapshot — last-writer-wins IS its correct semantics, no change; `StockSagePortfolio` (:59, real positions) and `StockSageVelocityHistory` (:116, per-day series) share the hazard class but have DIFFERENT merge semantics (position edits conflict; day-key union) — queued as C8 with analysis rather than rushed (money-critical portfolio merge deserves its own derivation).
**Result:** Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` 0 failures; all three journal reconciliation tests executed by name. Engine-store change only — no UI surface.

## 2026-07-09 · C8: lost-update hardening completes the store sweep — Portfolio (real position book) + VelocityHistory (durable trend series)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePortfolio.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageVelocityHistory.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSagePortfolioTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageVelocityHistoryTests.swift`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** Completes the cross-process lost-update sweep (paper store tick 8, journal tick 9) with per-store merge DERIVATIONS rather than a copy-paste: **Portfolio** — the owner's real position book; lots are IMMUTABLE once added (add/remove/clear only, no in-place edits exist), so the only hazard is a stale whole-array write DROPPING another instance's lot → reconcile by preserving foreign ids (per-id conflicts impossible); remove/clear bypass (explicit deletions, ponytail ceiling noted). **VelocityHistory** — the durable per-day series behind "since last session"; a stale write deleted other days' snapshots forever → reconcile by DAY-KEY union (this process's days win — its record() is the newest scan here; disk-only days preserved), re-sorted and re-capped to 60 exactly like record(); no deletion API exists, so every save reconciles. VelocityHistory gains the injectable `defaults:`/`key:` seam (Portfolio already had one). Three new two-store tests pin the contracts (foreign lot survives a stale save; remove/clear still delete; a stale process cannot delete another day's snapshot, order preserved). All four whole-array stores are now reconciling; `StockSageScanDelta` stays last-writer-wins BY DESIGN (single latest-snapshot semantics — documented in tick 9's entry).
**Result:** Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` 0 failures; all three C8 tests executed by name. Engine-store changes only — no UI surface.

## 2026-07-09 · Momentum-sign ablation — NULL; the campaign-milestone row's last named residual CLOSES (both sign directions now measured)

**What & why:** The corpus sweep named the momentum-sign sim variant as the sharpest open empirical cell (shipped `irrxWeights` is reversal-by-construction; 15/20 reversal configs were negative even gross on the 5y panel). Ran it fetch-free on the persisted 61-name/5y earnings-exclusion panel via a design→implement→2-verifier workflow: verbatim shipped NetCostSim+DeflatedSharpe (repo clean at 0c58648, SHA256s logged), generic weight-loop port-validated 30/30 bit-exact, reversal anchors reproduced exactly (0.541/0.553), anti-symmetry property exact-0, trials=60 honest accounting. **Result: NULL — 0/60 clear DSR>0.95; sign-flip long-short best 0.578; long-only best 0.911 beta-flagged by the EQW paired-diff guard (diffDSR ≤0.481 everywhere).** Both adversarial verifiers not-refuted (byte-identical independent recompile+rerun; independent loop re-implementation + look-ahead mutation probe); one implementer evidence inaccuracy caught and recorded (net-identity residual max 1.388e-17, not 6.939e-18 — 5 orders under the gate either way). Nothing wired; fences stand; remaining milestone shots are non-price data classes + delisting-inclusive substrate.
**Result:** Docs+tools only (no app code — no build impact); committed + pushed; CI verify by log.

## 2026-07-09 · CALIBRATION RUNTIME ACTIVATION — the selector goes from nominal to operational (owner-selected lever from the corpus sweep)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `StockSageStore.swift`, `StockSageStrategyBacktest.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Tools/QASnapshots.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageCalibrationSnapshotTests.swift` (new, 8 tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` (4 entries), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** The corpus sweep measured the shipped calibration selector as DATA-STARVED in production: `candidateSelectorEnabled=true` but the fit existed only in-memory after a manual Strategy-Backtest button press, and the real journal is empty — so runtime winProb collapsed to the 0.35+0.23·c prior in nearly every session. Now: the selected fit persists (`stocksage.calibration.v1`, binary-plist Codable snapshot with provenance {method, source, fittedAt, sampleCount, oosBrier=nil-honest}); restores at store init (decode-fail ⇒ prior); auto-fits OFFLINE from HistoryCache only (scan-end + launch-when-stale>7d; zero new network; thin cache ⇒ honest no-op — "win% assumed" persisting on a 252-bar cache is correct, the selector's thin-data floor working); manual backtest also persists. Safety-by-construction: the F01 identity clamp means an identity winner renders byte-identical to pre-change (pinned by test). Guards from the adversarial review (Fable APPROVE + 5 findings, all fixed) + design risks: `isRunningTests` gate (unit tests never spawn fits or write real defaults), recency-vs-richness persist rule (a rich fresh 5y fit is never displaced by a thin 1y scan fit; recency wins only at the 7d staleness boundary — orchestrator adjudication), `qaSuppressPersistedCalibration` single-choke-point QA determinism (all EV/chip consumers route through the getter), IL-23-class positive-path test (synthetic >205-bar trending histories ⇒ fit != nil, method != identity — would FAIL if the fit silently no-opped), injectable-defaults hermetic tests, fittedAt contract aligned with all three writers. UI: chip chrome byte-unchanged; one provenance sentence appends to the tooltip only when a non-identity persisted fit is effective ("Fit from <source> · data as of <date> · n=<count>").
**Result:** Worktree gate `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,816→1,824 passing, 0 failures) at impl + after the 6-fix wave; independent orchestrator gate on the squash-merged main tree: see verdict below in this entry's commit; snapshot QA all-surfaces (fixtures render the assumed path deterministically via the F6 suppressor); CI verify by log.

## 2026-07-09 · COST-TIER SCOPING — dedicated EM-suffix tier in defaultCosts; US small-cap half SKIPPED (no honest classifier)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageNetEdge.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageNetEdgeTests.swift`, `research/INDEX.md`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`
**What & why:** RESEARCH_2026-07-03_current_era_costs.md §2's residual dangerous-direction exposure (flagged in the 2026-07-09 corpus-reconciliation caveat): non-`.SR` small/illiquid/EM international names sat on the flat 30bps intl tier despite evidence for a 60–100+bps band. Part 1 (shipped): `defaultCosts(forSymbol:)` gains a private `emSuffixes` set (`.NS .BO .SS .SA .MX .AE .QA .CA .JO` — the genuinely-EM suffixes present in `StockSageUniverse`'s groups, plus `.BO` (no catalog symbol today — inert, and any future .BO name lands on the HIGHER tier)) checked AFTER the `.SR` branch and BEFORE the generic `.` intl branch; matches get `spreadBps 20 + slippageBps 10 + takerFeeBps 30 = 60bps`, `assetClass "intl (EM)"` — the same band-bottom anchor the `.SR` precedent used, decomposition identical to intl's spread/slippage with the fee leg carrying the estimate (UNLIKE `.SR`'s measured 24–36bps Tadawul fee leg, this 30 is a band-derived ESTIMATE per the code comment). Deliberately EXCLUDED: every developed-market suffix (`.L .DE .PA .AS .MC .MI .ST .SW .SI .T .HK .AX .TO`, which §2 ratifies as accurate at 30) and `.KS`/`.TW` — MSCI calls Korea/Taiwan EM but the universe's holdings there (005930.KS Samsung, 2330.TW TSMC) trade at developed-grade microstructure; the EM evidence can't honestly be cited for them. Part 2 (skipped, recorded): US small-cap re-tiering was refused — grepped `StockSage/` + `Views/` for `marketCap`: zero hits; the only encoded distinction (curated `groups` vs the mktcap-ranked `catalogExtra` "(2000-expansion)") is PROVENANCE not cap (the expansion set contains BRK-A/BRK-B/TSM/ASML/SONY/TM/SHEL/AZN/NVS/UNP — verified mega-caps), so tiering by expansion-membership would attach a false small-cap label even in the "safe" direction, which the honesty floor forbids; the crypto precedent's ADV-dollar liquidity path needs history at the call site and `defaultCosts(forSymbol:)` is called symbol-only from many sites (re-pointing it is explicitly fenced in the source). Display: `assetClass "intl (EM)"` flows to the existing cost-line string interpolation (MarketsView.swift, both the sheet R:R fusion line and the net-edge ledger row) with ZERO Views/ diff — pixel-verified live (see Result). Tests: re-pinned `defaultCostsScaleByAssetClass` (RELIANCE.NS → 'intl (EM)'/60, cited); new `emSuffixTierRoutesEveryUniverseMarketToTheSame60bps` covers one realistic symbol per EM suffix — universe members except 500325.BO, a realistic BSE code with no catalog entry today — (RELIANCE.NS, 500325.BO, 600519.SS, PETR4.SA, AMXB.MX, EMAAR.AE, QNBK.QA, COMI.CA, NPN.JO) all landing on 60bps/'intl (EM)'/takerFee 30; developed-suffix non-leak controls (7203.T, SHEL.L, 0700.HK, SAP.DE, RY.TO stay 30/'intl'); explicit Korea/Taiwan exclusion pins (005930.KS, 2330.TW stay 30/'intl' despite MSCI EM classification); `.SR` precedent untouched (2222.SR still 'intl (Tadawul)'/60); `^` prefix-ordering guard (^TASI.SR still 'index'/8, beats both the `.SR` and EM checks); US fallback untouched (AAPL, BRK-B still 13/'US large-cap'); direction assert (EM netRR < liquid-intl netRR < US netRR on an identical setup). Fixture sweep of `Salehman AITests/` for other EM-suffix symbols with cost-dependent expectations found none (StockSageTests.swift:239's RELIANCE.NS/NPN.JO/EMAAR.AE hits are membership/currency checks, not cost-dependent).
**Result:** Worktree gate `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` 0 failures (both the re-pin and the new test executing). Visual QA (live app, worktree build): RELIANCE.NS detail sheet Evidence line reads "After ~60bps est. intl (EM) costs + ~300bps/yr short financing: R:R 2.0:1 (gross) → 1.4:1 (net)" and the ledger row "Round-trip costs (~60bps intl (EM)) + ~300bps/yr short financing: −0.24R"; 7203.T (Tokyo, developed, control) still reads "~30bps est. intl costs" / "~30bps intl" — the EM branch does not leak. Both a sell-family sheet (RELIANCE.NS) and a buy-family sheet (HSBC, US large-cap fallback, unaffected) inspected. `research/INDEX.md`'s corpus-reconciliation caveat line gets a dated UPDATE recording the EM half shipped and the US half refused for lack of an honest per-symbol cap classifier (unblock: real market-cap metadata, or an ADV-based accessor wave); `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`'s `StockSageNetEdge.swift` entry updated with the new dispatch branch and UPDATE line.

**What & why:** Zero ranking/sizing/gate change; every new number computed from live data with formula+caveat in a `.help`. **L1** — new `StockSageCorrelationCluster.effectiveBets(symbols:returns:minBars:)` computes the Kish/design-effect n_eff = N ÷ (1 + (N−1)·ρ̄) over the SAME spark-return window the allocator's own correlation de-weighting reads; `MarketsView.deployEffectiveBets`/`.effectiveBetsCaption` map the Deploy-capital plan's positions into it and render one caption line ("Effective bets ≈ X.X of N — correlated positions count less") between the position rows and `plan.caveat`, appended to "Copy allocation plan" text iff shown; `.help` discloses the spark-basis bias as an OPTIMISTIC upper bound (RESEARCH_2026-07-03_weekly_concentration.md §3b). **L2** — `StockSageRefuseList.antiEdges` (3 entries: vol-managed momentum, betting-against-beta, MAX/lottery — evidence transcribed verbatim from RESEARCH_2026-07-03_candidate_edges.md lines 45-47) kept OUT of the test-pinned 7-entry `all` (these aren't 1-5 day setups); `policyNote` gains a second "Also refused at ANY horizon" clause, which every weekly-R `.help` already surfaces via `weeklyGrossHelp`. **L3** — `BacktestResult` gains defaulted `worstTradeR`/`stdevR` fields (populated in `summarize()`, `.empty` untouched); the detail-sheet's single "Wide ATR trail vs fixed 2:1" line is reframed as a two-channel block: a tail-channel headline (worst trade / worst drawdown / per-trade stdev, fixed→trail, realized net-of-cost) and a return-channel line folding in the SAME tie-aware verdict phrase logic, unchanged thresholds — per quant_engine_II.md checklist #3 (a stop is a regime bet, not a free win; the robust part is the truncation). Dropped per the design doc: L3 per-regime split (no regime series threaded through the sim) and an F14-grade date-aligned ρ̄ for L1 (would need a dataflow change, not display-only) — both explicitly labeled in their `.help`/caveat text.
**Result:** `tools/typecheck.sh` clean; worktree gate `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (18 new tests: `StockSageEffectiveBetsTests` ×8, `MarketsEffectiveBetsTests` ×5, `StockSageRefuseListTests` anti-edge ×3, `StockSageBacktesterTests` worstTrade/stdev ×2; existing 7-entry refuse-list pin + honesty-surface test unchanged and still green); `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` updated for all three touched modules; SOURCE_BUNDLE regenerated.

## 2026-07-12 · Feature — bias-corrected forward scoreboard panel (Markets → Portfolio)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePaperTrader.swift` (new `PaperForwardScoreboard` struct + pure `scoreboard(_:latestClose:)`), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (`updatePaperTrades` now builds `latestClose` from histories + calls `updateScoreboard`), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (`@ObservedObject paperStore` + `forwardScoreboardPanel` + `scoreCell` helper, referenced in `portfolioSection` above `tradeJournalPanel`), `Salehman AITests/StockSagePaperTraderTests.swift` (3 hand-derived tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** the existing `forwardStats` averages ONLY closed trades — and in a bracket-order system stop-outs (tight boundary) resolve before 2:1 targets, so the closed set is **enriched with fast losers**. That's why the engine's auto-paper track read −0.5R closed-only while the full-book mark-to-market read +0.07R (both ≈0; n≈314). The panel surfaces BOTH bounds honestly: `scoreboard()` marks still-open LONGs to their latest close (unrealized R) for a second bound, so the display shows closed-only (pessimistic, loser-enriched) alongside full-book (optimistic, open marks can reverse) + resolved-fraction, with the caveat that the truth sits between and both ≈0 = the engine's value is risk-discipline, not a proven edge. Also contrasts against the owner's OWN journal (`edgeStats.expectancyR`) or, when empty, a CTA to log real trades — "the one experiment no dataset can run for you." Display-only: NO change to score/conviction/stopPrice/targetPrice/suggestedWeight/sizing.
**Result:** typecheck EXIT 0; `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 3 new tests pass (`scoreboardBracketsRealizedAndFull`, `scoreboardNilUnderFourEvaluable`, `scoreboardSkipsShortsAndDegenerateAndMissingPrice`). Bundle regenerated.

## 2026-07-12 · Fixes — EXPORT-PARITY: 2 MISSED FX/pence surfaces in the pasted-into-broker plans (Gate-2 correction)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTradePlan.swift` (size line + leverage warning), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (deploy-plan copy-export line), `Salehman AITests/StockSageTradePlanTests.swift` (1 hand-derived test), MARKETS_TAB_MAP, SOURCE_BUNDLE, this log.
**What & why:** the /opus-operating protocol loaded mid-session; its Gate 2 ("the diff/grep is the fact, not your narration") caught that my earlier "FX/pence class verified closed" claim was OVER-BROAD — the closure grep was scoped to 3 View files and MISSED two EXPORT builders. The export-parity angle (the pasted-into-broker artifact — highest-stakes output) surfaced them: (1) `StockSageTradePlan.text` (the per-idea Copy-plan builder) hardcoded "$" on the native-currency `dollarsAtRisk` in its Size line + notional-exceeds warning — so a .SR/.L idea's pasted plan read "$99 at risk" (~3.75×/100× wrong) while the now-fixed on-screen sheet read "≈99 SAR"; (2) the deploy-plan copy-export (`MarketsView` ~4465) hardcoded "$" on `p.dollarsAtRisk`/`p.notional`, diverging from its own on-screen row (fixed earlier). Both now route through `StockSageCurrency.approxAmount(_:symbol:)` (currency-correct + pence ÷100). AFTER the fix a COMPLETE re-grep across ALL StockSage + Views money-string files is empty — the class is NOW genuinely closed (the earlier claim corrected on the record).
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — new hand-derived test (Gate 3): a .SR size line reads "20 shares · ≈100 SAR at risk" (entry 100/stop 95/1%/10k → 20sh×5 = 100 SAR, no minor-unit), USD keeps "≈$100"; no existing test pinned the old "$" format (checked). Honest note: the `pctOfAccount>100` leverage warning in `StockSageTradePlan.text` stays engine-pure (native-vs-account, over-warn-safe direction); the FX-correct pct lives at the MarketsView export call site. Autonomous-run item, /opus-operating-governed.

**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (R1 timeout→throttle flag; R2 saveSerialized callers), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageHistoryCache.swift` (R2 CacheWriter actor + nonShrinkMerge + saveSerialized), `Salehman AITests/StockSageHistoryCacheTests.swift` (2 R2 tests), MARKETS_TAB_MAP, SOURCE_BUNDLE, this log.
**What & why:** a 6th red-team, a NEW dimension — concurrency/state (races that show a wrong/stale number even when math+labels are perfect; distinct from the 4 display + 1 math sweeps). Workflow `wf_936c00e3-ec6`, each finding traced step-by-step + adversarially verified. 2 CONFIRMED MEDIUM races:
- **R1 (stale-publish) — watchdog timeout published as a COMPLETE success, poisoning the durable velocity trend.** A per-chunk 120s watchdog cancels only `chunkWork` (not the outer scan Task); on a slow-but-alive feed the fetch/build still return a PARTIAL-but-nonempty board (fetchHistories + the detached buildIdeas don't check cancellation), the outer `guard !Task.isCancelled` passes (false), the partial board publishes and the loop breaks — but `ChunkedScanResult` returns `wasCancelled:false, scanThrottled:false`. So the ~30-40% board is stamped `ideasUpdated=fresh` AND `MarketsView`'s durable per-day money-velocity record (guarded ONLY by `!lastScanCancelled && !scanThrottled`) fires over it, OVERWRITING a complete-scan snapshot for the same UTC day (permanent trend skew). Fix (1 line, root cause): on `chunkTimedOut` set `throttled=true; onThrottle?()` — a timeout IS the same "scan stopped early, partial board" state as a throttle trip, so the velocity guard skips it AND the partial-board banner surfaces it, zero new plumbing.
- **R2 (cache-race) — two detached HistoryCache savers land out of order and shrink a same-day-fresh cache.** The full-scan save (`:556`) and cancel best-effort save (`:510`) are both unstructured `Task.detached`; `.atomic` on write prevents a torn read but does NOT order two whole-file replacements. If a cancel-scan's smaller same-day save lands after a still-in-flight full-scan's larger save, the file shrinks — dropping symbols the next scan must re-fetch (the 429 risk the cache prevents); `cancelSaveDecision`'s non-shrink proof only covers the cache read at its own scan start, not a concurrently-queued larger write. Fix: funnel every write through a serial `CacheWriter` actor whose `write` does a read-merge-write (via the pure, tested `nonShrinkMerge`: a same-UTC-day disk cache not-subset-of the candidate is merged, not replaced) — actor isolation makes it indivisible + ordered, so two savers can neither reorder nor interleave. Both scan-path callers now `await …saveSerialized()`; no other HistoryCache writer exists (grep-verified).
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 new tests pin nonShrinkMerge (same-day smaller candidate can't shrink; new-day replaces + superset passes through + nil-disk). R1 is a 1-line semantic flag flip whose correctness follows from the already-tested `scanThrottled` consumption; its async timeout path isn't deterministically unit-testable (honest gap noted). This is the 3rd verification axis — the ideas card is now swept for correct MATH, honest LABELS, and race-free STATE. Autonomous-run item (owner away, full-auto).

## 2026-07-12 · Audit — ideas-card ENGINE-MATH red-team → CLEAN PASS (0 bugs; money engine mathematically sound end-to-end)
**Files:** none (report only; docs entry). Verification scripts in scratchpad (verify.py/verify2.py, session-local).
**What & why:** after 4 display-honesty sweeps (13 defects fixed, FX/pence class root-caused + grep-verified closed), a 5th red-team (Workflow `wf_acbed8ac-055`, 6 agents, each finding hand-verified) hunted a DISTINCT dimension — engine COMPUTATION correctness (wrong formula/sign, off-by-one lookback, boundary/empty NaN/Inf, unguarded division feeding EV/velocity/sizing/rank). **ZERO confirmed math bugs.** Six load-bearing formulas were independently re-implemented in Python and matched the shipped Swift to FULL DOUBLE PRECISION: netRR/netExpectancyR/breakEvenWinRate (StockSageNetEdge), expectedLogGrowth half-Kelly (StockSageExpectedValue), Wilson lower bound + PSR (StockSageConvictionCalibration/DeflatedSharpe), the rebalance closed-form churn cap (StockSageCapitalAllocator). Interaction traps checked safe: the 50:1 reward cap correctly protects the cost gate on hair-thin stops (uncapped netRR 42.9→breakeven 0.023 toothless; capped 21.2→0.045); the calibration band-lookup index is identical between fit() bucketing and winProb() retrieval (no off-by-one on a band edge); costAsPctOfReward divides by the normal-sized gross reward, not the tiny risk. Every math surface producing a card/sheet/velocity/backtest number was traced (Advisor scoring, ExpectedValue, NetEdge, Kelly, PositionSizer, CapitalAllocator, ConvictionCalibration incl. isotonic/Platt/Beta-IRLS/OOS-selector, DeflatedSharpe, Indicators, StrategyBacktest, Drawdown/RiskOfRuin).
**Result:** docs-only, no code change, build unaffected. RESULT STATEMENT: the ideas-card money engine is now verified sound BOTH ways — numbers correctly COMPUTED (this pass) and honestly LABELED (the 4 display sweeps). A clean pass after 5 waves signals the defect-finding search is at genuine diminishing returns; the engine's honesty+correctness floor is thoroughly established. Autonomous-run item (owner away, full-auto).

## 2026-07-12 · Fixes — ideas-card red-team wave 2 (6 defects: FX/pence root-cause + US-gated timing + turnover count + partial-scan CTA)
**Files:** `StockSageCurrency.swift` (approxAmount now pence-normalizes), `StockSageExecutionTiming.swift` (sessionNote US-gates the measured curve), `StockSageExpectedValue.swift` (weeklyTurnoverNote real lane count), `StockSageTodayPlan.swift` (2 at-risk sites), `Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift` (2 sites + timing symbol), `Views/MarketsView.swift` (pctOfAccountUSD helper + 3 leverage sites + best-opp CTA partial-scan caption), `StockSageStore.swift` (timing symbol), 4 test files, MARKETS_TAB_MAP, SOURCE_BUNDLE, this log.
**What & why:** a 2nd ideas-card red-team wave (Workflow `wf_1d9e3af7-0ba`, every finding verified) found 6 CONFIRMED defects (2 HIGH), all display-only. Four were the same FX/pence bug class the earlier fix didn't reach on these surfaces:
- **#3 (root cause) — `approxAmount` never applied the pence ÷100.** Every caller passes a RAW native amount (dollarsAtRisk/notional in pence for .L), so ".L" at-risk rendered ~100× over ("≈400 GBP" for £4). Fixed IN `approxAmount` via majorUnitValue → fixes ALL callers (incl. the idea-card "At risk" the prior wave only label-fixed). The pre-existing test `approxAmount(400,"BP.L")=="≈400 GBP"` ENCODED the bug — re-derived to "≈4 GBP" per spec (never edited toward the impl).
- **#1 (HIGH) — Today-plan/ranked-actions "$ at risk" hardcoded $ on native dollarsAtRisk** (2222.SR seeds into the first-launch plan). Fixed 4 sites (card visible+a11y, TodayPlan single-idea+export) via approxAmount.
- **#2 (HIGH) — best-opp/CTA "% acct" + "exceeds account" compared native notional to USD account** → false leverage warning on every non-USD winner (JPY 100×, SAR 3.75×). New `pctOfAccountUSD` helper (majorUnitValue × FX rate ÷ account) keys the 3 comparison sites; ps unchanged.
- **#6 — sessionNote applied a US-measured (64-name) intraday-cost curve to Tadawul/.L/.T equities under "Measured on this universe".** Threaded symbol; the market-agnostic overnight-premia rationale always shows, the US-ET microstructure sentence gates on currencyForSymbol=="USD".
- **#5 (LOW) — weekly-turnover tooltip hardcoded "across the top 3"** while the subtitle could read "top 1". Now counts the real lane members with a hold.
- **#4 (LOW) — best-opp global CTA crowned a first-scan provisional pick without the "best-so-far, order may change" caption** the sibling card carries. Added (visible + a11y) via the existing firstScanProgressCaption.
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 3 tests added/re-derived (pence contract, US-gated timing) + 1 format-updated (USD "≈$100" tightened, value unchanged). Bundle regenerated. Ideas-card FX/pence bug class now root-caused in approxAmount + pctOfAccountUSD; 13 total ideas-card defects fixed this session.

## 2026-07-12 · Feature — "Why this rank" honest EV-rank decomposition (ideas-card LANE 2)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift` (RankExplanation + rankExplanation), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (whyThisRankSection in the detail sheet), `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift` (2 tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** the owner scoped this session to the ideas card ("all 4"); this is LANE 2 (a new feature). The ideas card's whole ethos is the honesty floor, so the most on-brand feature is making the RANKING itself legible: when the engine puts idea A above B, the user can now see WHY. `StockSageExpectedValue.rankExplanation` decomposes an idea's EV rank key into the exact terms `rankByEV` sums — base regime-adjusted EV + seasonality tilt − earnings-soon penalty − thin-liquidity penalty — by calling the SAME term functions the ranker uses (never a parallel re-derivation), so the breakdown provably equals the real sort key. The detail-sheet `whyThisRankSection` (EV-sort only, since it decomposes the EV key) shows the active adjustments that actually moved the idea (boost green / demote amber), labeled as RANKING WEIGHTS not returns, with the standard "orders payoff, doesn't predict it" caveat. Display-only: it reads the rank, never changes it; shows nothing when only the base EV drove the order or for a nil-EV idea (no fabricated story).
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 new tests pin the honesty-critical invariant (total == base + seasonality − earnings − liquidity, each term matching its own function) + nil-EV → nil. Bundle regenerated. Lane 2 complete.

## 2026-07-12 · Fixes — ideas-card red-team batch 2 (gap-risk FX, laneCorrelation date-align, dead sort hidden)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift` (dated `laneCorrelation` overload), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (call the dated overload), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (gap-risk/leverage USD-convert at 2 sites; hide inert momentumWeighted sort), `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift` (2 tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** the rest of Lane 1's ideas-card red-team findings, all display-only (no score/EV/sizing/ranking change):
- **F4 (MEDIUM, FX) — gap-risk/leverage compared native-currency amounts to a USD account.** `ps.notional`/`dollarsAtRisk`/`entry` are in the symbol's own currency; a pence stock inflated ~100× → a FALSE leverage/liquidation warning on an unleveraged cash position, and the gap verdict could falsely read "MORE than you have / owe the broker". Fix: a per-symbol `usdFactor` (pence-normalized × FX rate) converts notional/at-risk to USD for the `%acct`/leverage compares, and the gap-risk account is passed in the symbol's native currency so its loss-vs-equity ratio is currency-consistent. The "At risk"/"Notional" chips now label their own currency (approxAmount). Same fix on the export/copy-plan gap site. Untracked FX → factor 1 (prior behavior).
- **laneCorrelation (MEDIUM, correctness) — crypto-vs-equity fast-lane correlation paired returns by TAIL ARRAY INDEX, not calendar date.** A crypto 7-day week vs equity 5-day week correlated mismatched days (every weekend bar shifts the pairing) → a meaningless number shown with a confident hedge verdict. New dated `laneCorrelation` overload aligns each crypto×equity pair to its common calendar days (`alignByDate`) before correlating; the store now passes dated returns. Same zero-variance exclusion; nil without ≥2 shared days.
- **Momentum-weighted sort (MEDIUM, mislabel) — the picker offered it and the board claimed it was applied, but it was INERT** (the momentum multiplier no-ops with the default empty `closes`, producing a list byte-identical to velocity). Hidden from the picker (`IdeaSort.pickerCases`) — the enum case stays for @AppStorage decode-compat (a persisted value still works, falling through to the velocity order it always produced). Re-add only once per-symbol closes are threaded into the rank (that would be an owner-gated ranking change).
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 new tests prove date-alignment recovers the true correlation + nil on no overlap. Bundle regenerated. Lane 1 (ideas-card harden) is now COMPLETE: 7/7 red-team findings addressed across batches 1–2.

**What & why:** the owner scoped this session to the ideas card + said "all 4" (harden / feature / research / polish). **Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 new tests pin the currency-threading + defined-mask contracts. Bundle regenerated. Remaining Lane-1 findings (F4 gap-risk native-currency vs USD account; laneCorrelation index-vs-date crypto/equity misalignment; the inert "Momentum-weighted" sort — a ranking-option question) go in a follow-up.

## 2026-07-12 · Fixes — 4 honesty-floor defects (red-team pass 3: analytics FX, price-alert oracle, what-if 1:1, crypto export)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (analytics weight pence-normalize + multi-currency flag), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (price-alert on-board guard, what-if + export FX conversion, crypto export band/optimism, analytics caveat), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, this log.
**What & why:** third red-team pass (Workflow `wf_00daa8c9-cf1`, every finding verified against source) — 4 CONFIRMED MEDIUM defects, all display-only. Two recurring themes: FX/pence conversion skipped on newer surfaces that copied an all-USD sibling, and a view reading the wrong oracle for a state.
- **③ portfolio risk analytics weights** (StockSageStore ~1399): Sharpe/Vol/VaR/MaxDD/β blended holdings by RAW local-currency price×shares — a `.L` (pence) holding was ~100× over-weighted, SAR treated as USD. Fixed the ~100× pence error unconditionally via `majorUnitValue`; cross-currency FX isn't fetched on this path, so added an `analyticsWeightsApproximate` flag (true when analyzed symbols span >1 quote currency) that surfaces a "multi-currency book — weights approximate" caveat in the panel (disclose, don't imply exactness).
- **② price-alert false "no quote"** (MarketsView ~870): `noQuote` used `currentPrice` (board-only), but `StockSageMonitor.checkPriceAlerts` fetches EVERY armed alert's symbol independently — so an off-board ticker's alert DOES fire, yet the row said "cannot fire, check spelling" → a user would delete a working alert. Now only warns when the symbol IS on the board yet unpriced.
- **what-if concentration 1:1 sum** (MarketsView ~6628 on-screen + ~5892 export): summed a multi-currency book at 1:1, so the "% of book" and >60% CONCENTRATED warning pointed at the wrong currency's holdings. Fixed both sites to FX-convert (mirror the allocation panel, exclude untracked-FX).
- **④ Copy-plan crypto export** (MarketsView ~5831): pasted net R:R at the flat 70bps floor with no band/optimism note — the export read MORE authoritative than the sheet (which shows the band + "your true net is lower"). Now mirrors the sheet via `costsDisplayLabel` + `costsOptimismSentence` (the pass-1 #1 helpers). No score/EV/sizing change on any of the four.
**Result:** typecheck EXIT 0; full test gate green (see result line). Autonomous-run item. Bundle regenerated. (Red-team tally: 3 passes → 10 real defects found + fixed.)

## 2026-07-12 · Fix — FX-pair rate² double-conversion (root cause, 4 call sites; red-team pass 2)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCurrency.swift` (new canonical `conversionCurrencyForSymbol`), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (4 valuation sites re-keyed + View method now delegates), `Salehman AITests/StockSageCurrencyTests.swift` (2 contract tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** a second red-team pass (Workflow `wf_ef56d1ef-b76`, every finding verified against source) found the `rate²` FX double-conversion bug — fixed in `portfolioTotals` on an earlier pass — SURVIVING in sibling call sites that were never updated. A `…USD=X` pair's price is already in USD (quote leg), but these sites keyed the USD conversion off `StockSageCurrency.currencyForSymbol` (the EXPOSURE leg → "EUR" for EURUSD=X), re-multiplying by the pair rate (~8% for EUR, up to ~27% for GBP-class). Confirmed + fixed:
- **HIGH — currency-exposure panel** (MarketsView ~1515): showed a second, contradictory USD total for the same book on the same screen as the headline; the FX-concentration risk warning magnitude was computed off the inflated baseline.
- **MEDIUM — allocation panel** (~1484): skewed every asset-class/region/sector slice %, understating equity.
- **LOW — rebalance trade plan** (~1418): printed a "$X of EURUSD=X" trade size ~8–27% wrong when an FX pair is a risk-parity target.
- **4TH SITE (audit missed it; found by grepping EVERY caller) — open-trade portfolio-heat** (~2049): the Kelly-sizer's open-risk heat gate rate²-inflated `…USD=X` journal trades, skewing the risk gate that feeds sizing. Same root cause.
Root-cause fix (ponytail): extracted the quote-leg logic into ONE canonical, unit-tested `StockSageCurrency.conversionCurrencyForSymbol` and pointed all 5 valuation call sites (incl. the pre-existing `portfolioTotals`) at it via the View's now-delegating method — so no caller can drift again. Display/plan-math only; no score/conviction/EV/stop/target/weight change.
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 new tests (`conversionCurrencyKeysOnQuoteLegNotExposureLeg`, `usdQuotedFXPairValuesAtRateOneNotRateSquared`) pin the quote-leg contract + the rate²-regression signature. Autonomous-run item. Bundle regenerated.

## 2026-07-12 · Fixes — 3 honesty-floor label/disclosure defects (adversarial red-team of shipped surfaces)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageNetEdge.swift` (new `costsDisplayNote` + `costsOptimismSentence` helpers), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (crypto optimism note wired into the R:R line + ledger; scoreboard head-to-head field swap), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift` (Today-card DSR selection-bias disclosure), `Salehman AITests/StockSageCryptoHonestyTests.swift` (contract test), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** an adversarial red-team (Workflow `wf_7e2736e3-d63`, 5 dimensions, every finding verified by re-reading source) surfaced 3 CONFIRMED honesty-floor mismatches — a number/verdict shown under a claim it didn't satisfy. For an engine whose measured edge is DSR≈0 and whose VALUE is its honesty floor, a mislabel is a first-class bug. All three fixes are display-only (label/field/disclosure), no scoring/sizing change:
- **#1 (MEDIUM, dangerous direction) — crypto band-vs-priced net.** The detail sheet shows the tier BAND (`costsDisplayLabel`, e.g. thin alt "~160–440bps") but computes net R:R + verdict at the flat `defaultCosts` 70bps (deliberately — the displayed net must equal the RANKING net per the 2026-07-02 rank-consistency contract; re-pointing `defaultCosts` is a separate owner-reviewed change). So a thin-alt loser rendered "net 2.4:1 — acceptable" while only 70bps was deducted, not the 160–440 the header claimed. Fix keeps the net at the ranking cost (no contract break) and adds `costsDisplayNote`/`costsOptimismSentence` — fires ONLY when the band low > priced flat cost (thin tier: 160>70) to say the net is OPTIMISTIC vs the band; empty on mid(70==70)/large/BTC-ETH(21<70, conservative)/non-crypto.
- **#2 (MEDIUM) — Today-card DSR.** `MarketsTodayActionsCard` showed the closed-only forward DSR with only the correlated-trials tooltip — no resolved-fraction, no selection-bias note (stops resolve before targets → early closes over-represent losers; the exact bias the Portfolio scoreboard fixes). The card predates the 08fe122 scoreboard. Added the resolved-fraction + "closed-only OVER-STATES the loss, see Portfolio → Forward scoreboard" disclosure when open trades exist.
- **#3 (LOW) — scoreboard head-to-head (my own 08fe122 bug).** The owner-vs-engine verdict compared owner-REALIZED-net (`edgeStats.expectancyR`) against the engine's OPTIMISTIC unrealized+gross `fullAvgR` — biased against the owner. Swapped to `realizedAvgR` (like-with-like: both realized).
**Result:** `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (new `cryptoOptimismNoteFiresOnlyWhenBandLowExceedsPricedCost` passes; contract pinned). Autonomous-run item (owner "work autonomously 24h", 2026-07-12). Bundle regenerated.

## 2026-07-13 · Research — non-signal money mechanics surveyed (the "fastest money" that isn't alpha): 2 real levers, 3 myths
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-13_nonsignal_money_mechanics.md` (NEW), `research/INDEX.md` (dated line), this log.
**What & why:** owner "continue" (away, full-auto, ultracode). The corpus exhausted SIGNAL/alpha but never surveyed the NON-SIGNAL money mechanics (0 corpus hits on tax/harvest/wash-sale/behavioral-gap). Workflow `wf_a45dccaf-7b5`, 17 agents, 3-vote, primary sources. Result: 2 REAL retail-accessible levers, 3 MYTHS — NONE is alpha (all cost/tax floor-raises on beta, consistent with the whole campaign). REAL: fund/advisory-fee minimization ~50-100 bps/yr (Sharpe's-arithmetic IDENTITY); tax-loss harvesting ~40-90 bps/yr high-bracket-taxable-only, front-loaded/decaying/DEFERRAL (Chaudhuri-Burnham-Lo FAJ 2020, recent-window ~81bps not the marketed 1.5%); broker-choice execution up to ~39bps round-trip one-time (Schwarz et al. JF 2025). MYTHS (the valuable debunks, now sourced): behavioral "return gap" ~3-10bps (Fulkerson-Jordan-Riley-Yan FAJ 2026 corrected Morningstar 1.7%→0.03%, DALBAR discredited); rebalancing bonus ~0 excess for a correlated equity book (Willenbrock 2011); sequence-of-returns-as-return-lever = identity ZERO (Π(1+r) commutative). PRACTICAL ANSWER: the honest fastest-money is the boring one quantified — minimize fees+execution+tax + stop overtrading ≈ ~1.0-1.9%/yr + ~39bps one-time, a floor-raise not an edge. IDEAS-CARD candidates (flag-only, owner-gated, ALL account-data-blocked — no tax-status/cost-basis/broker field in the app): TLH flag on a losing held taxable position; overtrading warning (redundant with shipped); fund-timing warning = DO NOT BUILD.
**Result:** docs-only (no app code, no build impact). Survey not ablation — nothing promoted/wired; feature candidates account-data-blocked. Milestone (ALPHA net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET and unchanged — this space never contained alpha, only friction reduction. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.

Rather than one at a time, ONE exhaustive workflow (`wf_1e448d76-ad6`, 13 agents, 3-vote, primary sources) surveyed all 5 vs the three-part bar. **ALL 5 REFUSE, 0 survivors → the accessible non-price data-class space is EXHAUSTED.** (1) ALT/WEB DATA — institutional-only ($100K–$2M/yr; retail Quiver carries none); (2) SUPPLY-CHAIN GRAPH — Cohen-Frazzini 1.58%/mo in-sample decayed to 0.6%/mo AND lost significance OOS (Pinchuk 2018/2023) before cost; (3) PATENT/R&D — the ONE survivorship-PASS class (USPTO permanent, covers delisted filers) but dies on the other two walls: tradable IE signal needs the institutional Compustat R&D-capital denominator, and the ~1.4%/yr net-of-58%-haircut residual = GP/A's already-measured-NULL level (quality's innovation cousin); (4) HIRING/LABOR — Revelio $85K/yr + the tradable Glassdoor-rating-change strand is high-IVOL/low-inst-ownership limits-to-arb, net-negative at retail; (5) ESG-CHANGE — the whole signal is a LOOK-AHEAD ARTIFACT (Berg-Fabisik-Sautner "Rewriting History": vendor scores retroactively rewritten; original point-in-time scores show NO return association). Surveys not ablations — nothing promoted.
**Result:** docs-only (no app code, no build impact). The corpus's unsurveyed-class enumeration is now COMPLETE; every route to a measured edge is now ONLY new data-ACCESS (owner institutional-feed purchase) or the owner's own fills. Milestone (net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET and unchanged. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.

## 2026-07-13 · Research — options order-flow / GEX-DEX surveyed → REFUSED (structural; last "options" unsurveyed class closes)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-13_options_orderflow_survey.md` (NEW), `research/INDEX.md` (dated line + milestone-row UPDATE), this log.
**What & why:** owner "continue 24 hour work" (away, full-auto). With the ideas card verified-complete and defect-finding exhausted, the one genuinely-open non-pollution thread was to SURVEY (not ablate) options order-flow/GEX-DEX — the class the 2026-07-12 completeness survey EXPLICITLY listed as unsurveyed. Workflow `wf_c1704d3c-187`, 10 agents, 3-vote + a LIVE data-access probe. Verdict: **REFUSE-and-index (structural).** A genuinely distinct MECHANISM from the already-refused IV-surface class (dealer gamma/delta positioning; gross ~10%/yr cross-sectional net-gamma, Soebhag 2023) that dies on the SAME two structural walls: (a) SURVIVORSHIP — retail per-name options data is current-universe only; a delisted name (SIVB, failed Mar-2023) returns "Options data not available" on the live GEX tool → zero retail delisted GEX/DEX history, not reconstructable, the IV-surface twin; (b) RETAIL-COST — options expression eaten by the ~60-100× retail single-stock option spread (12.6% Bogousslavsky-Muravyev 2025 / 17.2% Muravyev-Pearson 2020), equity expression ~10%/yr → ~4.2% after the 58% haircut → ~0 net. GEX/DEX's own return content is last-30-min intraday momentum that fully reverts + dies past 4pm (Baltussen et al. JFE 2021). Distinct class, identical structural grave. Both options classes now surveyed + refused → the options class is CLOSED. 2 claims 3-vote verified (both REVISED-but-held: spread numbers corrected, disposition unchanged); 1 agent dropped on an API error, no load-bearing claim rests on it.
**Result:** docs-only (no app code, no build impact). Survey not ablation — nothing promoted/wired. Milestone (net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET and unchanged. Autonomous-run item.

## 2026-07-13 · Ideas card — fix the LAST FX-basis honesty defect: "% of acct" rendered native-currency over a USD account (completeness-critic gap 2)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePositionSizer.swift` (summaryLine `pctOverride:` param), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (3 call sites threaded), `Salehman AITests/StockSagePositionSizerTests.swift` (1 hand-derived test).
**What & why:** an adversarial completeness-critic workflow (Opus, `wf_91d5cdeb-a9c`, 5 lenses each falsifying a load-bearing "terminal-state" claim) found that the 2026-07-12 wave-2 FX fix closed only HALF the currency-basis bug on the "Size it now" line. It corrected the at-risk AMOUNT (`approxAmount(symbol:)`) but left the neighbouring **"% of acct"** figure rendering `ps.pctOfAccount` = native-notional ÷ USD-account — ~3.75× over for a `.SR` (SAR) name, ~100× for a `.L`/`.JO` pence name — as VISIBLE + SPOKEN idea-card text (idea card, best-opportunity CTA a11y string, settle-lens), while the FX-corrected sibling `pctOfAccountUSD` was wired only into the leverage-warning FLAGS. So a 2222.SR idea showed the at-risk dollars correctly in SAR but the % beside it ~3.75× wrong: two figures, one line, inconsistent currency bases — a live honesty-floor label defect (a % that implies a wrong risk fraction). Root-cause fix at the shared function: `summaryLine` gains an optional `pctOverride: Double? = nil` (nil → `ps.pctOfAccount`, byte-identical for USD/untracked-FX callers and the test-lock); the 3 MarketsView call sites pass the already-computed `pctOfAccountUSD(ps, symbol:, account:)` (two sites already computed it for the tint/flag — zero new FX call there). No ranking/sizing/gate change; `ps` and the flag logic untouched; display-only.
**Verification (opus-operating gates):** `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` (pasted); full PositionSizer suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; the new `summaryLinePctOfAcctHonorsFXCorrectedOverride()` FIRED (by name, not skipped) — fixture HAND-DERIVED (account 10000·1%·entry 100·stop 90 → 10 shares, notional 1000, ps.pctOfAccount = 10%; a SAR name's USD-correct pct ≈ 1000÷3.75÷10000·100 ≈ 2.67% → "3% of acct"; 10% vs 3% straddle unambiguously at %.0f), never read off the code under test. **Falsifiability probe:** reverted the override in summaryLine (`pctOverride ?? ps.pctOfAccount` → `ps.pctOfAccount`) → the new test failed **by name** (`** TEST FAILED **`) → restored → re-gated green (gate 6). Caught + avoided a WHIPPYX-style vacuous pass: a deep single-method `-only-testing` path ran ZERO test cases yet printed `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; re-ran at the CLASS level (which selects correctly) for the real red/green. No owner gate touched (honesty-preserving display fix; the owner-gate class was retired 2026-07-09 regardless).
**Result:** the FX/pence honesty-floor bug class is now closed on the "% of acct" surface too — the last leg the wave-2 fix missed. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.
