## 2026-07-13 · Research — VALUE factor (B/M + E/P) ablation: the completeness-critic's largest gap, MEASURED null (was assumed)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-13_value_factor.md` (NEW), `research/INDEX.md` (detail line + milestone-row correction), `tools/eodhd_panel/PREREG_2026-07-13_value_factor.md` (prereg + 6 design-review fixes), `tools/eodhd_panel/value_factor.py` (runner), `tools/eodhd_panel/edgar_pull_value.py` (data pull), `research/trials_ledger.jsonl` (+12 arms family `value-factor`), this log.
**What & why:** the 2026-07-13 completeness-critic workflow (Opus, `wf_91d5cdeb-a9c`) FALSIFIED the corpus's "accessible space exhausted" claim — the classic VALUE / valuation-ratio FF factor (book-to-market, earnings-yield) had ZERO adjudication (grep-verified); it was silently ASSUMED priced-in as the "incremental-to price/size/value/momentum" baseline, never measured on the frozen panel. Distinct from the GP/A profitability null (loads OPPOSITE in FF5, HML vs RMW; its LONG leg — cheap stocks — is retail-occupiable, dodging the short-leg wall). Ran the FIRST price/fundamentals-ratio ablation: B/M = StockholdersEquity/MarketCap, E/P = NetIncomeLoss/MarketCap, top-tercile-LONG, signal-swap on the twice-verified `gpa_quality.py` machinery, 12 arms (2 signals × 2 scopes × 3 H) on the frozen 34,701-series survivorship-free panel (5,135 clean names, 2010-2026), SEC EDGAR companyfacts (3,807 value-fact extracts), net 13/60bps, trials=12, varTrialSharpe floored 0.0343 BINDING. **RESULT: VALID NULL — 0/12 arms; best B/M DSR 0.053; E/P diffs NEGATIVE (2010s value-trap/drought signature) but symmetric-negative pre-commitment correctly did NOT fire.** Value leg of the FF factor space now MEASURED null, not assumed.
**Rigor (the prereg discipline working — 2 defects caught PRE-decision-statistic):** (1) 3-lens Opus DESIGN REVIEW pre-lock (`wf_00ad60b4-3ab`) → COMMIT-WITH-FIXES, 6 fixes A-F, 2 BLOCKERS one root cause — the inherited machinery stores split-ADJUSTED close but EDGAR shares are RAW, so naive `adjClose×rawShares` mis-scales market cap 28× for AAPL 2013 (biases toward null AND a fake pass); FIX A = split-consistent `MarketCap = adjClose(p)×sharesAdj` with a pass-blocking AAPL-2013 selfcheck. (2) FIX-B dei-window bug — dei cover-page shares are dated after the FY-end, so exact-end matching fell through to the corrupt us-gaap MAX (AAPL 2013's 6.29B restated comparative, ~7× the true 899M); caught by the MANDATED AAPL ground-truth reproduction; fixed to dei-within-[end,end+45d] + drop-if->2×-disagreement. (3) placebo price-leak — the first placebo shuffled only the (num,shares) scalar and left each name's REAL price in the denominator, so low-price names still clustered in the "random" top tercile → placebo maxed DSR 1.000 → first run INVALID; CORRECTED to permute the FULL ratio (S1″) + a returns-shuffle null (S1‴), both then <0.58 (VALID). The invalid run's 12 ledger arms were REMOVED before the valid re-run appended its own. Neither correction touches the decision rule or base arms.
**Verification (2-lens):** Lens 1 (independent AAPL B/M repro, imports nothing from the runner) — bit-exact 0.2483, split-consistency identity `adjClose×sharesAdj = $497.7B ≡ rawClose×rawShares = $497.7B`, AAPL a LOW-B/M growth name (not cheap tercile). Lens 2 (Opus code-audit vs prereg) — all 6 conjuncts CONFIRMED bit-exact, "REAL, TRUSTWORTHY NULL — no defect": the placebo correction touches ONLY `run_valid` (a gate that can only block a pass), so it moved the run from "null-by-invalidity" to "null-by-merit" and categorically cannot have manufactured the null; DSR port responsive (a Sharpe-0.5 edge would clear at 0.957); best arm 0.053 decisive not borderline.
**Result:** research + tooling (a research runner, not app code — no build/bundle impact). The completeness-critic's largest gap is closed; the exhaustion claim is now MORE honest (value MEASURED null vs assumed). CAVEAT: 2010-2026 is value's documented DROUGHT decade — a null here does NOT refute the academic premium. Nothing wired; milestone (net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.

## 2026-07-15 · Research — investment/issuance FF legs (asset-growth CMA + net-issuance) ablation: rank-2 gap, MEASURED null; a SMOKE→full collapse the DSR discipline caught
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-15_investment_issuance.md` (NEW), `research/INDEX.md` (detail line + milestone-row UPDATE), `tools/eodhd_panel/PREREG_2026-07-15_investment_issuance.md` (prereg + 2 design-review fixes), `tools/eodhd_panel/investment_issuance.py` (runner), `tools/eodhd_panel/invest_results.json` (results), `research/trials_ledger.jsonl` (+12 arms family `investment-issuance`), this log.
**What & why:** the 2026-07-13 completeness-critic's rank-2 gap — the investment factor (FF5 CMA / Cooper-Gulen-Schill asset growth) + net-share-issuance factor (Pontiff-Woodgate) had ZERO corpus adjudication (grep-verified), never surveyed OR ablated. Data ALREADY on disk (Assets from the GP/A pull, shares from the value pull) — no new EDGAR fetch. Ran the THIRD fundamentals-side ablation: AG = Assets(t)/Assets(t-1)−1, ISS = sharesAdj(t)/sharesAdj(t-1)−1, LONG=low (stored as negated Δ so top-tercile-by-signal = low-growth/low-issuance long — the CMA/low-issuance long leg, OPPOSITE direction to value/quality's top-tercile-long). Δ-signal swap on the twice-verified value machinery, 12 arms, 4,629 clean names (usable AG 3,590 / ISS 2,984; delisted 1,232 / active 2,560 — survivorship-free), net 13/60bps, trials=12. **RESULT: VALID NULL — 0/12; AG diffs POSITIVE but tiny (best DSR 0.287, |t|≤1.59); ISS NEGATIVE, symmetric-negative correctly did NOT fire.** The LONG leg of the investment/issuance FF factors is now MEASURED null, not assumed.
**The headline — a SMOKE→full collapse the discipline caught:** a 600-name SMOKE showed AG at t=2.24/DSR 0.696 — the most-alive fundamental signal the campaign had produced, and it survived the FIX-1 interval guard. On the FULL ~3,590-name universe it COLLAPSED to t=1.51/DSR 0.051 — small-universe idiosyncratic luck averaging out (diff dropped ~5×) AND the DSR bench rising with arm-Sharpe dispersion (varTrialSharpe 0.023→0.052). A naive read of the SMOKE would have claimed "asset growth works"; the full-universe, trials-deflated, DSR-gated pre-registration correctly says NULL. The code audit independently confirmed the collapse is legitimate averaging-out (the full AG cohort still selects low-growth names; the gross book is pure beta; the paired-diff Sharpe 0.19 < the multiple-testing bench 0.38), NOT a suppression bug.
**Rigor (design review + 2 fixes pre-decision-statistic):** focused Opus design review (`a2796f0610`) → COMMIT-WITH-FIXES: FIX 1 pair-interval guard ([340,380]d — drops skipped-year 2yr-growth + fiscal-year-change 6mo-stub pairs that would dilute toward null); FIX 2 price-exogenous path (the signal is Assets/shares-only, fed through a dedicated `_invest_run_leg` reading the scalar directly, NOT the value `run_leg` which divides by price — so the value run's 1/price placebo-leak structurally cannot arise). Load-bearing correctness (selfcheck-locked): the ISS split-neutrality — a 7:1 split reads ISS=0 (not a fake +600%) because both years' sharesAdj cancel the split. 2-lens verify: Lens 1 independent AAPL AG+ISS repro bit-exact (AG 2024→2025 −1.57% stored +0.0157; ISS −2.25% buyback); Lens 2 Opus code-audit CONFIRMED all 5 items, "REAL, TRUSTWORTHY NULL", recomputed all 12 arms (0 disagreements).
**Result:** research + tooling (research runner, not app code — no build/bundle impact). The completeness-critic's rank-2 gap is closed; with value AND investment/issuance now measured, the accessible FF factor legs (value, quality, investment/issuance) are all MEASURED nulls on this population. CAVEAT: LONG-leg only (the tradable alpha is short-concentrated) + 2010-2026 regime — NOT a refutation of the academic factors. Nothing wired; milestone (net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.

## 2026-07-15 · Research — ACCRUALS (Sloan 1996) long leg: the last argued-refuse MEASURED; the canonical fundamental axis is COMPLETE
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-15_accruals.md` (NEW), `research/INDEX.md` (detail line + milestone-row UPDATE), `tools/eodhd_panel/PREREG_2026-07-15_accruals.md` + `accruals.py` + `edgar_pull_accruals.py` (committed pre-statistic at fd5f3c3), `tools/eodhd_panel/accruals_results.json`, `research/trials_ledger.jsonl` (+6 arms family `accruals`), this log.
**What & why:** the 2026-07-09 non-price survey REFUSED accruals "as a decision, not an oversight" (short-leg-concentrated + long-dead post-2003). The investment/issuance precedent (also refused-by-analogy, then measured null) justified converting the argument into a measurement — the LAST canonical fundamental anomaly with no measured null. Sloan balance-sheet accruals = [(ΔCA−ΔCash)−(ΔCL−ΔSTD−ΔTP)−Dep]/avg(Assets), consecutive FYs, LONG = LOW accruals (high earnings quality), price-exogenous on the twice-verified invest machinery; fresh EDGAR working-capital pull (3,807 files, 0 errors); 2,820 usable names (937 delisted / 1,883 active); 6 arms, trials=6, floor 0.0343 BINDING. **RESULT: VALID NULL — 0/6; the campaign's MOST NUANCED null.** ALL 6 diffs POSITIVE (t 0.99–1.43, Sloan-consistent) and raw DSR reached 0.921/0.927 on H252 — the closest-ever approach — but the rule refuses on THREE independent conjuncts: the variance floor binds (six correlated arms understate selection risk); winsorization collapses the diff to ≤0.203 (TAIL-DRIVEN — skew 0.84–3.33); S1′ lag sign-agreement fails on both H252 arms with dramatic decay everywhere (FULL/H126 +0.0079→+0.0006 at +6 months — QUICKLY-PRICED). A thin, tail-concentrated, fast-decaying echo of the pre-decay premium — points the right way, nowhere near tradable.
**Near-bar placebo, honestly handled:** returns-shuffle max 0.941 (within the pre-committed ≤0.95 gate). Lens 2 reproduced it bit-exact and corrected the initial guess: seed3 LOWLIQ/H63, n=64 WELL-POWERED — a permutation lottery (same arm: 0.149/0.020/0.941 across seeds); the reframe: pure noise reaching 0.94 puts the real arms' raw 0.92 INSIDE the noise envelope — CORROBORATING the null. A 4th seed would be outside the prereg and was correctly not run.
**Rigor:** design review pre-lock → COMMIT no defects (Sloan formula verified numerically twice + against real-panel economics: 11,395 events median −0.033, exactly Sloan's small-negative; a dep-in-candidate-ends bug caught by the hand-derived selfcheck pre-review and fixed). 2-lens verify: Lens 1 independent AAPL repro bit-exact (accruals −0.066568); Lens 2 all conjuncts recomputed bit-exact, placebo max bit-identical, S2a==S2b equality explained (0 screened names carry the signal), census reconciled (2,820+964=3,784), ledger honest.
**Result:** research + tooling (no app code, no build impact). **The canonical fundamental-anomaly axis is COMPLETE: value, quality (GP/A), investment/issuance, accruals — ALL measured nulls on the survivorship-free panel; no canonical fundamental anomaly remains argued-but-unmeasured.** CAVEATS: LONG-leg only; 2010-2026 entirely post-decay — not a refutation of the pre-decay academic anomaly. Nothing wired; milestone (net-of-cost DSR>0.95) UNMET. Autonomous-run item, ultracode.

## 2026-07-16 · OWNER DIRECTIVE — universe restricted to Tadawul + NASDAQ ONLY (2,420 → 901 names)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageQuoteService.swift` (the `nasdaqListed` 872-symbol baked set + `isAllowedUniverseSymbol` + the `build(_:)` choke-point filter + `marketCount` recount), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (`infraFXSymbols`/`infraFX` + `refreshInfraFX()` + 2 call sites), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (`fxRatesToUSD` infra fallback + 3 "worldwide"→"Tadawul + NASDAQ" copy fixes), `Salehman AITests/StockSageTests.swift` (5 universe tests re-ratified to the new spec), `Salehman AITests/StockSageUniverseTests.swift` (crypto-discoverable pin inverted), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** owner directive (2026-07-15/16, ALL-CAPS "ONLY KEEP TADAWUL AND NASDAQ"; the NASDAQ-literal-vs-US-market ambiguity was put back to the owner via a scope question and answered verbatim **"I WANT ONLY NASDAQ AND TADAWUL ONLY"** — the literal reading). The app carries NO per-symbol exchange metadata (US groups are sector-labeled), so NASDAQ membership was classified externally: **one AlphaVantage `LISTING_STATUS` call** (14,189 active listings with an exchange column; sanity anchors AAPL/MSFT/QQQ→NASDAQ, JPM/SPY/BRK-B→NYSE) after a first attempt via per-symbol Yahoo chart-meta immediately hit an HTTP-429 wall and was killed (2,218 requests replaced by 1). Of the prior universe's 2,218 US names: **872 NASDAQ (kept), 1,341 other-exchange (dropped), 5 not in the active listing (AYA/BMNP/GOOGM/GOOGN/STRD — dropped)**. Implementation: the baked `nasdaqListed` set + a filter at `build(_:)` — the single choke point every accessor (`worldwide`/`catalog`/`core`) routes through, so all views of the universe stay consistent; the full curated literals stay in source as the historical record (rollback = delete the filter). New universe: **901 names (29 `.SR` incl. ^TASI.SR — Tadawul's own index passes the `.SR` rule — + 872 NASDAQ, which includes the NASDAQ-listed ETFs QQQ/TLT: literal-correct, disclosed)**; `marketCount` honestly recounted 35 → **9** (groups with ≥1 kept name). **Infra preserved:** the SAR→USD rate (which powers every `.SR` honesty correction — holdingValue, pctOfAccountUSD, USD totals) no longer arrives via the universe scan; `StockSageStore.refreshInfraFX()` fetches `USDSAR=X` directly (like the regime's ^VIX) into `store.infraFX`, and `fxRatesToUSD` falls back to it — never a feed row or idea. ^GSPC/^VIX regime+benchmark fetches were always direct-by-symbol (unaffected). Legacy holdings in other currencies keep the existing honest degradation (excluded from USD totals + disclosed).
**Tests (spec-change re-ratification, each citing the directive):** the old universe pins were correct for the OLD spec — re-derived for the new one by standalone extraction of the source literals (never calling the code under test): worldwide == 901 (29+872 shown inline), marketCount == 9, Aramco-first preserved (worldwide[0] == 2222.SR), exclusion pins (SHEL.L/7203.T/0700.HK/RELIANCE.NS/BHP.AX/JPM/SPY/EURUSD=X/USDSAR=X/BTC-USD/ETH-USD all OUT), a whole-universe membership-rule sweep (.SR ∪ nasdaqListed), `infraFXSymbols == ["USDSAR=X"]`, the equity-classifier ballpark re-derived (901 − 1 index = 900 equities, bound 800–950), and the catalog "crypto discoverable" pin inverted.
**Verification:** typecheck ✅ clean; full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (one iteration: the equity-count ballpark bound from the old universe failed first, re-derived + re-ratified, suite then green). QA pixel pass + CI follow in the shipping trail.
**Result:** the board scans/ranks/searches ONLY Tadawul + NASDAQ; the banner reads "Live Tadawul + NASDAQ quotes across 9 market groups (901 names)"; the `.SR` FX honesty corrections keep working via the infra rate. Owner-directed scope change, not an evidence promotion — no engine scoring/threshold change.

## 2026-07-16 · Review fixes — the universe restriction's adversarial review caught 2 un-threaded FX-resolution copies (one DANGEROUS-direction); all 3 sites collapsed onto ONE resolver
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (new shared `fxRateToUSD(_:)` resolver; `fxRatesToUSD`, the currency-exposure card, and the portfolio-heat site all route through it), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageScanChunking.swift` + `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (stale 2,420/10-chunk comments → 901/4, history kept as "was 2,420"), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** the post-ship adversarial review of 7ba81b7 returned NOT CLEAN: the infraFX fallback (which keeps `.SR` conversions honest now that FX pairs left the universe) had been threaded into only ONE of THREE private copies of the FX-resolution logic in MarketsView. The two missed copies broke for `.SR` positions post-restriction: **(1) DANGEROUS direction — the portfolio-heat computation silently EXCLUDED an open `.SR` journal trade** (no tracked rate → `return nil`) so the heat gauge/risk gate read the book cooler than it is; **(2) display-only — the currency-exposure card showed a `.SR` holding as "unpriced"** while the headline USD total (via the one fixed site) priced it — an internal inconsistency on the same screen. Root-cause fix per the review's own recommendation (and the file's existing `conversionCurrencyForSymbol` precedent): ONE shared `fxRateToUSD(_ ccy:)` resolver — tracked quote direct → inverse → `store.infraFX` direct → inverse, nil = exclude-never-1:1 — and all three sites collapsed onto it, so the copy-drift that caused this is structurally impossible. Review residuals dispositioned: R1 (the fast-lane Crypto picker segment is empty-by-default post-restriction but degrades gracefully and still populates from a watchlisted crypto name — left as-is, a UX call beyond the directive); R2 (stale comments) fixed here; the review also CONFIRMED-OK the chunking (count-agnostic, 901→4 chunks), infraFX reachability on every refresh path (cold-launch gap = the documented honest fallback), the sample-backtest divergence (non-lying, fetches direct-by-symbol), and watchlist semantics (user picks still fetch — scan scope ≠ watchlist).
**Verification:** typecheck ✅ clean; full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`. Noted test gap (review): no test holds a `.SR` position across the three FX consumers — the structural collapse onto one resolver is the stronger guarantee (the copies no longer exist), and the resolver is exercised via `fxRatesToUSD`'s existing behavior.
**Result:** open `.SR` trades count toward portfolio heat again; the exposure card and headline total agree; comments describe the 901-name reality. The Tadawul+NASDAQ restriction wave is now CLOSED (implementation 7ba81b7 + this review pass).

## 2026-07-16 · Ideas card — stale Crypto/Forex affordances made DATA-DRIVEN (the universe review's R1 residual, completed)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (fast-lane board picker rendered only when a crypto lane exists + a stale persisted `.crypto` selection can never blank the strip), `Salehman AI/Views/BrowseMarketsView.swift` (static `matches(_:filter:)` classifier + static data-driven `availableFilters`; the picker offers only classes with ≥1 catalog match), `Salehman AITests/BrowseMarketsFilterTests.swift` (NEW — 2 hand-derived pins), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` (both entries), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** the universe-restriction review's R1 residual: with crypto/FX gone from the universe, the fast-lane "Crypto" segment and Browse's Crypto/Forex filters were permanently-empty-by-default affordances — visible contradictions of "only Tadawul and NASDAQ." Shape chosen: DATA-DRIVEN visibility, not removal — a watchlisted crypto name that yields ideas re-surfaces the crypto lane and (via the catalog, if the universe ever changes) the Browse filters, so nothing is hard-coded to the current restriction. Fast lane: the segmented picker renders only when `split.crypto` is non-empty, and the equity section's condition gains `|| split.crypto.isEmpty` so a pre-restriction persisted `.crypto` @AppStorage selection (now unreachable-to-change since the picker is hidden) can never blank the whole strip — the review's exact dead-selection finding. Browse: the classifier extracted to a testable static (instance delegates), `availableFilters` computed once off the static catalog.
**Verification:** typecheck ✅; full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` with both new pins FIRED by name (available-filters drop crypto/fx + keep all/stocks/etf/index — the .index survivor is ^TASI.SR, .etf is the QQQ/TLT group label; classifier per-class suffix rules on hand-built symbols). **Falsifiability probe:** made `availableFilters` unconditional (`AssetFilter.allCases` — the exact stale-affordance bug class) → `availableFiltersDropPermanentlyEmptyClasses()` failed **BY NAME** → restored → re-gated `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`. QA pixels: `FAILURES: none — all surfaces pass` (the QA fixtures seed a crypto idea, so the fast-lane picker still renders in captures — no drift, as expected).
**Result:** the UI no longer offers markets the owner removed; both affordances follow the data automatically. Autonomous follow-through of the 2026-07-16 owner directive.

## 2026-07-16 · Verification — the restricted universe proven against the LIVE feed (sentinel-gated headless measurement)

**Measured (one gated run, 23.2s — the duration itself proves the test FIRED, not skipped):** all four live assertions passed — coverage > 85% of the 901 names priced live; **2222.SR priced** (the Tadawul feed works post-restriction); **AAPL priced** (the NASDAQ side works); **USDSAR=X priced via `StockSageStore.infraFXSymbols`** (the new infraFX plumbing's one live dependency confirmed — the `.SR` honesty corrections' rate supply is real, not just unit-tested). ## 2026-07-16 · Ideas card — FIRST-REAL-TRADE review (owner "do more"): Tadawul tick-size placeability + .SR session facts (both display-only, both sourced)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTickSize.swift` (NEW — pure helper, sourced band table), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (fullPlanText appends the placeability note for `.SR`), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExecutionTiming.swift` (sessionNote's non-US branch gains the Tadawul session line), `Salehman AITests/StockSageTickSizeTests.swift` (NEW — 4 hand-derived tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` (new entry + ExecutionTiming note), `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** ideas-card-full-review invoked with focus = the owner's FIRST REAL TRADE journey (they're opening a Saudi broker account; the app's copy-plan becomes a real broker ticket). Two fix-now findings, both journey-blocking or journey-degrading on the `.SR` half of the restricted universe: **(F1)** the app has ZERO tick-size awareness (grep-verified) while Tadawul rejects off-grid orders — an ATR-derived "Stop: 28.63" is UNPLACEABLE in the 0.02 band; the owner's first real order would bounce. Fixed display-only: `StockSageTickSize.tadawulTick/Rounded/Aligned` carry the SOURCED band table (Saudi Exchange amended regime effective 2025-06-29; two agreeing sources — Argaam #1823880 + Sahm Capital support, fetched 2026-07-16: <25→0.01 · 25–49.98→0.02 · 50–99.95→0.05 · 100–249.90→0.10 · 250–499.80→0.20 · ≥500→0.50), and `fullPlanText` appends "⚠ Tadawul tick … place as …" naming each off-grid leg with its nearest-tick equivalent (≤½-tick drift disclosed). The engine's stop/target — and every EV/R:R derived — UNCHANGED (the note is the honest bridge to the ticket). **(F2)** sessionNote told `.SR` names to "enter near the close" without saying when Tadawul closes — the non-US branch now adds the session facts (Sun–Thu, continuous 10:00–15:00 Riyadh, closing auction to ~15:10; sourced from saudiexchange.sa Trading Cycle and Times + tradinghours.com, fetched 2026-07-16), same flag-only pattern as the shipped US note. **Confirmed-OK (no finding):** journal-fill flow, sizing/FX honesty (verified earlier this session), NASDAQ placeability (US $0.01 ticks — any 2-dp price places, no note needed).
**Verification:** typecheck ✅; full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` with all 4 new tests FIRED by name (every band boundary a genuine straddle: 24.99/25.00, 49.98/50.00, 99.95/100.00, 249.90/250.00, 499.80/500.00; hand-derived roundings 28.63→28.64, 101.37→101.4; note fires only for misaligned .SR legs; the .SR session line present for trending .SR, absent for US/range). **Falsifiability probe:** broke the 25–50 band to 0.05 (the plausible wrong-table bug) → `tickBandsMatchTheSourcedTableAtEveryBoundary()` failed **BY NAME** → restored → re-gated green. QA pixels: `FAILURES: none — all surfaces pass`.
**Result:** the copy-plan is now a placeable Tadawul ticket, and "near the close" is actionable in Riyadh time. Owner-visible on their first real `.SR` trade.

## 2026-07-16 · Sizing F3 — the share COUNT was currency-mixed: a "1%/trade" .SR plan actually risked ~0.27% (FX-correct now, root-cause fix)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePositionSizer.swift` (new pure overload `size(accountUSD:…:rawUnitToUSD:)`), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (ONE shared `sizedPosition(…)` helper beside `pctOfAccountUSD`; all 10 direct call sites rewired), `Salehman AITests/StockSagePositionSizerTests.swift` (+4 hand-derived tests), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** continuation of the first-real-trade review (F1/F2 shipped earlier today). `StockSagePositionSizer.size` divides a **USD** risk budget by a risk-per-share in the symbol's **RAW quote unit** (SAR for `.SR`, pence for `.L`) — so at USDSAR≈3.75, a $10k/1% plan on 2222.SR (risk 0.50 SAR/share) floored to 200 shares risking 100 SAR ≈ $26.7 = **0.27% of account while every surface said "at 1.0%/trade"**. The 2026-07-12 F1 and 2026-07-13 completeness-critic fixes corrected only the *labels* (both explicitly documented "`ps` is unchanged") — the count itself was never addressed; no prior deferral found (grep). Fix at root: a pure FX-aware overload converts the account into raw quote units first (making shares hit the stated risk fraction AND `pctOfAccount` native÷native — now agreeing with the view's `pctOfAccountUSD`), and one shared MarketsView helper (`sizedPosition`) routes ALL 10 sizing surfaces (idea-card size-it-now, best-opp CTA + accessibility labels, detail-sheet sizer panel, copy plan, per-order-minimum notes ×2, what-if) through it: tracked-FX symbols convert (`.SR` via the guaranteed `USDSAR=X` infraFX from the restriction wave), USD symbols and untracked-FX currencies stay **byte-identical** (rate-1 identity test-pinned; never guess a rate).
**Verification:** typecheck ✅; full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (1,924 cases) with all 4 new tests fired by name — SAR case hand-derived from the sizer's own contract (750 shares, 375 SAR ≈ $100 = the stated 1%; old path 200 shares = 0.27%), rate-1 byte-identity, pence generic (200 shares, 8,000 pence = $100), unusable-rate rejection. **Falsifiability probe:** removed the conversion (`accountUSD / rawUnitToUSD` → `accountUSD`) → `fxOverloadSizesTheStatedRiskFractionInTheSymbolsOwnCurrency` **and** `fxOverloadHandlesMinorUnitQuotesGenerically` failed BY NAME (identity/rejection unaffected — correct selectivity) → restored → class re-gated `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`. Visual QA run post-change.
**Residual (documented, all safe-direction):** engine callers (`StockSageCapitalAllocator` ×2, `StockSageTodayPlan`, `StockSageDecisionSnapshot`, `StockSageExpectedValue.weeklyDollarsIncludesUnfundableRow`, unwired `StockSageAllocationOptimizer`) still call the plain `size()` — every one errs toward UNDER-risking/over-flagging `.SR` (never over-risks); threading FX into those pure modules touches their test-locks and is queued as its own wave.
**Result:** an `.SR` "Size it now" / copy plan now genuinely risks the stated risk% — the claim on the ticket is true. Direction of change: .SR share counts RISE ~3.75× to match the contract the surfaces already stated.

## 2026-07-16 · Review fixes — tick-size placeability note: display-precision evaluation + per-leg band ticks (owner /code-review "markets")
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTickSize.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTickSizeTests.swift` (+1 test, existing note test re-pinned to the per-leg format), `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` (the missing new-module line — review score-75 process gap), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** owner invoked /code-review on the markets lane; no open PR exists (both repo PRs merged 2026-06-11), so the target was the newest unreviewed markets commit `e2c9f15`. 4 parallel review agents (CLAUDE.md, shallow-bug, git-history, code-comment lenses) → 3 deduped findings → Haiku confidence-scored: **(score 100)** `placeabilityNote` checked alignment on the RAW Double but displayed via `%.2f` — in the 0.01 band (every `.SR` name under 25 SAR) the tick grid IS the 2-dp grid, so ANY multi-decimal ATR-derived leg fired a self-contradictory "entry 23.46 → place as 23.46"; **(score 75)** the single headline tick (`stop ?? entry ?? target`) mislabeled legs straddling a band boundary (e.g. 49.97→49.98 at 0.02 beside 52.03→52.05 at 0.05); **(score 75)** PROJECT_CONTEXT.md missed the new-module line CLAUDE.md requires. Fix at root: each leg is normalized to its 2-dp DISPLAY price (`(v*100).rounded()/100` — the price the owner reads and types) before the grid check, and each clause carries its own `(%.2f tick)`; the headline tick is gone. `v.isFinite` added to the leg guard (inf/NaN can never render). Engine numbers untouched (display-only invariant unchanged).
**Verification:** fixtures derived STANDALONE (`derive_tick2.swift`: 23.456→23.46 aligned-in-0.01; 24.999→shows 25.00, aligned in the 0.02 band it displays into; 28.634→28.63→1431.5→away-from-zero 28.64; cross-band 49.97→49.98 @0.02 + 52.03→52.05 @0.05). Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 0 failures, all 5 tick tests fired by name. **Falsifiability probe:** removed the display-precision normalization (`shown = v`) → `evaluatesLegsAtDisplayPrecisionWithPerLegTicks()` failed **BY NAME** (others passed — correct selectivity) → restored → class re-gated `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`. No pixels change (the note reaches only the clipboard via fullPlanText → NSPasteboard; nothing renders it) → visual QA not applicable.
**Result:** the `.SR` ticket note now fires only when the displayed price is genuinely unplaceable, and every suggested price carries the tick that produced it. Sub-25-SAR names no longer get a nonsense advisory on every plan.

## 2026-07-16 · Sizing F3 wave-B — the DEPLOY plan sizes .SR at the stated fraction and the heat ledger goes USD-normalized (the F3 chain's last wired residual)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCapitalAllocator.swift` (`allocate(fxRatesToUSD:)`; step-4 sizes via the F3 map overload, step-5 heat = Σ native-at-risk × `rawQuoteUnitToUSD` ÷ account), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePositionSizer.swift` (`rawQuoteUnitToUSD(symbol:fxRatesToUSD:)` extracted as the ONE resolution both the map overload and the ledger use — overload behavior byte-identical), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (the single `allocate` call site threads `sizingFXRates(for:)`), `Salehman AITests/StockSageCapitalAllocatorTests.swift` (+2), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md`, this log.
**What & why:** last wired piece of the F3 chain (7f73d69 view surfaces → 19ce6f0 pure builders → this, the allocator). Before: the Deploy card's .SR rows sized ~3.75× under the stated risk fraction (currency-mixed budget), while the heat ledger summed their NATIVE at-risk against the USD account — numerically ≈ the USD budget, so heat looked right while overstating true .SR risk 3.75× (safe direction, but both numbers false). After: with a tracked rate, .SR sizes at the genuine fraction and each position's ledger entry converts native→USD (`×1` when USD/untracked — never guess). The whole-share-floor invariant `Σ ≤ cap·account` holds in USD terms by construction (native ≤ scaled·accountNative ⇒ ×rawUnit ≤ scaled·accountUSD). `AllocatedPosition` fields stay raw-native — every consumer already renders them through `approxAmount(symbol:)`/`majorUnitValue`. `suggestAdd`/`rebalanceToEdge` have NO app callers (view-unwired; only the unwired AllocationOptimizer references them) — deliberately untouched, inert until wired.
**Verification:** typecheck ✅; allocator class + full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; hand-derived pins off the existing python-verified fixture math (conviction 0.5 → halfKelly 0.1433333): 2222.SR @$10k with SAR 1/3.75 → 537 shares / 5,370 SAR at-risk (≈$1,432 = the stated 14.33%) beside AAPL's untouched 143/$1,430; totalHeat 0.28620 USD-normalized + a definitional re-derivation identity ≤1e-12; empty-map byte-identity pinned INCLUDING the old mixed .SR 143-share behavior. **Falsifiability probe:** stripped the `× rawUnit` from the heat sum (the realistic future "simplification" regression) → `fxMapSizesSRAtTheStatedFractionAndNormalizesHeatToUSD` failed BY NAME, byte-identity test unaffected (correct selectivity) → restored → class re-gated green. Visual QA post-change.
**Result:** the Deploy plan's share counts and its heat gauge are now both true for `.SR` names; the F3 currency-mixing defect class is closed everywhere it was wired. COORDINATION.md wave-B claim struck.

## 2026-07-16 · Sizing F3 follow-up — display-basis fixes that shipped INSIDE 009c4b8 (session-A work, documented here because the wave-B add-by-name swept the shared dirty tree)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (already committed in 009c4b8 — this entry documents the part of that diff the wave-B entry's Files line doesn't describe), this log.
**What & why:** three MarketsView display sites computed FX-corrected figures off the **portfolio-only `fxRatesToUSD` dict**, which lacks SAR whenever no `.SR` position is held — silently reverting "FX-corrected" numbers to the wrong basis for exactly the owner's first-trade case (an `.SR` idea, empty book). Root-cause fix: ONE shared `usdAmount(_ raw:symbol:)` converter backed by the `fxRateToUSD` RESOLVER (portfolio dict + infraFX `USDSAR=X`), consumed by (1) `pctOfAccountUSD` (wave-2 #2's pct was dict-based), (2) the detail-sheet "At risk" tooltip — whose `ps.dollarsAtRisk / acct` native÷USD pct became visibly wrong (3.75% shown for a true-1% `.SR` position) once F3 made `dollarsAtRisk` genuinely native — and (3) `positionSizerPanel`'s `usdFactor`. All fall back to prior behavior when a rate is untracked (never guess).
**Verification:** shipped inside 009c4b8's gates (typecheck ✅, full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, visual QA post-change — run by the wave-B session on the merged tree that contained these edits) + an **independent session-A full-suite re-run on the final 009c4b8 tree: `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 1,930 cases, zero failures** (two-session cross-verification of the same head). View-internal display math has no unit-test surface; QA pixels are the gate.
**Result:** every FX-corrected display figure now resolves SAR the same way the sizing does — dict-vs-resolver drift eliminated. Coordination note: shared-worktree add-by-name swept these dirty edits into 009c4b8; both sessions' logs now jointly describe that commit's full diff.

## Standing notes / known issues
- **Disk pressure (2026-06-07):** volume hit 100% full (tooling failed with ENOSPC). Cleared DerivedData + Trash → ~5 GB free. Keep an eye on it; `rm -rf ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/*` reclaims the Xcode cache safely. (Update: later cleanup of `AIFramework/.build` + scaffolds brought it to ~10 GB free.)
- **DeepSeek key exposed (2026-06-07) → RESOLVED by removal (2026-06-12):** owner pasted a DeepSeek key into chat; on 2026-06-12 the owner ordered the provider removed entirely. The integration is gone and the stored Keychain item was deleted. ONE owner action remains: **revoke the key server-side** at platform.deepseek.com/api_keys (it transited chat transcripts, so revoke even though the app no longer uses it).
- **Disk:** the volume is at/near 100%. `ollama rm qwen2.5-coder:32b` reclaims
  ~19 GB if the heavy model isn't needed. **(2026-06-18: ~2.8 GiB free — full
  `xcodebuild test` runs out of space mid-run; isolated suites still pass. Levers:
  `ollama rm qwen2.5-coder:32b` ≈19 GB, `rm -rf ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/*`
  ≈1.8 GB, empty Trash.)**
- **Gemini free tier:** user's Google account returns `limit: 0` (429) — account
  state, not an app bug.
- **Anthropic key:** still in UserDefaults (Chat A's lane); Keychain migration
  recommended for parity with the other 6 cloud brains.
- **Two-session coordination** lives in `COORDINATION.md` — read it before editing
  a file the other session owns.
- **🟥 Build verification in the sandbox (2026-06-13):** `xcodebuild` CANNOT compile
  here — it dies before compilation on `couldn't create cache file
  '/var/folders/.../T/xcrun_db-…' (errno=Operation not permitted)` + SimService
  crashes. So a "build green" claim from xcodebuild is **unverifiable** and once
  masked a real RED for a full day. Verify syntax with `swiftc -parse "<file>"`
  (lexical only — the `Cannot find 'DS'` errors it/`-typecheck` show in single-file
  mode are cross-module false positives, NOT real). Whole-tree sweep, count only
  source-located errors: `find "Salehman AI" -name '*.swift' -not -path '*/.*' |
  while read f; do swiftc -parse "$f" 2>&1 | grep -E "\.swift:[0-9]+:[0-9]+: error:"; done`.
- **Full Swift-6 verification recipe (2026-06-13, flag-parity corrected EOBD):** to catch
  Swift-6-mode-only errors (actor isolation, `#ConformanceIsolation`, captured-var races) you
  MUST pass `-swift-version 6` — without it they silently downgrade to warnings. **You MUST ALSO
  pass `-default-isolation MainActor -enable-upcoming-feature NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault`** —
  these are the two frontend flags `SWIFT_APPROACHABLE_CONCURRENCY = YES` expands to in this
  project. Omit them and swiftc analyzes a *different dialect* than Xcode: it treats unannotated
  code as `nonisolated` (not `@MainActor`), which BOTH hallucinates "called from nonisolated
  context" errors AND hides redundant-`await` warnings — the EOAN→EOBD bug (5 phantom warnings I
  chased for a day). With all three flags, swiftc matches the real build exactly (verified: same
  0/0, and a phantom `OpenAIClient SendingRisksDataRace` error disappears). Emit the app as a
  testable module (`swiftc -emit-module -module-name Salehman_AI -enable-testing -swift-version 6
  -default-isolation MainActor -enable-upcoming-feature NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault
  -module-cache-path $TMPDIR/mc -o $MODDIR/Salehman_AI.swiftmodule <app files>`), then typecheck
  the unit tests against it: `swiftc -typecheck -swift-version 6 -default-isolation MainActor
  -enable-upcoming-feature NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault -I $MODDIR -F
  "$(xcode-select -p)/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/Library/Frameworks" -plugin-path
  "$(xcode-select -p)/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/swift/host/plugins[/testing]"
  <test files>`. The `-plugin-path` is REQUIRED or every `@Test`/`#expect` reports
  "TestingMacros plugin not found." Pass file lists via a `find -print0` array (repo path has a space).
- **Verification blind spot — XCUITest `XCTAssert*`:** the recipe above CANNOT typecheck the
  `Salehman AIUITests` target — `XCTAssertTrue/False/XCTFail` are macros needing Xcode's
  XCUITest test-host linkage, so bare `swiftc` reports "cannot find 'XCTAssertTrue' in scope"
  even for canonical code (proven with a minimal probe). UI tests are NOT in the canonical gate
  (`-only-testing:"Salehman AITests"`) and don't `@testable`-import the app, so this is expected,
  not a real error — don't chase it. The unit suite uses Swift Testing `#expect`, which DOES
  resolve, so the actual test gate is fully verifiable.
- **Smart/curly-quote hazard (recurring):** some editor/tool turns `"` into `“ ”`.
  Broke the build 06-12→06-13 (curly used as a string DELIMITER, even on an SF
  Symbol name). Convention: **straight outer, curly inner** — `Text("No X match
  “\(q)”.")`. Curly INNER quotes are valid literal chars and read as premium
  typography; curly OUTER delimiters do not compile.
- **Xcode "live issues" go STALE after EXTERNAL edits (2026-06-13, EOBH):** when a `.swift` file is
  edited outside Xcode (CLI, the Edit tool, scripts), SourceKit's in-editor live-issue annotations
  can persist, pinned to line NUMBERS whose CONTENT has changed — e.g. a "no async operations occur
  within 'await'" warning lingering on a line where the `await` was already removed (the tell: a
  warning that's impossible for the current source). The real build is unaffected (the compiler
  reads disk). Authoritative signals: `swiftc -typecheck` (full isolation flags) + a clean Xcode
  Build — NOT the lingering navigator count. Clear the ghosts with Product → Clean Build Folder.
  Related: `swiftc -emit-module` under-reports body-level warnings (it skips function-body
  diagnostics); use `-typecheck` when you need those.
[2026-06-09 23:37] Read SOURCE_BUNDLE.md and CODEBASE_REVIEW.md. Identified brainReady switch in SettingsView.swift (8+ cases causing Keychain calls per review P2). Ready for refactor steps (1) BrainAdapter in LocalLLM, (3) extract brainReady.

[2026-06-10] tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py — background-mode injection rewrite
  Files: tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py
  What: Rewrote _safari_inject_and_send() with two-tier strategy.
    Strategy A (new primary): pure JS via 'do JavaScript' — execCommand('insertText')
    fills the composer, then geometric button scan finds+clicks Send. Zero focus steal;
    runs entirely in Safari background.
    Strategy B (fallback): clipboard + System Events quick-switch that saves the
    previous frontmost app, activates Safari for ~1.2s to paste+Enter, then
    immediately re-activates the previous app. User sees a brief flash instead of
    a permanent focus switch.
  Also: Grok Victor (via bridge session) prepended a project-context comment to
    tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py and created tools/grok_terminal_bridge.bak.
    Kept the comment (accurate); .bak file not tracked.
  Why: User asked bridge to run in background without interrupting work.
  Result: Bridge now attempts fully silent JS send; System Events is last resort.

[2026-06-10] Salehman AI/Persistence/JSONFileStore.swift — JSONStore protocol
  Files: Salehman AI/Persistence/JSONFileStore.swift
  What: Added `protocol JSONStore<Item>` with associated type + primary associated
    type syntax (Swift 5.7+). JSONFileStore<T> now declares `: JSONStore`. Zero
    changes to method bodies or call sites.
  Why: Enables test doubles — tests can inject an in-memory fake conforming to
    JSONStore instead of writing real files to Application Support.
  Note: Grok reported this as done but hadn't actually made any file changes.
    Applied here directly after verification.
  Result: BUILD SUCCEEDED, no new warnings.

[2026-06-10] tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py — UI noise filter + --auto shortcut
  Files: tools/grok_terminal_bridge.py
  What: (1) Added _UI_NOISE regex + _clean_ui_noise() that strips grok.com overlay
    lines (Upgrade to SuperGrok, SuperGrok, Thinking about your request, Explore/
    Investigate/Regenerate chips, Like/Dislike/Pin/Delete Chat buttons) before
    parse_commands() sees the page text — fixes the exit-127 spam from UI elements
    being run as shell commands. (2) Added --auto flag as shortcut for
    --mode auto --safari so users don't have to remember the two-part flag.
  Why: Every bridge session had 3-5 "Upgrade to SuperGrok" lines running as
    failed commands (exit 127), confusing Grok and wasting turns.
  Result: Both fixes verified. --auto shows in --help. Grok's patch attempts all
    failed (quote escaping in python3 -c); applied directly.

## 2026-06-11 (night) — launch lag fixed: 2.2s → 0.25s first-3s CPU (~9×)
**Owner:** "app always lags when its launched." Profiled (sample of first 3 s): main
thread pegged in AttributeGraph/metadata building the SwiftUI tree. Three causes, three fixes:
1. **Chat tree built at launch for nothing** — ContentView (the heaviest surface) was
   always-mounted while the default tab is Today. Now lazy like every other tab
   (`visitedChat`), with a RootView **mount-and-re-pulse**: a Settings/Live/New-chat/
   search signal arriving while the chat is unmounted mounts it, swallows the flag, and
   re-fires it 0.4 s later so the fresh `onChange` observers see the transition (0.1 s
   was too tight — the sheet missed once in testing; 0.4 s verified in pixels).
2. **knowledge.json (4.8 MB) decoded synchronously on main** — `KnowledgeStore.shared`
   first touch happened in TodayView.onAppear (default tab!). Count refresh now
   `Task.detached`; the store is lock-guarded so off-main first touch is safe.
3. **QA captures taxed normal launches** — a pending `qa/SNAPSHOT_REQUEST` made ANY
   launch render ~30 surfaces + audit ≈1 s in (and the parity watcher re-plants
   requests all day). Capture hooks now require the `--qa` launch argument
   (`open … --args --qa`, which tools/qa.sh passes); a request without the flag is left
   in place, never eaten. **QA-owner note: any direct `open` in watchers must add
   `--args --qa`.**
**Also:** Release build installed to **/Applications/Salehman AI.app** — the owner had
been daily-driving the Debug build from DerivedData.
**Measured:** first-3s CPU 2.16s→0.27s (Debug), 2.32s→0.24s (Release). Verified in
pixels: Settings opens from Today (re-pulse), chat mounts on ⌘2, QA loop green under
--qa, all audit surfaces pass.
**Files:** Views/RootView.swift, Views/TodayView.swift, Tools/QASnapshots.swift,
Tools/QACapture.swift, tools/qa.sh.

## 2026-06-12 — Code tab marathon (owner: "3 hours straight") · A: conversation persistence
**What:** Code-tab messages were pure `@State` — every quit (and the QA loop relaunches
the app all day) wiped the conversation, while the chat tab kept its history. Last 100
turns now round-trip through `JSONFileStore<[ChatMessage]>` (`code_history.json`): load
once on appear (off-main decode), save on change debounced 0.8s (off-main), clears
propagate via the same onChange. Swift-6 note: the store is built inside each detached
task (non-Sendable value can't be a shared static under default MainActor isolation).
**Verified:** seeded `code_history.json` → relaunch → screenshot shows the conversation
restored (user bubble + assistant markdown) — pixels, not claims.
**Files:** `Views/CodeView.swift`.

## 2026-06-11 (night) — marathon slice 5: multiple attachments
**What & why:** The composer now takes SEVERAL files: per-file chips (individually removable,
middle-truncated names, horizontal scroll when crowded), multi-select open panel
(`AttachmentLoader.pickFiles`), multi-file drop (all providers, was first-only), Finder
multi-copy paste (all URLs, was first-only). The send pipeline deliberately stays
single-attachment: `Attachment.merged` collapses N files into one synthetic attachment at
submit (every name + content, `––– name (kind) –––` separators) while a SINGLE file passes
through untouched so the image-vision path keeps firing. 3 new `AttachmentMergeTests` pin that
contract (empty→nil, single identity + vision fields, multi carries all names/bodies and never
claims vision).
**Files:** `Views/ContentView.swift`, `Persistence/Attachments.swift` (additive: merged +
pickFiles), `Salehman AITests/ChatComposerLogicTests.swift`; bundle regenerated. (Thanks to
whichever session added the missing `import Foundation` to the test file — caught pre-red.)

## 2026-06-11 (night) — marathon slice 9: slash-menu keyboard navigation
**What & why:** ↑/↓ now move a visible selection through the `/`-menu and ↵ picks the selected
row (was: ↵ always took the top). Selection + hover share the same highlight; the ↵ glyph
follows the selection; typing resets it to the top (piggybacked on the draft-persist
`onChange`). No conflict with ↑-recall: the menu only opens on a non-empty composer, recall
only fires on an empty one. Selection is clamped against current matches at use-time so a
narrowing query can never index out of bounds.

## 2026-06-12 (early) — marathon: post-adopt gate CLEAN + archive prune + a QA-eyes lesson
**What & why:** (1) Post-adoption cycle: failures `[]`, every chat surface within budget against
the new baselines — the marathon's visual state is fully baselined. (2) `pruneArchives(keep:100)`
after each archive write — timestamped names sort chronologically, so name order is age order;
unbounded growth would bloat `archives()` (it decodes every file). (3) **Eyes lesson:** the
21.7% `agents` drift looked like a light-mode leak in my image preview (white canvas, black
title) — but raw pixels, histogram (93.8% dark), and canvasFlat all said the file is a healthy
dark capture. My preview renderer inverts SOME mostly-dark PNGs; the audit reads raw pixels and
was right. Rule reinforced: a suspicious PICTURE gets verified with pixel math before any
cross-lane flag (almost sent Chat A/C on a snipe hunt). The drift is Chat C's intentional
agents polish (`4d3deb6`), passing.
**Files:** `Views/ContentView.swift`; bundle regenerated.
**Result:** Typecheck EXIT=0.

## 2026-06-12 (early) — marathon finale: adversarial self-review fixes (2 real defects)
**What & why:** Closing sweep over all 15 marathon slices found two real bugs, both mine:
(1) **multi-load race** — `loadingAttachment` was a Bool, so on a multi-file drop the FIRST
finished load cleared "loading" while siblings were still reading, briefly enabling Send with
half the files attached. Now a counter (`attachmentLoads`, computed `loadingAttachment`) —
every load site increments/decrements, Send waits for all. (2) **restore-under-stream** —
restoring an archive mid-generation would swap `vm.messages` underneath the running task;
`restoreArchive` now calls `vm.stop()` first (same graceful cancel as the stop button).
Reviewed and cleared the rest: newChat-while-running (startNewChat stops internally; partial
replies deliberately not archived), slash-selection clamping, balanced counter on the
no-pasteboard path, archive↔restore file lifecycle (no leak: restore deletes, prune caps).
**Files:** `Views/ContentView.swift`; bundle regenerated.
**Result:** Typecheck EXIT=0.

family (name / role-ending-in-known-profession / workplace / location / preference /

## 2026-06-12 — marathon H: Restore Checkpoint (run-level undo) + per-file revert
**What (deep-research #1+#2 — the trust features every shipped agent converged on):**
- **Restore all** in the Changed-files header: one click reverts EVERY file the last
  run touched to its pre-run snapshot (the snapshots already existed for the diff
  pane — this completes the loop Cursor/Claude-Code/Zed all ship).
- **Per-file revert**: hovering a changed-file row swaps its +N −M stats for an undo
  button — accept the good files, revert just the bad one.
- Engine: `CodeWorkspace.revert(file:toSnapshot:)` (snapshot back, or DELETE files the
  run created — that is their pre-run state) + `restoreFromSnapshot`/`restoreAllChanged`
  which sync changed-list, stats, tree, open file/diff pane, and git dots.
**Verified:** build green; `RestoreSnapshotTests` (3 temp-dir round-trips: modified→
snapshot restored, created→deleted, missing→throws) — TEST SUCCEEDED.
**Files:** `Views/CodeView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/RestoreSnapshotTests.swift`.

## 2026-06-12 (~01:1x) — Chat C: Swift 6.2 concurrency-isolation audit → MemoryStore.recall off-main fix
**What & why:** Owner-requested deep-research on Swift 6 strict concurrency + SwiftUI macOS, then a
codebase audit of the 3 to-dos it surfaced. Findings: (1) build settings confirm BOTH
`SWIFT_DEFAULT_ACTOR_ISOLATION = MainActor` and `SWIFT_APPROACHABLE_CONCURRENCY = YES` (all 6 configs,
Swift 6.0 mode) — unmarked types are MainActor-isolated; `nonisolated async` is `nonisolated(nonsending)`.
(2) Under that default, `MemoryStore` (lock-based `@unchecked Sendable`) had `remember`/`embed`/`autoExtract`/
`extractFacts`/`persist` correctly `nonisolated`, but **`recall` and `cosine` were missed** → silently pinned
to MainActor. `recall` is the heavy path (NLEmbedding + cosine over every stored memory), called
**synchronously at `AgentPipeline.swift:458`** → ran on the main thread. Marked both `nonisolated` (lock-safe;
identical to the sibling pattern; no longer main-pinned). (3) No live Codable-conformance trap: every
`Codable` type is a `struct` (several explicitly `Sendable`/`nonisolated`) and the app builds — empirical proof.
**FLAGGED for Chat A (not edited — their lane):** to move recall fully off-main, offload the
`AgentPipeline.swift:458` call site (`Task.detached`/`@concurrent`).
**Result:** MemoryStore change compiles clean (whole-module: **0 MemoryStore errors**). Full
`** TEST SUCCEEDED **` marker currently BLOCKED by an UNRELATED red — `CodeView.swift:895-899` `welcomeAppeared`
not in scope (Chat B's active CodeView WIP, landed red after my 01:00 green). Flagged on COORDINATION board;
not fixing (not my lane). Will reconfirm green once CodeView compiles.
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Persistence/MemoryStore.swift`, `COORDINATION.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-12 (~01:3x) — Chat C: tabs marathon cycle 8 (Memory viewer sort)
**What & why:** Memory sheet (`Views/MemoryView.swift`) had a search filter but no sort.
Added a pure `MemorySort` enum (`newest`/`oldest`/`alphabetical`) with `apply(_:filter:)`
that folds the case-insensitive substring filter in before ordering — single source of
truth for the list, and unit-testable. Facts arrive oldest-first from `allFacts()`, so
`newest` reverses; `alphabetical` is `localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare`. Wired a sort `Menu`
into a new `controlsRow` (search shown when >3 facts, sort when >1), mirroring the proven
`KnowledgeSort` enum→Menu pattern. New `MemorySortTests` (7 cases): store-order, reverse,
case-insensitive A–Z, filter-before-sort, blank/no-match, empty input, title+icon non-empty.
**Result:** Feature verified green — isolated `MemorySortTests` run compiled the whole app
(MemoryView + the prior MemoryStore fix included) and passed: `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (commit
`60d7934`). The subsequent FULL-suite run went red, but **100% in `SettingsView.swift`
(Chat B's lane, `activeBrain*` scope, 10×)** — landed between my two runs; 0 errors in any
of my files. Flagged on the COORDINATION board (banner); not fixing (not my lane). This run
also CONFIRMED the previously-pending green for the `458e4c5` MemoryStore `recall`/`cosine`
`nonisolated` fix (CodeView red cleared by Chat B).

## 2026-06-12 (~02:00) — Chat D slice 1: Settings brainReady perf seam + 5th blocked suite enabled + Hijri filename fix
**Who:** Chat D (new session tonight — owner: "work on salehman with 3 other sessions", ultracode/xhigh inline, full-auto).
**What & why:**
- **CODEBASE_REVIEW HIGH perf fix:** `SettingsView.brainReady` fired live Keychain
  `hasKey()` syscalls per visible grid cell on EVERY body recompute (each keystroke +
  each 5s poll tick ≈ 25+ `SecItemCopyMatching`; `.salehman` alone walked the 10-key
  `SalehmanEngine.hasAnyCloud` chain) while the cached `@State` *KeySaved flags sat
  unused. Extracted the rules to NEW `Views/SettingsBrainReadiness.swift` —
  `BrainReadiness` (pure per-`BrainPreference` reachability over plain Bools) — and
  `brainReady` is now a thin caller fed ONLY by cached flags: **0 Keychain syscalls
  per recompute**. Behavior preserved exactly (rule-for-rule copy of the old switch).
- Same file gains `ActiveBrainProbe` (the overlapping testActiveBrain-runs counter as
  a value type), `BrainPing.verdict` (ping-reply classification), and
  `AnthropicKeyPresentation` (no-leak key subtitle) — SettingsView rewired to all
  three (3 @State vars → 1 probe; logic unchanged).
- **`SettingsBrainReadyTests` ENABLED** (4th of the 5 blocked §4 suites): the 5
  disabled stubs replaced by 7 real tests pinning `.auto` local-only (cloud keys must
  never light it), `.freeAuto` never-spends, `.salehman` cloud-first + named-model
  local floor, ensemble/coding pool membership, probe overlap rules (superseded run
  never publishes; spinner clears at zero in-flight), ping verdict, and subtitle
  no-leak assertions. (Stub names referenced the pre-cloud-first model — renamed to
  today's semantics.)
- **Cross-lane one-liner (claimed on board): `ContentView.swift` `exportFilename`** —
  first real run of Chat A's exporter tests caught a genuine cross-locale bug: a bare
  `DateFormatter` follows the DEVICE calendar, and this Mac runs Hijri (xcresult path
  literally `…1447.12.26…`), so export filenames rendered Hijri-era dates and
  `usesTitleAndLastActivityDate` failed. Fix: `df.locale = en_US_POSIX` (Apple's
  fixed-format rule).
- Docs: PROJECT_CONTEXT (SettingsView row + new seam row + §4 suite status).
**Process note (own fault, logged per house rules):** my sequential edits opened a
~minutes-long red window (`activeBrain*` decls replaced before usages) that Chat C's
typecheck caught and flagged as Chat B's. Resolved + apologized on the board; lesson:
order multi-site renames usages-first, decls-last.
**Verified (by measurement):** app `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` (canonical command); QA
capture run — `settings` surface passes all checks (baselineDiff 0.33%, in budget,
render 402ms; grid dots pixel-identical through the cached-flag path); AITests run:
**454 passed, my 7 SettingsBrainReadyTests all green**; sole failure was the
pre-existing Hijri filename bug above → fixed, full-suite re-run pending behind
another session's build-DB lock (will confirm the `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` marker
before releasing the lane).
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/SettingsBrainReadiness.swift` (new),
`Salehman AI/Views/SettingsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/ContentView.swift`
(one-liner, claimed), `Salehman AITests/SettingsBrainReadyTests.swift`,
`PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`, `COORDINATION.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-12 — marathon M: /shot — attach your latest screenshot with on-device OCR
**What (owner: "add send last screenshot"):** the composer gets a camera button + a
`/shot` slash command. One click finds the newest image in the user's REAL screenshot
location (`com.apple.screencapture location` → ~/Pictures/Screenshots here; Desktop
fallback) and attaches it — and because the local 14B has no image input, the
screenshot's TEXT is extracted **on-device** (Vision OCR, accurate mode, language
correction) and attached as context: error dialogs, terminal output, UI text all become
usable words. Design language on the chip: live thumbnail in a machined micro-tile,
an accent OCR badge, seated ✕ with press physics.
**Verified:** build green; `ScreenshotGrabberTests` ×3 (newest-image picker with
injected dir + mtimes, non-images ignored, OCR reads a rendered "SALEHMAN OCR 42"
PNG) — TEST SUCCEEDED. One test-only trap fixed: the enumerator returns
/private/var/… for /var/… temp paths, so the picker test compares names, not URLs.
**Files:** `Views/CodeView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/ScreenshotGrabberTests.swift`.

## 2026-06-12 (~03:0x) — Chat D slice 2: R1 brain-routing seam + Offline-Mode leak FIX + last suite enabled
**Who:** Chat D. **What & why:**
- **CODEBASE_REVIEW R1 (the top refactor):** routing decisions (gating, roster
  membership, order, offline rules) were re-implemented across **11 sites** in
  `LocalLLM` — the drift class behind multiple confirmed bugs. NEW
  `LLM/BrainRouting.swift` is now the ONLY place they live: `CloudProvider`
  (ten providers; free/coding/ensemble roster constants; the ten key checks;
  model/client maps), `BrainRouteConfig` (pure snapshot; `.live()` keeps
  currentBrain's per-pref probe laziness), `BrainRouting.dispatch` (exactly one
  target per pref) + roster builders + `reachableBrain`/`anyBrainReachable`.
  The `generate`/`generateStreaming`/`chat` cascades each became one `switch`
  over the dispatch with per-provider execution helpers (`cloudOneShot`/
  `cloudStream`/`cloudConversational`); `currentBrain` is a thin caller;
  every roster site consumes the plan. The 13 `*Allowed` gates deleted.
- **🔴 FIXED — Offline-Mode cloud leak** (same class as the fixed WebTools
  leak): the three cascades had NO `isOfflineOnly` gate, so a pinned cloud
  brain still made real HTTP calls from direct callers (Settings ping,
  StockSage briefings, title gen) under Offline Mode — and a pinned
  `.salehman` walked its whole cloud chain **including the PAID DeepSeek
  backstop**, while `SalehmanEngine.generate/generateStream/generateWithTools`
  never checked offline at all. Fixed at BOTH layers: `dispatch` hard-gates
  the ten cloud pins → `.unavailable` (the contract `currentBrain` always
  documented), and SalehmanEngine now skips cloud chains offline with
  endpoint engines qualifying only on loopback (the `generateOnDevice` rule).
  Regression-pinned in tests.
- **`BrainRoutingDispatchTests` ENABLED** — the LAST of the 8 review suites
  (all 8 now active): 5 tests pin single-dispatch/no-fallthrough + the
  pin↔provider bijection, `.auto`/`.ollama` never-cloud (even with every key
  configured), the offline hard-gate + all-rosters-empty-offline, freeAuto
  free-only membership/order, and documented roster sets.
- **🟠 FOUND, NOT fixed (owner call): ensemble/DeepSeek drift** —
  `anyBrainReachable` counts DeepSeek but the ensemble fan-out roster never
  included it → a DeepSeek-only setup reads "reachable" with an empty
  fan-out. Preserved verbatim (visible behavior change to fix), documented in
  `CloudProvider.ensembleRoster` + pinned by a test; one-line opt-in when
  wanted. **Open question:** Unsloth/vLLM REMOTE (non-loopback) endpoint pins
  are still untouched by Offline Mode (matches currentBrain's documented
  contract) — flag if that should tighten.
- Docs: PROJECT_CONTEXT (LocalLLM row + new BrainRouting row + §4 all-8 note),
  ARCHITECTURE tree. One test-side lesson re-learned: `#expect(xs.allSatisfy(\.p))`
  macro-expands to a throwing call — use a closure (same as the QAGeometry fix).
**Verified (by measurement):** app `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` after EVERY rewire
step (additive file → currentBrain/rosters → ladders → engine gates — no red
windows); full `Salehman AITests` **`** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (466 passed**, incl.
the 5 new routing tests); QA capture post-rebuild: `settings` 0.33% / `today`
0.00% stable (the `chat_*` drifts + `chat_history` failure render Chat A's
just-committed design choreography — their baseline re-adopt, flagged).
**Files:** `Salehman AI/LLM/BrainRouting.swift` (new), `Salehman AI/LLM/LocalLLM.swift`,
`Salehman AI/LLM/SalehmanEngine.swift`, `Salehman AITests/BrainRoutingDispatchTests.swift`,
`PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `COORDINATION.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-12 (~03:0x) — Chat C: SESSION STOP (owner "STOP AND LOG EVERYTHING") + deep-research capture
**Owner directive:** stop the high-end-visual marathon and log everything. Marathon halted, no further
wakeups scheduled. All code work this session is already committed + logged; this entry preserves the
three inline deep-research reports (run inline/solo per the absolute no-Workflow rule) and their open
to-dos so they aren't lost.

**This session's shipped work (all committed + individually logged above):**
- Cycle 7: `MemoryStoreFactsTests` — 16 cases (extractFacts patterns + remember dedup/trim). `3d7e8a1`.
- Swift 6.2 concurrency audit → `MemoryStore.recall`/`cosine` marked `nonisolated` (heavy NLEmbedding+cosine
  scan was MainActor-pinned, called sync at `AgentPipeline.swift:458`). `458e4c5`.
- Cycle 8: `MemorySort` (newest/oldest/A–Z + filter) wired into the Memory sheet + 7 tests. `60d7934`.
- Onboarding high-end visual pass (ambient glow, eyebrow, dimensional tile, hover CTA, entrance). `da3630d`.
- **Grey backdrop** (owner request "today should be grey"): `DS.Palette.bgTop`/`bgBottom`/`modalBG` warm-red
  → neutral grey + `BackgroundView` glows red→white; accent kept red #FA2E4A. Cross-lane (DS=Chat B,
  BackgroundView=Chat A), board-flagged. `ff065ec`.
- Refined the `run-salehman-ai` skill (24 surfaces, CVD pass, backdrop+DB-lock gotchas). `9e58716` (skill is gitignored).
- AboutView high-end pass + honest copy (de-staled "on-device"→"cloud-first"). `23cc98b`.

**Deep-research #1 — Swift 6 strict concurrency + SwiftUI macOS (findings):** project confirmed on
`SWIFT_DEFAULT_ACTOR_ISOLATION=MainActor` + `SWIFT_APPROACHABLE_CONCURRENCY=YES` (Swift 6.0). `nonisolated async`
⇒ `nonisolated(nonsending)` (runs on caller); use `@concurrent` to offload heavy work. Observation `@Observable`
only invalidates on read properties. ACTIONED: recall/cosine nonisolated (above). OPEN: offload the
`AgentPipeline.swift:458` recall call site (`Task.detached`/`@concurrent`) — Chat A's lane, flagged.

**Deep-research #2 — high-end macOS visual design (open to-dos):**
1. Soften primary text `Color.white`→~0.94 (#F0F0F0) to cut glare (DS-level, Chat B's lane — flag, don't do unasked).
2. Widen surface-elevation step to ~5–8% luminance (codeSurface vs codeSurfaceSide currently ~3%).
3. Extend hover-lift (scale 1.03 + glow, `.smooth`) from Onboarding/About to Today/Markets cards + Knowledge/Notes rows.
4. Codify a ~1.2 type scale (13–15pt base, ~6 steps) in DesignSystem to stop `.system(size:)` drift across sessions.
5. (macOS 26+) Liquid Glass on chrome only, `if #available` gated, Regular variant, under-glassed.
Premium values: button press 0.92/0.18s, hover 1.03/0.22s, spring response 0.3 snappy/0.55/0.9, damping 0.5/0.75/0.95.
Dark-mode: no pure black/white, elevation by luminance not shadow, desaturate accents on dark, WCAG 4.5:1.

**Deep-research #3 — shipping / local LLM / icon / agent UX (open to-dos):**
1. Plan DIRECT distribution (Developer ID + notarized DMG + Sparkle) — App Store sandbox blocks the shell/Ollama
   tool layer. Pipeline: Hardened Runtime, `notarytool`+`stapler`, NEVER `codesign --deep` (sign inside-out),
   Sparkle EdDSA-signed appcast, `CFBundleVersion` must increment. (notarytool accepts .dmg/.zip/.pkg.)
2. Graduated tool-approval friction — auto-allow read/search, reserve the safety card for shell/destructive/spend
   (biggest UX win; currently approves every tool equally). Touches ToolPolicy/Agents (Chat A lane) — coordinate.
3. Recommend Ollama 0.19+ (MLX backend, +57% prefill/+93% decode, needs ≥32GB); surface measured tok/s; default 4-bit.
4. (macOS 26+) Icon Composer 2-layer icon (sparkles glyph + tile), bold/simple, don't bake effects; AppIcon fallback.
App is already well-aligned with 2026 agent-UX (named agents, Memory sheet, Restore Checkpoint, .auto routing).

**Result:** session stopped cleanly; tracked working tree clean (all source committed); research preserved.
**Files:** `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.

**What:** 13 edits across 8 files — every conditional `Image(systemName: condition ? A : B)` that was missing animated transitions now has `.contentTransition(.symbolEffect(.replace)) + .animation(DS.Motion.smooth, value:)`:

- `ContentView`: `mic`↔`mic.fill` dictation button

## 2026-06-13 — marathon EOT: test coverage — FileKind.icon + FileTreeBuilder.build (Chat A)
**What:** `FileKind.icon(for:)` and `FileTreeBuilder.build(files:root:)` had zero coverage. Added `FileTreeTests.swift` (new file, 22 tests across two structs). `FileKindIconTests` (14 tests): pins the SF Symbol string for every extension family (swift, py, js/jsx/mjs/cjs, ts/tsx, json, yml/yaml/toml, md/markdown/txt/rst, html/xml/css/scss, sh/bash/zsh, C family, Rust/Go/Ruby/Java/Kotlin, image/PDF), verifies the "doc" fallback for unknown extensions, and confirms the `.lowercased()` guard makes matches case-insensitive. `FileTreeBuilderTests` (8 tests): empty→[], single file at root with correct URL and no-dir flag, nested file creates intermediate dir node with nil URL, directories-before-files ordering, case-insensitive sorting for both files and directories, out-of-root file fallback to lastPathComponent, and a deeply-nested 3-level hierarchy end-to-end.
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/FileTreeTests.swift` (new, 22 tests).
**Result:** 22 new tests; SOURCE_BUNDLE.md regenerated.

## 2026-06-13 — marathon EOS: test coverage — GrokWatchTool.parse log-parser (Chat A)
**What:** Unlocked `GrokWatchTool.parse` for testing by removing its `private` modifier (access unchanged at the Swift level — it stays module-internal). Added `GrokWatchToolTests.swift` (new file, 13 tests across 5 assertion categories) to pin all five parsing behaviors: (1) task extraction from the `task: '...'` header with escaped-quote unescaping and 180-char truncation, (2) session-ID from filename, turn counting, and elapsed-time extraction from `[HH:MM:SS|XmYYs]` timestamp prefix, (3) CMD/output pair collection with bridge-line filtering (`[`, `→`, `✓`, "sending output back" filtered out) and 120-char output truncation, (4) DONE detection from `[[DONE]]` and `TASK_COMPLETED_SUCCESSFULLY` tokens, and (5) 6-entry ring buffer eviction (cmd_1/cmd_2 dropped when 8 turns accumulate).
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Tools/GrokWatchTool.swift` (private→internal on parse), `Salehman AITests/GrokWatchToolTests.swift` (new, 13 tests).
**Result:** 13 new tests; API sig verified; SOURCE_BUNDLE.md regenerated.

## 2026-06-13 — marathon EOU: test coverage — CodeWorkspace.lineDiff + CodeView.sanitizedHistory (Chat A)
**What:** Exhaustive survey of all remaining `nonisolated static func` targets across the entire codebase confirmed near-saturation; two genuine gaps remained in `CodeView.swift`. (1) `LineDiffTests` (8 tests) — pins `CodeWorkspace.lineDiff`'s LCS invariants: identical content → all `.same`, added line at end / prepended line both emit `.add` at the correct position, removed-from-middle emits `.remove`, changed single line emits `.remove` then `.add` (the `>=` tiebreak guarantees red-before-green), and the accounting invariant `#same + #add == new-line-count` / `#same + #remove == old-line-count`. (2) `SanitizedHistoryTests` (5 tests) — pins `CodeView.sanitizedHistory`'s narration-stripping pass: empty list, user messages are never modified (even if they contain scaffold markers), clean assistant messages return with unchanged id, dirty assistant messages have `\nResponse:` scaffold stripped (only the payload survives), `<think>` blocks stripped, and id/timestamp/imagePath/duration are preserved when text is cleaned.
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/CodeViewTests.swift` (new, 13 tests).
**Result:** 13 new tests; full survey confirms no other pure-function gaps remain; API signatures verified; SOURCE_BUNDLE.md regenerated.

## 2026-06-13 — marathon EOR: test coverage — RepoPacker.byteString + Attachment.merged (Chat A)
**What:** Two new test structs plugging the last clearly-identified pure-function gaps after a full survey of Chat A's lane. (1) `RepoPackerByteStringTests` (3 tests, appended to `RepoPackerTests.swift`) — covers all three branches of `RepoPacker.byteString`: bytes path (0, 512, 1023 B), KB boundary (1023 B / 1024 KB crossover, 512 KB), MB boundary (1 048 576 = 1.0 MB, 2 621 440 = 2.5 MB). (2) `AttachmentMergeTests` (new file, 6 tests) — covers `Attachment.merged`'s three-case collapse contract: empty list → nil, single item → identity pass-through with fileURL+isImage preserved for cloud vision, multiple items → text-only merged attachment with combined name, kind="files", icon="doc.on.doc", `––– name (kind) –––\ntext` section format, and fileURL/isImage reset to nil/false. Confirmed via API-signature grep (DerivedData sandbox blocked xcodebuild). Full survey of remaining pure-function statics in Agents/*, Tools/*, LLM/*, Intelligence/*, Media/*, Persistence/* confirmed all other pure helpers are already covered.
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/RepoPackerTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/AttachmentMergeTests.swift`.
**Result:** 3 new tests + 1 new file (6 tests) added; API signatures verified; SOURCE_BUNDLE.md regenerated.
