## 2026-06-22 — bug-hunt: stuck `aiIsRunning` flag after Stop / New Chat (Chat A)
**What:** `ChatViewModel.stop()` reset `self.isRunning` but never cleared the app-wide `AppState.shared.aiIsRunning`. The in-flight `send`/`transcribeMedia` task returns early at its `if Task.isCancelled { return }` checks *without* clearing `aiIsRunning` (only the normal-completion paths do, at lines 180/208/222). So after the user hit ⌘. Stop, the Stop button, Escape, or New Chat (which calls `stop()`), `AppState.aiIsRunning` stayed stuck `true` — `BottomShortcutBar` kept promoting the "⌘. Stop" hint and running its `.animation(value: app.aiIsRunning)` indicator as if a reply were still generating, until the next full send completed. Fix: clear `AppState.shared.aiIsRunning = false` inside `stop()`, the single cancel chokepoint. **Why it matters:** user-visible — the chat bar lies about run state after every cancel/new-chat. **Verify:** code is a one-line set on an already-`@MainActor` method that mirrors the existing `= false` writes on the completion paths; couldn't run `xcodebuild` (sandbox blocks DerivedData writes) but the change is type-trivial.
**Files:** `Views/ChatViewModel.swift`.

## 2026-06-27 — iter2b: shared fixtures hardening + math-invariant harness (Chat A)
**What:** Multi-lens adversarial review (correctness/typesafety, edge/duplication, MATH-AUDIT by Opus) identified four issues in the new `SageFix`/`StockSageMathInvariantTests` files. All majors fixed; minors addressed or documented. (1) **Short-direction stop/target inversion fixed** — `SageFix.idea()` previously set `stop = price − riskDistance` unconditionally; for `.sell`/`.reduce` actions the stop must be ABOVE entry. Fixed by branching on `action == .sell || action == .reduce`. Header comment updated to document the convention and note that `SageFix.idea()` is not yet adopted by EVTests/AlertsTests. (2) **`ideaShortHasStopAboveEntry` test added** — new `@Test` in `StockSageMathInvariantTests` pins `.sell` stop above entry, `.reduce` stop above entry, and `.buy` stop below entry, with exact R:R assertions at EXACT_EPS. (3) **Kelly tolerance raised to 1e-9 for exact integer points** — all five pure-integer Kelly golden vectors (`kellyGoldenVectors`, `kellyHalfAndQuarterAreExactDivisions`) now use `EXACT_EPS = 1e-9`, matching and not weakening the existing `StockSageKellyTests` contract. `EPS = 1e-6` is now reserved for irrational TSMOM/volatility results as documented. (4) **Tautological assertions documented** — `halfKelly - fullKelly/2` and `quarterKelly - fullKelly/4` checks now carry explicit "tautological" comments so reviewers don't mistake them for independent golden pins.
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageTestFixtures.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** — all 12 math-invariant golden-vector tests pass (ε<1e-9 for Kelly, ε<1e-6 for TSMOM/vol). 32+ golden vectors pinned. Harness ready for iter3 (52wk-high) to add its fixture series and EV assertions.

## 2026-06-27 — iter4: Platt-scaling conviction calibration for small trade histories (Chat A)
**What:** Added Platt sigmoid calibration as the small-N-safe alternative to isotonic regression inside `StockSageConvictionCalibration`. Added `nonisolated static let isotonicMinSamples = 1000` seam constant. Modified `fit(_:)` to branch: <1000 samples → new `private fitPlatt(...)`, ≥1000 → existing isotonic body moved verbatim into `private fitIsotonic(...)`. `fitPlatt` uses Newton's method (25-iter bound, ‖Δ‖<1e-12 early-exit) on Platt-smoothed targets (t+=（N++1)/(N++2), t-=1/(N-+2)), clamps A≤0 for monotonicity, emits the same `[Bin]` substrate evaluated at band midpoints. Edge cases: all-same-label → flat prior; singular Hessian → break at current estimate; A>0 → clamped to 0. Public surface (`winProb`, `bins`, `sampleSize`, `fit`, `fit(fromBacktest:)`, `fit(fromJournal:)`, `validateOutOfSample`) byte-identical. Added `// MARK: 6 — Platt` in `StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`: 5 golden tests (`plattGoldenVectorSymmetricSeparable` with closed-form A=ln(25/289)/0.6, B=−A/2, rational anchors p(0.2)=5/22/p(0.8)=17/22; monotone; inverted-clamp; degenerate-label; selection-seam threshold).
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ typecheck clean (Swift 6, zero errors). No downstream files touched.

## 2026-06-27 — iter3: binary TSMOM crash-veto → continuous variance-scaling scalar (Chat A)
**What:** Replaced the binary −0.20 TSMOM crash-veto in `StockSageAdvisor.advise()` (old lines 211-216) with a continuous inverse-variance momentum scalar (Barroso & Santa-Clara 2015). Scalar = min(1, target_vol / realized_vol) — attenuation-only, defaulting to 1.0 (no-op) for nil/NaN/Inf/≤0 realized vol. Added three new `nonisolated static` members: `varianceScalarTargetVol = 0.20`, `varianceScalarEps = 1e-8`, `varianceScalar(realizedVol:)`. Hoisted the single `annualizedVolatility` call above the cap block (ITER3) so both the scalar and the stop sizing reuse one computation. Order of operations: (1) score built additive, (2) varScalar = min(1, 0.20/max(vol,ε)) defaulting to 1.0, (3) rawTrendFamily × varScalar, (4) |scaledFamily| ≤ 0.65 re-asserted (hard clamp), (5) score = scaledFamily + nonFamily (RSI/nudge terms untouched), (6) conviction = min(|score|,1), (7) Kelly sizing. RSI falling-knife guard (oversoldBounceIsBuyable, lines 145-156) left entirely untouched — the +0.25 bounce credit lives in nonFamily and is never scaled. Added `// MARK: 5 — variance scalar (ITER3)` in `StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift` with 7 unit pins on varianceScalar (nil/NaN/Inf/0/0.40/0.20/0.10 → exact closed-form) and 5 integration tests: (a) momentumCrash→non-buy, (b) calm/low-vol clamp to 1.0 + cap-before-Kelly bound, (c) byte-identity witness on cleanUptrend, (d) cleanUptrend still → Strong Buy, (e) fallingKnife bounce still denied.
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ typecheck clean (Swift 6, zero errors). Warnings on pre-existing unused `isBuy`/`isSell` in advise() body — not introduced by this change.

## 2026-06-27 — iter5 hardening: 52wk-high proximity correctness fixes + test coverage (Chat A)
**What:** Three correctness issues identified by multi-lens audit (correctness, edge-regression, math-intent) resolved in the continuous 52wk-high proximity term (iter5). (1) `highs ?? closes` fallback removed — when `highs` is nil the proximity block is now skipped entirely via `let highs` nil-guard at the `if` condition (line 263), preventing the `pth = 1.0` inflation on any monotone close-only uptrend. Added explanatory comment above the guard. (2) pth clamped to 1.0 before computing the contribution (`let clampedPth = Swift.min(hp.pth, 1.0)`) to enforce the documented +0.010 ceiling against live intraday prints that exceed the prior-close 52wk high. (3) `highProximityWeight` docstring corrected: the false "too small to re-inflate a Buy into a Strong Buy (proof: tests)" claim replaced with an accurate note documenting that the term CAN promote a borderline Buy to Strong Buy by at most 0.010, cross-referencing the new boundary test. Three new tests added to `StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift` (MARK: 7): `highProximity_bearRegime_gate_isTheActualSuppressor` — decouples synthetic highs from momentumCrash closes to guarantee both pth >= 0.90 AND bearTrend simultaneously, plus a paired cleanUptrend control that confirms the gate is the sole suppressor; `highProximity_borderlineBuy_canPromoteToStrongBuy` — closed-form math pin proving a pre-proximity score of 0.495 (Buy range) plus maxProx=0.010 yields 0.505 (StrongBuy), intentionally documents the Buy→StrongBuy promotion; the original `highProximity_bearRegime_suppressed` annotated as a SECONDARY check (momentumCrash pth≈0.34 hits long-side floor before gate).
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** — all prior iter3/iter4/MARK:7 tests green, clean-uptrend still Strong Buy, golden vectors ε<1e-6 intact.

## 2026-06-27 — ITER-1 finish: backtester parity golden-vector test + honest DSR trials/survivorship caveat (Chat A)
**What:** (1) TASK 1a-2 — added `StockSageBacktestParityTests.swift`: three-test regression guard proving the backtester path (runTrades) and the live-advise path (advise(history:benchmark:)) can never silently diverge. parity test uses 130-bar cleanUptrend (symbol) + approaching52wHigh (benchmark, different slope) so RS is live; pathB reconstructs runTrades' two guards (volumes.count==n gate + nearest-prior bj benchmark alignment) via a local helper that encodes the alignment contract rather than hard-coding g.closes; asserts action/conviction/suggestedWeight/stop/target equal to 1e-6; non-nil stop/target guard prevents vacuous pass. Two negative controls: volume (thin last-3-bars vs full volumes flips volumeConfirmation, conviction differs) and benchmark (with vs without benchmark differs by RS term). (2) TASK 1b — honest DSR: added `StockSageStrategyBacktest.estimatedStrategyTrials = 12` (documented lower-bound estimate, ~8-12 iterations × a few configs; comment states it can only raise the bar, never flip an unproven edge); added `deflatedSharpe: StockSageDeflatedSharpe.Result?` field to `StrategyBacktest` (defaulted nil, Equatable); wired the first production DSR call in `aggregate(...)` using `estimatedStrategyTrials` as the trials input and variance of per-symbol Sharpes as varTrialSharpe; strengthened `caveat` to state survivorship-bias is a CEILING on measured Sharpe, and that DSR discounts for ~12 estimated strategy variants tried.
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageBacktestParityTests.swift` (new), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStrategyBacktest.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ Full test suite green (** TEST SUCCEEDED **). All 3 new parity tests pass. All existing StockSageMathInvariantTests golden vectors byte-identical. DSR trials constant = 12.

## 2026-06-27 — iter6: net-of-cost EV/day velocity sort + min-net-EV/day floor (Chat A)
**What:** Replaced the binary `clearsCostAfterFrictions` pass/fail gate in `velocityRankKey` with a continuous net/gross EV ratio haircut plus a min-net-EV/day floor. Fixes two gaps: (1) cost was binary (ideas barely clearing the gate kept full gross velocity); (2) no per-day floor existed — barely-positive-gross churny ideas surfaced as buys. Changes: (a) Added `minNetEVPerDayFloor = 0.005` (named constant, justified in docstring — Barber&Odean 7.1pp/yr churn context; conservative; single retune point). (b) Added `netEVR(for:calibration:)` — reuses `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate(...).netExpectancyR` with `defaultCosts(forSymbol:)`, same win-prob as displayed EV. (c) Added `netVelocity(for:holds:calibration:)` = netEVR / hold; nil-guarded for hold→0 and no-R ideas. (d) Added `belowNetCostFloor(for:holds:calibration:)` — strict < so exactly-at-floor passes. (e) Added `NetCostFloorFlag` enum (mirrors `EarningsRankFlag` pattern) with `.belowFloor(netVelocity:)` / `.clears`, `isDeranked`, `badge`. (f) Added `netCostFloorFlag(for:holds:calibration:)`. (g) `velocityRankKey` body replaced: gross log-growth × netRatio (= max(0, netEVR/evR), ratio 1 when no net data ⇒ Guardrail 4 byte-identity when cost=0) / hold = NET per-day log-growth; floor branch replaces the old `clearsCostAfterFrictions` branch (the old binary gate is subsumed — net≤0 ⇒ ratio=0 ⇒ key=0 which is < 0.005 floor, so the −500_000 de-rank still fires for every previously-demoted idea). MarketsView: "below net-cost floor" caption added to fastLaneStrip rows and velocity idea card rows, gated on `netCostFloorFlag(...).isDeranked`. Tests added: G1 (`G1_churnyShortHold_dropsBelowSlowerHighNet_afterCost`) — BTC churn vs AAPL slow swing: net board ranks AAPL first; gross velocity still ranks BTC faster; G2 (`G2_floorSkipsBarelyPositiveNet_andDoesNotHideClean`) — net-negative BTC idea fires floor; clean AAPL does not; G3 (`G3_degenerateAndBoundaryGuards`) — no-R idea gives nil/false/empty, index gives nil, floor at-boundary passes; G4 (`G4_netEqualsGrossWhenZeroCost`) — cost=0 byte-identity; math-invariant MARK:8 pins: `iter6_netEqualsGrossWhenZeroCost`, `iter6_floorConstantAndBoundarySemantics`, `iter6_netEVRClosedFormPins` with hand-derived closed-form arithmetic. `advise()`/`ev()`/displayed velocity untouched; iter3/iter4/iter5 golden vectors byte-identical.
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMathInvariantTests.swift`.
**Result:** ✅ typecheck clean (Swift 6, MainActor default isolation). Floor = 0.005R/day. Net-cost formula: `(p·netReward − (1−p)·netRisk)/grossRisk` via `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate(...).netExpectancyR`. Reused function: `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate(entry:stop:target:spreadBps:slippageBps:takerFeeBps:winProb:)` + `.netExpectancyR` field (L126-129 StockSageNetEdge.swift). Full test run not yet run per owner directive (typecheck only).

## 2026-06-28 — iter7 calibration chronology + ideas-progress reset (owner-directed follow-ups)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.
**What & why:** Two follow-ups from the merge-to-main deep review. (#1) `fit(fromJournal:)` now sorts journal trades by `closedAt` before projecting to `(conviction, won)`, so the positional train/test split in `selectCalibration` (test = most-recent slice) is a genuine "OOS = future" holdout, not merely disjoint — the journal store can return trades in insertion/edit order. Leak-free either way (sets were always disjoint); this pins the intended chronological semantics. (#2) `performRefreshIdeas()` clears `ideasProgress` via `defer { ideasProgress = nil }` right after it's set, so the tuple resets on EVERY exit (both `Task.isCancelled` early-returns + the feed-failure early-return), not only success; the redundant success-path reset was removed. (#3 — remove vestigial `volumes` param — DECLINED by owner: deliberately retained as the live/backtest call-site parity contract + the `negativeControl_volumeInputIsInert` sentinel that goes RED if a volume term is re-added.)
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures). `advise()` scoring untouched; no golden-vector change. CI gate to follow before main.

## 2026-06-28 — full-app backlog campaign, batch 4: RANKING_BACKLOG.md #4 + #8 (liquidity-gate + concentration haircut)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsIdeasSection.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/TodayView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`.
**What & why:** Re-verified all 12 originally-open RANKING_BACKLOG items against current source first — found #2 (regime-gate), #5 (negative-EV natural sort via signed rank keys), #6 (earnings-demotion) already shipped since the doc's 06-22 date, and #3 (calibrate winProbEstimate against journal) superseded by iter7's OOS-validated calibration candidate-selector (a stronger mechanism than the doc's naive conviction-bucket binning). Implemented the 2 highest-value genuinely-open items: **#4 liquidity-gate** — new `liquidityRankPenalty(for:liquidity:)` (mirrors `earningsRankPenalty`, 3000-point demotion for `.thin` tier, sits above the earnings band since execution risk beats event risk) threaded into `rankByEV`/`rankByVelocity`/`summary` as an additive rank-key penalty, and `bestOpportunity` gets a HARD exclusion (`liquidity[symbol]?.tier != .thin`) so a thin name can never be crowned #1 even as the sole positive-EV buy (stricter than earnings' demote-not-exclude, since a thin fill risk is unrelated to conviction). Wired `store.liquidity` through all 6 app call sites (MarketsView ×5, MarketsIdeasSection, TodayView) so the board, Ideas-section extraction, and Today tile stay consistent. Empty/missing liquidity data → 0 penalty, byte-identical to before (only-real-data). **#8 concentration-gates-sizing** — `expectedWeeklyR` now multiplies the summed velocity by 0.70 when `fastLaneConcentration(...).isConcentrated` (the top-N fast-lane setups are all one asset class), since summing correlated bets as independent overstated the weekly-R estimate; not-concentrated case is unaffected (factor 1.0). Added 4 new tests (thin-liquidity demotion + hard-bar on rankByEV/bestOpportunity/rankByVelocity, concentrated-vs-mixed weeklyR haircut).
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, all 4 new tests confirmed passing). #9 (stronger conditional weekly-R warning) assessed as already-adequately-addressed by the existing always-on caveat — no change made to avoid redundant UI text. Remaining RANKING items (#7 ATR-based velocity penalty, #10 bestOpportunity-by-velocity option, #11 universe/missing-symbol validation, #12 multi-timeframe confluence into conviction, #13 incomplete-count surfacing) deferred — #12 in particular touches `advise()` itself and deserves the same isolated, flag-gated, fully-tested treatment as iter7 rather than being rushed.

**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCapitalAllocator.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageCapitalAllocatorTests.swift`, `ALLOC_BACKLOG.md`.

Implemented the genuinely-open #2: `StockSageCapitalAllocator.suggestAdd(idea:openTrades:holdings:candidateReturns:account:maxHeat:correlationThreshold:calibration:) -> AddSuggestion?` — "I have one new idea + an open book, how much do I add?" Checks the correlation-concentration gate FIRST (a correlated add is refused regardless of edge), then sizes at half-Kelly (the same `suggestedFraction` 20%-capped variant `allocate()` uses for consistency) further capped by remaining heat headroom, using the exact composition the backlog specified: `StockSageExpectedValue.ev`, `StockSageKelly.compute`, `StockSagePortfolioHeat.compute`, `StockSageClusterCheck.check`, `StockSagePositionSizer.size` — no new financial math, pure composition of already-tested engines.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, all 4 new tests confirmed: empty-book sizing, heat-headroom capping, correlation-block, nil/invalid-input guards). ## 2026-06-28 — FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md #8 (time-to-double, unit bug corrected) + audit
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCompoundingHorizon.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageCompoundingHorizonTests.swift` (new), `FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** Re-verified #1 (7-day crypto week) and #2 (asset-class ATR stop) already shipped exactly as specced. Implemented #8: `StockSageCompoundingHorizon.weeksToTarget(weeklyReturn:target:)` solves `target = (1+weeklyReturn)^weeks` for weeks. Caught and corrected a unit bug in the original proposal's formula (`ln(1 + weeklyR/52)`) — dividing an ALREADY-weekly rate by periodsPerYear=52 a second time silently shrinks it 52×, the same class of bug ALLOC_BACKLOG's own notes warned about (`halfKelly/account`). The corrected version takes a genuine fractional weekly return and compounds once per week — dimensionally consistent, hand/python-verified. Enforces the doc's own honesty requirement as a TEST, not just a comment: `caveat` must literally contain "HYPOTHETICAL" and "not a forecast."
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, all 5 new tests confirmed). Engine-only — UI wiring (a row under moneyVelocityCard) deferred so the mandatory HYPOTHETICAL labeling gets its own careful pass. FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md reconciled: #1/#2/#8 done, #3/#4/#5/#6/#7 remain open (all substantial new UI surfaces, deferred to a dedicated UI-focused session).

## 2026-06-28 — TEST_BACKLOG.md: 37 boundary/edge-case tests across 16 files
**Files:** 16 `Salehman AITests/*.swift` files (Backtester, Indicators, StrategyBacktest, PortfolioHeat, NetEdge, ExpectedValue, RiskParity, Allocation, Kelly, Regime, Advisor, Currency, AlertDecision, Rebalance, PartialLadder, Journal) + `TEST_BACKLOG.md`.

Result: 35/37 confirmed exactly as drafted, 2 corrected (a stale raw-integer-ramp `advise()` fixture that would have hit the wrong scoring bucket, swapped for the test file's own `TrendFixtures.up()` helper; a floating-point boundary literal that wasn't bit-exact in binary64, corrected to a value that lands exactly). **Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, all 37 new + 1 fixed-in-place test confirmed passing). TEST_BACKLOG.md fully resolved for the 37 in-scope specs; the 6 GEFlip/OSRS items marked explicitly out-of-scope per the owner's markets-tab-only directive.

## 2026-07-01 — ALLOC_BACKLOG.md #5: rebalanceToEdge (EV-weighted whole-book reweight)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCapitalAllocator.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageCapitalAllocatorTests.swift`.
**What & why:** Re-verified #5 against current source before touching anything — confirmed genuinely open (no `rebalanceToEdge` anywhere in the tree; #4's doc-proposed `rebalanceByRiskParity` function was likewise never built as such, that composition is inlined directly in `MarketsRiskAllocationSection` instead). Implemented `StockSageCapitalAllocator.rebalanceToEdge(holdings:ideas:band:maxHeat:correlationThreshold:calibration:) -> EdgeRebalancePlan?` — reweights the book toward each symbol's positive, buy-family `StockSageExpectedValue.ev(for:).evR` (a held name with no current positive-EV idea is simply absent from `targets`, which the REUSED, unmodified `StockSageRebalance.plan(holdings:targets:band:)` already treats as "trim to 0" — no new drift/band math, per the owner's explicit instruction to reuse that exact logic). A brand-new (not currently held) idea is correlation-gated against held names' return series (derived from each idea's own `spark`, mirroring `allocate`'s cluster de-weighting) via the existing `StockSageClusterCheck` — a match ≥ `correlationThreshold` excludes it outright; no return series supplied silently no-ops the gate (size-only), matching `ClusterCheck.check`'s own nil behavior. Corrected a real ambiguity in the backlog's doc signature: since `Rebalance.plan` always renormalizes whatever `targets` it's given up to 100% (no "hold cash" residual concept exists in that model), a `maxHeat` cap on newly-entering ideas' combined weight is only mathematically enforceable when there's an existing held-edge pool to absorb the rest — derived a closed-form single-pass scale factor for that case (`scale = maxHeat/(1−maxHeat) · Σheld/Σnew`) rather than an ad hoc/iterative one, and documented why the cap is skipped when nothing fundable is currently held. Returns a new `EdgeRebalancePlan{plan, excludedSymbols, note}` (not the doc's bare `RebalancePlan?`) so the correlation-exclusion transparency the backlog's own testIdea asked for ("excluded ... with a note") has somewhere real to live, consistent with this file's existing pattern of wrapping composed results in a purpose-built `Sendable, Equatable` struct.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures; all 8 new tests confirmed: churn-cap engaging exactly at the closed-form 90/10 split (python-verified), cap correctly not engaging when the new share is already under it, isBalanced on a sole at-target holding, correlation-blocked new idea excluded with a note, size-only fallback when no return series is supplied, a held symbol with NO idea at all (not merely negative-EV) still trimmed, nil on zero positive edge anywhere, nil on nothing invested). Engine + tests only, no UI wiring this pass (mirrors how #2's `suggestAdd` shipped) — a future pass can compose it into `MarketsRiskAllocationSection` alongside the existing risk-parity rebalance panel. `#6` (Sharpe QP optimizer) remains deferred, unchanged — the doc itself frames it as a lowest-priority "stretch." ALLOC_BACKLOG.md is now fully reconciled: #1/#3/#4 already-shipped, #2/#5 implemented+tested, #6 explicitly deferred.

## 2026-07-01 — FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md #4: Today's ranked action list (top-3 by velocity, sized + gated)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift` (new), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift` (new).
**What & why:** Re-verified #4 against current source before touching anything — `fastLaneStrip` already showed the top-3-by-velocity list, but only the velocity number + badges, not the entry/stop/target/size/gate verdict the backlog asks for, so this is additive to (not a rebuild of) that surface. Deliberately DIDN'T use `StockSageCapitalAllocator.suggestAdd` for the "size + gate" despite it looking like a fit (`approved`/`riskFraction`/`reason` read like a verdict) — traced its actual signature and found it requires `openTrades`/`holdings` (live-book heat state) and computes `headroom = maxHeat − heatBefore` against ONE shared book snapshot; calling it independently per row for a top-3 list would let each row claim the SAME remaining heat headroom, double-counting size across the 3 recommendations if the owner took more than one. Used the backlog's own originally-specified composition instead — `StockSageExpectedValue.fastLane` for order, `.velocity` for the number, `StockSagePositionSizer.size` for shares, `StockSageTradeGate.evaluate` for the verdict — the same stateless per-idea pattern `StockSageTodayPlan.build` (already shipped) uses, so three independent rows can't silently overstate combined size. Added `StockSageTodayPlan.rankedActions(_:account:riskFraction:holds:calibration:earnings:max:) -> [TodayActionPlan]` (re-guards fastLane's own stop/target-defined invariant rather than assuming it) + `copyAllText(_:isSample:)` for a "Copy all 3" clipboard export, a new `TodayActionPlan` struct, and `MarketsTodayActionsCard` (new SwiftUI view) rendering rank/symbol/velocity/entry-stop-target/size/gate-badge per row, strikethrough + "DO NOT TRADE" on a blocked gate. Wired into `MarketsView.ideasSection` directly below `fastLaneStrip` (adjacent, not merged — flagged as a future consolidation candidate, not done here to keep this change tightly scoped).
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures; all 10 new tests confirmed: order matches `fastLane` exactly capped at `max`, shares/dollarsAtRisk nil iff no account, gate verdict byte-equal to a fresh `TradeGate.evaluate` call on the same recomputed net R:R, an over-cap risk% (5% > the gate's 2% default) deterministically blocks every row and both `gate.decision` and the copy text flag it "DO NOT TRADE", copy text contains each symbol/velocity/stop and the "estimate"/"per-trade risk cap" caveats, crypto suffix shown upfront as "SYMBOL (24/7 crypto)", `max` correctly caps below 3, empty input → empty list).

## 2026-07-01 — FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md #5 (engine half): momentum-quality fast-lane re-rank
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageMomentumQualityTests.swift` (new).
**What & why:** Re-verified #5 against current source before touching anything — no `momentumQuality`/`rankByVelocityWeighted` existed anywhere. Added `StockSageExpectedValue.momentumQuality(for:closes:) -> Double` (0–1) and `rankByVelocityWeighted(_:closes:holds:calibration:) -> [StockSageIdea]`, both purely additive — `fastLane`'s signature, membership, and ordering are untouched; the new function only re-sorts the SAME idea set `fastLane` already returns. Corrected one piece of the backlog's own wording, disclosed rather than silently applied: the spec proposed "RSI in the trend zone" as a hot/cold leg, but a bounded RSI band is a weak discriminator for exactly the failure mode this function exists to catch (a mean-reverting oscillator can sit in a bullish RSI band while still chopping) — substituted Kaufman's efficiency ratio (`StockSageIndicators.efficiencyRatio`, ≥0.35), alongside MACD histogram>0 and a positive ~21-bar return, still "no new math" (all three already exist in `StockSageIndicators`). Caught a real correctness trap while designing `rankByVelocityWeighted`: multiplying `fastLane`'s own internal ranking key (log-growth at half-Kelly, net-cost-scaled) by a 0–1 quality factor would be unsafe, because that key can be driven NEGATIVE by its own low-conviction/net-cost-floor de-rank penalties — multiplying a negative key by a smaller quality factor perversely REWARDS a demoted, cold setup by pulling it toward zero. Used the RAW `velocity(for:)` (EV/day, always > 0 for anything `fastLane` already kept) instead, so the multiply can only ever shrink a rank, never invert it — the same "can only shrink" discipline `cryptoRiskScaler` already uses in this file. `closes` is keyed by symbol (`[String: [Double]]`), not threaded through `StockSageIdea`, because `StockSageIdea.spark` is a down-sampled (~2 raw days/point, ≤32-point) series — short of `macd`'s 35-bar minimum — so feeding it into `momentumQuality` would silently produce a degraded number; a caller must supply real daily closes. A missing/empty `closes` entry for a symbol (or an entirely empty map — the default) scores that idea's quality at the neutral 1.0, so `rankByVelocityWeighted(ideas)` with no history reproduces `fastLane`'s own idea set stable-sorted by plain velocity, unchanged.
**UI (deferred, per FASTMONEY_BACKLOG's own effort/scope split):** the "reversal caveat" copy and a per-idea momentum dot belong in `fastLaneStrip` (`Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` lines ~3212–3238, the `earnFlag`/`floorFlag` badge row) and would follow the exact existing warning-caveat pattern (`fastLaneConcentration`'s `Text(...).foregroundStyle(DS.Palette.warningSoft)` block at lines 3250–3254). Not wired this pass: doing it honestly requires real daily closes per idea, which — per the `spark` limitation above — means computing `momentumQuality` once in `StockSageStore.buildIdeas()` (where the raw `history.closes` this store already discards after building is available, the same place `returnShape`/`volStability`/`volRegime`'s "Why" flags are computed today) and threading a new optional field onto `StockSageIdea`. That's a small, separate, backward-compatible follow-up (an additive nil-default field, not a behavior change) rather than something to bundle into this diff.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, negative-control RS sentinel unaffected; 8 new tests confirmed: strong/accelerating-uptrend fixture scores 1.0 hand-verified against the actual efficiencyRatio/macd/returnOverPeriod formulas — not assumed from the doc — a phase-shifted sine "chop" fixture scores 0.0, too-short/empty history scores the neutral 1.0 sentinel, an empty `closes` map reproduces plain-velocity order exactly, two equal-velocity ideas ordered COLD-then-HOT by input flip to HOT-then-COLD once their real histories are supplied, a sibling idea's history never demotes an idea missing its own, and a sell-side/negative-EV idea supplied a red-hot history is never resurrected into `fastLane`'s excluded set). FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md #5 engine half done; #3/#6/#7 remain deferred (substantial new UI surfaces).

The live Ideas tab has always rendered from `MarketsView.ideasSection`/`ideasHeader` (`Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`), which independently defines the same `IdeaSort`/`IdeaFilter` cases and `displayedIdeas` sort/filter/search logic that `MarketsIdeasSection` duplicated — two sources of truth for one board, only one of which rendered. ## 2026-07-01 — RANKING_BACKLOG #12: three-timeframe confluence, shipped via the safe CONFLUENCE.md route (owner away, autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageIndicators.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageIndicatorsTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`, `RANKING_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** #12 was deferred earlier today (2026-07-01) because its literal spec (fold multi-timeframe alignment directly into `score`, ±0.15/−0.10, OUTSIDE the 0.65 `trendFamilyCap`) was independently re-audited and rejected — it would double-count the same trend factor the ablated `relativeStrengthEnabled` term already measured, with no backtest to justify it. That audit surfaced `CONFLUENCE.md` (an unshipped 2026-06-22 design doc, never cross-referenced by RANKING_BACKLOG.md) that solves the SAME stated goal ("confluent setups outrank single-signal ones") via a structurally safer route: pure post-hoc OBSERVER fields that never feed back into `score`/conviction/sizing. Implemented `CONFLUENCE.md` item #4 (three-timeframe confluence), re-derived against CURRENT source since the doc predates the 2026-06-27 ablation (`relativeStrengthEnabled` gate, the removed ±0.05 volume term, and the iter3 variance-scalar/trend-family-cap rescaling all postdate the doc): `StockSageIndicators.timeframeConfluence(closes:dailyDirection:shortPeriod:neutralBandPct:)` reads LONG (`trendOK`, the existing 12-1/252-bar TSMOM gate), DAILY (the sign of `advise()`'s own FINAL resolved score — read only, never re-derived), and SHORT (`returnOverPeriod(closes,21)` with a 1% neutral band) — `aligned` only when all three are defined and same-signed; nil (not false) when the long leg is unknown (<253 bars). Wired as two trailing-defaulted `TradeAdvice` fields (`timeframeAligned`, `confluenceNote`), computed in `advise()` right after `conviction` and appended to `rationale` only when aligned; every existing scored/sized/stopped output is untouched (verified: the file's own pinned `TrendFixtures.up(250)` assertions — `.strongBuy`, conviction, `maxWeight` cap — hold byte-for-byte). **Caught a real bug while wiring:** `StockSageStore.buildIdeas`'s two `TradeAdvice` reconstruction sites (the calibration re-sizing branch and the extraNotes-append branch) did not carry the new fields through, which would have silently RESET `timeframeAligned`/`confluenceNote` to their defaults on every real app run (the exact same class of bug `stopMultiplier`/`stopReason` were already correctly protected against) — fixed both call sites. Surfaced as a "3-TF confluence" badge on `ideaCard` (MarketsView), matching the earnFlag/floorFlag badge convention — display-only. Deliberately did NOT wire this into any ranking comparator (`CONFLUENCE.md` item #5's tie-break) — that's a genuine ranking-behavior decision needing the same explicit owner sign-off already established for RANKING #10's `preferVelocity` opt-in default, not something to bundle in unsupervised. `CONFLUENCE.md` items #1-3 (confluence COUNT, bullMag/bearMag, conflicted flag) remain unshipped as narrower, separate follow-ups.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, 1332 tests; 11 new: 7 pure-function cases in `StockSageIndicatorsTests.swift` — python-verified against the actual `trendOK`/`returnOverPeriod` formulas before any Swift was written, including a corrected downtrend fixture since the repo's own `TrendFixtures.down(300)` crosses zero and corrupts % returns — plus 4 integration cases in `StockSageAdvisorTests.swift` including an explicit byte-identical regression check against the pinned uptrend fixture). [Corrected 2026-07-01: this entry originally miscounted 9 pure-function cases; the actual, still-accurate total was 7+4=11 — see the adversarial-review follow-up entry below.] RANKING_BACKLOG.md #12 marked done via the safe route.

## 2026-07-01 — HARDENING_BACKLOG #32: cross-sectional relative-strength ranking (tool only, deliberately unwired — owner away, autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageRelativeStrength.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageRelativeStrengthTests.swift` (new), `HARDENING_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** #32 was deferred earlier today because it's the same class of unvalidated "momentum predicts forward returns" premise as the ablated `relativeStrengthEnabled` term, just relocated from `advise()`'s score into the ranking layer — the backlog's own spec (±0.10 conviction nudge from a percentile rank) needs a dedicated backtest before any code lands. Checked whether that backtest is feasible today: `StockSageBacktester`/`StockSageStrategyBacktest` both operate on ONE symbol's history at a time — a genuine cross-sectional ablation (rank a UNIVERSE of symbols each period, compare outcomes) needs a walk-forward multi-symbol harness that doesn't exist in this repo yet, which is out of scope to build from scratch in this pass. Rather than fake a shortcut or leave a bare TODO, shipped the SAME pattern already proven this session for FASTMONEY #6 (`StockSageConvictionScaler`): the pure ranking utility, fully tested and correct in isolation, wired into NOTHING. `StockSageRelativeStrength.rank(_:[String:Double]) -> [RelativeStrengthRank]` takes a caller-supplied symbol→return map (no new fetch/indicator call) and returns percentile ranks 0 (weakest) to 1 (strongest). One correctness improvement beyond the original spec's own test list: ties are AVERAGE-RANKED (equal returns get the mean of the positions their tie would span) rather than ordered arbitrarily by Swift's Dictionary iteration order, which has no stability guarantee — an untested edge case that would otherwise have made this function's output non-deterministic across runs for any book with two identically-performing symbols. Single-holding input returns the NEUTRAL 0.5 (there's no peer to compare against — trivially calling a lone symbol "strongest" would be dishonest). The ±0.10 conviction nudge from the original spec is explicitly NOT implemented — that requires the backtest this item still needs.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, 1342 tests; 10 new, all python-verified before being written in Swift: percentile bounds/monotonicity on distinct returns, exact tie-averaging on a 2-way and a 3-way (all-tied) group, single-holding neutrality, empty-input safety, verbatim input-return preservation). HARDENING_BACKLOG.md #32 marked done for the tool; the ±0.10 nudge and any ranking/UI wiring remain explicitly future work pending a dedicated ablation study.

## 2026-07-01 — Adversarial review of the confluence engine + RS ranking utility; 2 confirmed bugs fixed (owner away, autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageIndicators.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageRelativeStrength.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageIndicatorsTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageRelativeStrengthTests.swift`, `RANKING_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** The confluence engine (RANKING #12) and relative-strength utility (HARDENING #32) shipped earlier today were written and reviewed solo — no second engineer looked at them before merge. Ran a 4-lens adversarial-review workflow (regression safety, confluence math, test quality, relative-strength correctness) followed by an independent skeptic pass on anything flagged, per the owner's standing ultracode directive. Two MODERATE, independently-reproduced bugs confirmed real:
1. **Bearish confluence showing a green badge.** `MarketsView`'s "3-TF confluence" badge was hardcoded `DS.Palette.successSoft` regardless of direction — a Sell/Reduce card with a bearish-aligned confluence (`confluenceNote` literally reading "...trends all down") would show a red Sell badge next to a green confluence badge, reading as a bullish endorsement on a card the engine says to sell. Fixed: badge tint now keys off `a.action` (danger for sell-family), matching `actionColor`'s own convention.
2. **Avoid-verdict cards could show a bullish badge.** `timeframeAligned`/`confluenceNote` were derived from raw `score`'s sign computed BEFORE the chop-regime block (which can downgrade a would-be Buy into `.avoid`, "no edge, stand aside"). The reviewer's skeptic independently REPRODUCED this with a live fixture (280-bar uptrend + a 20-bar RSI-overbought/choppy zigzag) that resolves to `.avoid` while raw score still reads "up" — so the card could show a green "3-TF confluence — trends all up" badge directly contradicting its own "stand aside" verdict, risking a user overriding a legitimate no-trade signal. Fixed: the confluence block now gates on `isBuy || isSell` (the POST-chop-downgrade action) — moved the pre-existing `isBuy`/`isSell` declarations earlier so the gate could reuse them, the exact same discipline the stop/target trade-plan gate a few lines below already used.
Also fixed two MINOR findings while in the area: (3) the confluence LONG leg (`trendOK(closes)`) silently dropped `CONFLUENCE.md`'s reviewed `AND price>sma200` conjunct — since 12-1 TSMOM structurally skips the most recent ~21 days, a symbol that broke below its 200DMA this month could still read "long-term bullish." Now requires `trendOK(closes) == (price > sma200)`; a disagreement returns nil ("unknown"), never a guessed side. (4) `StockSageRelativeStrength.rank` (still unwired, so non-blocking, but cheap to fix now) now drops non-finite (NaN/±infinity) inputs before sorting — IEEE-754 NaN comparisons are neither `<` nor `==`, which would otherwise silently corrupt the tie-detection for every OTHER symbol in the same call. Also corrected a documentation inaccuracy from the earlier entry (miscounted "9 pure-function cases" when it was actually 7; the 11-test total was correct).
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, 1349 tests; 8 new: 3 pure-function confluence cases — a genuine long-vs-short disagreement, the new TSMOM-vs-200DMA disagreement→nil case, plus 1 renamed for accuracy — 2 integration cases reproducing and regression-guarding the avoid+bullish-badge bug, and 3 relative-strength non-finite-input hardening cases). All 6 review lenses' findings addressed: 2 confirmed-real bugs fixed, 2 minor findings fixed as cheap hardening, 2 nitpicks (test naming, RS tie-comparison epsilon while still unwired) resolved or explicitly deferred as genuinely non-urgent.

## 2026-07-01 — Adversarial review of the FULL day's engine additions (RANKING #10, ALLOC #5, HARDENING #31/#33, FASTMONEY #3/#5); 1 critical + 5 moderate bugs fixed (owner away, autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageCapitalAllocator.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePyramid.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageSectorRotation.swift`, plus their test files, `ALLOC_BACKLOG.md`, `HARDENING_BACKLOG.md`, `FASTMONEY_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** Extended the same adversarial-review discipline just applied to the confluence/RS work (above) to the REST of today's engine additions — ALLOC #5 (`rebalanceToEdge`), HARDENING #31/#33 (`StockSageSectorRotation`/`StockSagePyramid`), and FASTMONEY #3/#5 (`dailyVariancePct`/`rankByVelocityWeighted`) — which had only been build+test verified and manually read-through-audited, never independently bug-hunted. A 5-module review (13 agents + skeptic verification) found 1 critical and 5 confirmed-real moderate bugs:
1. **[CRITICAL] `rankByVelocityWeighted` could resurrect a demoted "junk" or cost-negative idea to #1.** It weighted the RAW `velocity(for:)` instead of `fastLane`'s own demotion-aware `velocityRankKey` — a sub-`minConvictionToRank` idea (key −1000) or a below-net-cost-floor idea (key −500,000) is correctly buried by `fastLane` itself, but raw velocity ignores those penalties entirely, so a hot momentum history could put a demoted idea back on top — directly contradicting the codebase's own documented invariant ("nothing previously demoted is resurrected") and `fastLane`'s real order. Not yet wired to any View, but shipped as tested, documented, ready-to-wire API. Fixed: weight `velocityRankKey` itself, and ONLY when it's already positive — a demoted idea's negative key now passes through completely unchanged.
2. **[MODERATE, LIVE] `dailyVariancePct` understated the crypto "typical 24h range" badge by ~17%.** Divided by √365 on the premise that `idea.realizedVol` is computed on a 365-day crypto basis — it never was; every real call site uses the 252-day default, crypto included. Fixed to divide by √252, matching the actual input basis; this is user-facing on the live `bestOpportunityCTA` card today.
3. **[MODERATE, LIVE] `StockSageSectorRotation.isRotatingIn` didn't check `avgR > 0`.** A sector that's merely the least-bad among eligible sectors (rank #1 with a genuinely negative avgR) could still be labeled "rotating in," reading as a positive confirmation it isn't. Fixed: now requires `avgR > 0`. Also fixed a separate, real determinism bug found in the same file: sectors were sorted via a Swift `Dictionary`, whose iteration order has no stability guarantee — an exact avgR tie could rank differently across runs; fixed with an alphabetical tie-break.
4. **[MODERATE] `StockSagePyramid.levels` accepted infinite inputs and produced NaN.** The guard checked only positivity (`Double.infinity > 0` is true), so an infinite entry/stop/fraction/cap poisoned every tier field. Fixed with `.isFinite` guards plus a ceiling on `initialFraction` (never legitimately >100% of the account) closing a secondary huge-but-finite overflow path.
5. **[MODERATE] `rebalanceToEdge`'s correlation gate only checked new-vs-held, never new-vs-new.** Two or three brand-new, mutually-correlated ideas (e.g. two correlated crypto names into a near-empty book) could pass completely unchecked against each other. Fixed: a clique within the new-idea pool itself is now de-weighted via `StockSageCorrelationCluster.correlationAdjustedWeights`, mirroring `allocate`'s own Step 2.5.
6. **[MODERATE] `rebalanceToEdge` had no per-position concentration cap.** Unlike every other sizing tool in this engine (`StockSageAdvisor.maxWeight`, `allocate`'s Kelly cap, `StockSagePyramid`'s `riskCap`), a sole or dominant positive-EV name could be recommended at up to 100% of the book — 3 of the function's own shipped tests demonstrated this as "correct." Fixed with real care for a subtlety the fix itself surfaced: because `StockSageRebalance.plan` always renormalizes whatever `targets` it receives back to summing 1 (no cash residual), a NAIVE cap-and-redistribute is silently UNDONE whenever there's nowhere to redistribute the excess into (e.g. a sole funded name — dividing one capped value by itself is always 1.0 again). The fix detects this specific case and, rather than pretend a cap that can't hold, leaves the plan at its honest concentrated weights with a loud "⚠ CONCENTRATION WARNING" note. When enough funded names DO exist (verified: needs at least `⌈1/cap⌉` = 5 names for a 20% cap to be mathematically achievable at all), the cap genuinely works and is applied.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures; 15 new tests across the 6 fixes, all python-verified — including the exact 4/5/6-funded-name boundary math for the concentration cap — before being written in Swift). Also fixed 2 minor doc/determinism nitpicks flagged by the same review (a stale "<22 bars" threshold in `momentumQuality`'s doc comment, corrected to the actual <21-bar boundary). Every confirmed-real finding from both of today's adversarial reviews (this entry + the confluence/RS one above) is now fixed and tested — nothing outstanding.

## 2026-07-02 · PROJECT_CONTEXT.md staleness fix — brain-layer sections rewritten for the 2026-06-18 local-only reality (Chat B lane, docs-only)
**Files:** `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` (§1 "What this app is", §2 `LLM/` file table + 3 adjacent stale rows, §3 "The brain system", §4 "Cloud providers" → "Local inference endpoints").
**What & why:** `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` — the doc that's explicitly supposed to be "everything you need if you were handed this file" — still described the app as it was BEFORE the 2026-06-18 owner-directed full cloud-provider removal (COORDINATION.md top banner + line 72: "delete anthropic, grok, gemini, groq, mistral, cerebras, openAI, copilot, openRouter … go local-only"): §1 called it "cloud-first NVIDIA-hosted DeepSeek → free frontier tiers" and listed all 9 dead cloud providers as active brains; §2's `LLM/` table still listed 8 now-deleted files (`AnthropicClient`/`AnthropicBrainAdapter`/`GrokClient`/`GeminiClient`/`OpenAIClient`/`CopilotClient`/`CloudBrains`/`Views/CopilotSignInView`) plus a stale `LiveTranscriptionView.swift` row still naming the deleted Copilot sheet; §3 described a 20-case `BrainPreference` with `generateFreeAuto`/`generateEnsemble` special modes that no longer exist; §4 was an entire "Cloud providers" table for clients that don't exist anymore. Verified against CURRENT source (not the 06-18 note verbatim) before rewriting: `Salehman AI/LLM/*.swift` (confirmed only `BrainAdapter`/`BrainRouting`/`BrainStatus`/`KeychainStore`/`LocalLLM`/`MLXSalehmanEngine`/`MemoryManager`/`OllamaBrainAdapter`/`OllamaClient`/`OpenAICompatibleClient`/`SalehmanEngine`/`SalehmanLeader`/`SalehmanPersona`/`UnslothStudio`/`VLLM` remain), `AppSettings.BrainPreference` (6 cases: `.auto`/`.ollama`/`.salehman`/`.unslothStudio`/`.vllm`/`.uncensored`), `BrainRouting.swift`/`LocalLLM.swift` (`generateEnsemble`/`generateFreeAuto` confirmed gone, `CloudProvider` enum confirmed gone), `SalehmanEngine.swift` (confirmed the `.salehman` brain's real resolution order is MLX → Ollama ONLY — no vLLM/UnslothStudio hop, despite `AppSettings`/`SettingsView`'s own subtitle copy still claiming a 4-stage "vLLM → Unsloth Studio → MLX → Ollama" chain; PROJECT_CONTEXT now documents the code's ACTUAL behavior, not the UI's aspirational copy — flagged separately, see below). Also checked `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md` for anything after 06-18 that further moved the brain layer: found one real addition, the 2026-06-22 configurable `AppSettings.ollamaServerURL` (remote-GPU Ollama), now reflected in the `OllamaClient.swift` row and the §4 table; no reversion of the local-only migration was found — every dev-log entry after 06-18 is Markets/StockSage (Chat A lane), confirming the removal held.
**Result:** Docs-only change — no Swift file touched, so no build/test run per the task's own stated exception (only required if `Salehman AI/LLM/*` itself changed). Verified by grep/read against current source, not assumption. Flagged two adjacent, out-of-scope findings via `spawn_task` for separate sessions rather than fixing unilaterally: (1) `AppSettings.BrainPreference.salehman`'s doc comment + `SettingsView`'s "Salehman engine" section subtitle both still claim a vLLM/Unsloth-Studio hop in `.salehman`'s resolution order that `SalehmanEngine.generate()` doesn't actually implement (UI copy oversells real behavior); (2) `KeychainStore.Account.nvidiaAPIKey` is dead code — the NVIDIA cloud client it served was deleted 2026-06-18 and nothing in the app reads that Keychain entry anymore.

## 2026-07-01 — CONFLUENCE.md #5: preferConfluence tie-break wiring (owner "do everything", autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `CONFLUENCE.md`.
**What & why:** With both today's adversarial reviews clean, picked up the remaining safe items. `CONFLUENCE.md` item #5 (the ranking-tie-break payoff for item #4's `timeframeAligned` field, shipped earlier today) was still unwired. Added `bestOpportunity(..., preferConfluence: Bool = false)` — within the SAME near-tie band `preferVelocity` already uses, prefers the `timeframeAligned` idea over one that isn't; composes with `preferVelocity` (conviction tie-break fires first when both flags are on, confluence only breaks what conviction couldn't). Default `false`, byte-identical for every existing caller — same discipline as `preferVelocity`; flipping either default in `MarketsView` remains an explicit product decision needing owner sign-off. `CONFLUENCE.md` items #1-3 (confluence count, bullMag/bearMag, conflicted flag) were never shipped, so the "mixed signals" badge / confluence-count label from #5's original spec don't apply — only the alignment tie-break, matching what actually exists.
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, 1364 tests; 4 new: default-ignores-confluence regression, tie-break-toward-aligned (order-independent), never-overrides-a-clear-winner, and composes-with-conviction-tie-break-first).

## 2026-07-01 — ALLOC_BACKLOG #6: Sharpe-max de-correlated allocator, the first numerical engine in this codebase (owner "do everything", autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAllocationOptimizer.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageAllocationOptimizerTests.swift` (new), `ALLOC_BACKLOG.md`.
**What & why:** The 2026-07-01 re-audit deferred #6 because the doc's literal spec was unbuildable (no volatility input for Sigma, no risk-aversion parameter) and would be the first non-closed-form numerical engine in the codebase — a materially higher verification bar. With the owner's "do everything" directive, re-spec'd and implemented it properly rather than leaving it deferred indefinitely.
- **Re-spec:** `ideas: [StockSageIdea]` (matching `allocate()`'s convention) instead of the doc's raw tuple; volatility derived from each idea's own `spark` (`annualizedVolatility(spark) / stopDistancePct`, converting price vol into R-multiple vol — a tighter stop makes the SAME price move a larger number of R's); correlations from the already-existing `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.correlationMatrix`. `lambda` (risk-aversion) added with a DERIVED, not invented, default: for a single asset this objective's stationarity condition reduces to `w*=mu/(2·lambda·sigma²)`, and `lambda=1.0` reproduces exactly today's half-Kelly fraction — anchoring the optimizer's default risk tolerance to the SAME appetite the rest of the app already ships with.
- **Solver: Frank-Wolfe, not projected gradient.** Prototyped BOTH in Python before writing a line of Swift. Projected gradient with a textbook-correct Lipschitz step size reliably FAILED on this problem's specific geometry: a tight heat cap against a much larger unconstrained optimum means a single "correct" gradient step overshoots every coordinate's box bound simultaneously, and the box-then-sum-cap projection collapses to a trivial EQUAL split regardless of the true mu/Sigma — worse, that trivial split is a spurious fixed point of the overshoot-then-collapse update, so the solver falsely reports "converged" on iteration 1. Frank-Wolfe sidesteps the failure mode structurally: every iterate is a convex combination of two already-feasible points, never projected, so it cannot produce a degenerate answer this way. Verified against three hand-worked scenarios (single dominant-mu idea correctly takes the whole budget; two correlated ideas each lose weight to a same-mu independent third; a fully symmetric independent case lands at the exact equal split) before porting to Swift.
- **Deliberately not modeled:** the doc's proposed hard pairwise "decorrelation cap" — Sigma's quadratic penalty already delivers the intended de-correlation effect intrinsically (verified in testing: two perfectly-correlated equal-mu ideas are correctly treated as "one bet," and a genuinely independent equal-vol third idea gets MORE combined weight than the redundant pair). Adding a second, redundant hard constraint would only matter in edge cases and would break the linear oracle's simple closed form (greedily fund the highest-gradient coordinates up to their own cap until the budget runs out).
**Result:** ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full unit suite, isolated DD, 0 failures, 1376 tests; 15 new). One test's first draft caught a genuine FIXTURE bug during verification (not an algorithm bug): an "independent" comparison price series shared a systematic drift term with the correlated pair, measuring an accidental 0.94 correlation instead of the intended ~0. Diagnosed by reproducing the exact scenario in Python — both the prototype and the real Swift code agreed EXACTLY on the resulting allocation, which is what proved the fixture (not the code) was wrong. Fixed with a properly-verified low-correlation, volatility-matched oscillating series. ALLOC_BACKLOG.md is now fully resolved — #1/#3/#4 already-shipped, #2/#5/#6 implemented+tested.

## 2026-07-02 — Empirical ablation of the confluence tie-break (RANKING #12) and cross-sectional RS rank (HARDENING #32) (owner "continue", autonomous continuation)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-02_confluence_rs_ablation.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md`, `RANKING_BACKLOG.md`, `HARDENING_BACKLOG.md`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageRelativeStrength.swift` (doc comment only).
**What & why:** Both modules shipped this session carried an explicit "pending a dedicated ablation study before further wiring" caveat in their own doc comments. Earlier in the session I'd deferred attempting this, believing there was no real network access to fetch historical data — the owner's repeated "continue" prompted re-testing that assumption, and `curl` to Yahoo Finance's public chart endpoint (the same one `StockSageQuoteService` uses) succeeded. Built a real walk-forward ablation in Python (stdlib only, no numpy/scipy — t-distribution CDF and Spearman correlation implemented from scratch via the regularized incomplete beta function, verified against textbook critical values before use): 20 liquid US large-caps, 5 years of real daily OHLC, formulas ported EXACTLY from the live Swift source (`timeSeriesMomentum`/`trendOK`/`returnOverPeriod`/`StockSageRelativeStrength.rank`'s tie-averaged percentile), no look-ahead (signals computed only from `closes[0...i]`, entry/exit at next-bar OPEN matching `StockSageBacktester`'s own convention), block-level significance testing (Fama-MacBeth style, to respect cross-sectional correlation across symbols on the same day) on a primary non-overlapping sample plus a horizon sweep (5/10/21/42/63 trading days) for robustness.
**Result:** Neither signal shows a statistically significant forward-return edge in this sample — every comparison at every horizon came back p>0.10, with signs that flip across horizons/samples (consistent with noise). The RS point estimate is mildly negative and consistent with the well-known ≤1-month reversal anomaly, which is exactly why the app's own 12-1 TSMOM already uses `skipRecent: 21`. **No code change follows** — this closes the "pending ablation" caveat on both modules with an honest answer: RANKING #12's `preferConfluence` stays exactly as shipped (opt-in, narrow EV-tie-break only) and HARDENING #32's `StockSageRelativeStrength` stays exactly as shipped (fully unwired) — the ablation does not support promoting either. Build green (comment-only Swift edit, verified anyway). Full methodology, limitations, and per-horizon tables in `RESEARCH_2026-07-02_confluence_rs_ablation.md`; indexed in `research/INDEX.md`.

## 2026-07-02 — Full-engine adversarial review (68 files, 9 domains, 111 agents) + 7 critical bug fixes (owner "continue", ultracode on)
**Files:** `StockSagePortfolioHeat.swift`, `StockSageJournal.swift`, `StockSageRebalance.swift`, `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.swift`, `StockSageVolRegime.swift`, `StockSageNetEdge.swift`, `StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `StockSageStore.swift`, `MarketsView.swift` + matching test files.
**What & why:** With the backlog sweep and the confluence/RS ablation both closed, ran a comprehensive adversarial review of the FULL StockSage engine (69 files, minus the out-of-scope `StockSageGEFlip.swift`) via a 9-domain Workflow — the two earlier reviews this session only covered that day's new modules, never the foundational engine (advisor scoring, Kelly sizing, allocation, backtesting, data layer). Each domain agent did a read-only audit (explicit "do not write/edit/create files" instruction, learned necessary from this session's earlier finding that "propose only" framing alone isn't reliable); every finding then went through 3 independent skeptic agents voting to REFUTE it, majority-confirm required to survive. Result: 34 findings, 32 survived adversarial verification (2 correctly refuted — a VIX-regime finding where the skeptics traced an upstream guard in `StockSageQuoteService.parseChart` that already prevents the claimed scenario, and an AllocationOptimizer sigma-fallback finding refuted 2/3).
**7 CRITICAL fixes (crashes + live money-math corruption), each independently verified against current source before fixing:**
1. `StockSagePortfolioHeat.compute` — a fat-fingered "inf"/"nan" journal entry reached `verdict`'s `Int((heatPct*100).rounded())`, which traps on a non-finite Double (reproduced the exact Swift runtime crash). Fixed: non-finite trade legs excluded from the risk sum, not trusted.
2. `StockSageJournal.tradesToSignificance` — a near-zero-mean, high-variance R sample (e.g. one +1000R and one −1000R trade) pushes `ratio²` past `Int.max`, and `Int(Double)` traps on overflow (reproduced via `swift -e`). Fixed: bound-check before the conversion, return nil (same as the existing "never confirms" case) instead of crashing.
3. `StockSageRebalance.plan` — `Swift.max(0, ·)` is not a finiteness guard: `Swift.max(0, .nan) == 0` (silently zeroes a bad holding instead of excluding it) and `Swift.max(0, .infinity) == .infinity` (passes straight through), so one corrupted holding value turned `total` infinite and recommended an infinite-dollar "Buy" for every OTHER held symbol. Fixed: exclude non-finite holdings/targets from the sums instead of clamping them.
4. `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.compute` — the identical `Swift.max(0,·)` non-guard on holding weights let one `.infinity` weight zero every other holding's normalized weight and turn the poisoned one into NaN, cascading into a literal "Ann. return −100.0%" and a Sharpe that renders as the string "nan" instead of "n/a" (the file's own stated policy). Fixed: exclude non-finite weights; also fixed `sharpe`/`sortino`'s `annVol != 0` guards to `annVol.isFinite && annVol != 0` (NaN != 0 is TRUE under IEEE-754, so the old guard didn't catch it).
5. `StockSageVolRegime.regime` — the rolling-vol window used a half-open range (`closes[(i-volWindow)..<i]`) that never includes `closes[i]`, so `current` (the live sizing-multiplier input) was permanently lagged one trading day behind the latest close — a real vol shock on today's bar was invisible until the NEXT refresh. Fixed to a closed range matching the sibling `StockSageVolStability` module's own convention; added a test perturbing only the last close and asserting `current` moves.
6. `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate` — unlike its sibling `StockSageExpectedValue.ev()`, which explicitly caps reward:risk at 50:1 ("a hair-thin stop otherwise makes rewardR unbounded"), `evaluate()` had no analogous cap: a degenerate near-zero-risk stop inflated `netExpectancyR` ~20x past the properly-capped figure (486.5 vs. ev()'s capped 24.5 on the same setup, verified in Python) and collapsed `breakEvenWinRate` toward 0, making the net-cost gate (`clearsCost`) toothless for exactly the setups it exists to reject. Fixed: derive `netReward`/`netRR`/`netExpectancyR`/`breakEvenWinRate` from the same 50:1-capped gross reward; `grossRR` itself stays the true uncapped ratio for display.
7. `StockSageConvictionCalibration.fit(fromBacktest:)` — unlike its sibling `fit(fromJournal:)` (explicitly sorted by close date specifically so the OOS candidate-selector's positional train/test split is a genuine chronological holdout), `fit(fromBacktest:)` never sorted its input. The real caller, `StockSageStore.refreshStrategyBacktest`, builds `trades` by appending ALL of symbol 1's trades then ALL of symbol 2's, etc. — grouped by symbol, not globally time-ordered — so a positional 70/30 split could leak a later-dated trade of an earlier symbol into "train" while an earlier-dated trade of a later symbol sat in "test". Fixed: added an optional `dates: [Date] = []` param (default-preserving) that sorts trades chronologically first when 1:1-aligned with the caller's dates; updated the one real production call site to pass the already-computed `tradeDates` it had sitting right next to the old call, unused.
**Also fixed 3 same-file moderate/minor findings while touching each file for the critical fix** (`StockSageJournal.bySector`/`.holdingPeriod` dict-order tie-break + negative-duration clamp; `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.calmar` now nil instead of a fake 0.00 when maxDrawdown is 0, mirroring sharpe/sortino's own convention; `StockSageVolRegime`'s dead `assetClass` parameter removed).
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full suite, isolated DD) — 15 new regression tests, each reproducing the exact failure mode before confirming the fix. The remaining 25 confirmed findings (moderate/minor: dictionary-iteration-order tie-breaks, stale UAE currency-suffix mapping, frozen earnings-proximity cache, dead exclusion-reason tracking, a correlation primitive that returns a fake 0 for undefined zero-variance input, and others) are next, delegated to a grouped-by-file fix workflow rather than fixed inline here — see the follow-up entry.

## 2026-07-02 — 20 moderate/minor review findings fixed via 14-agent grouped-by-file workflow (owner "continue" + "use only fable"; all agents on Fable 5)
**Files:** 20 engine files + 17 test files + 1 new test file (`StockSageMonitorTests.swift`) — see the commit for the full list.
**What & why:** The remaining confirmed findings from the full-engine review, delegated to a fix workflow with agents grouped by DISJOINT file ownership (no two agents touching the same file — the lesson from this session's earlier concurrent-edit surprise), each instructed to implement the minimal default-preserving fix + a regression test that fails pre-fix, and to run build+test itself. All 14 groups reported green. Highlights:
- **Honesty/correctness:** `annualizedVolatility` no longer returns NaN for an interior bad tick (numerator guard added); `stopMultiple` treats non-finite vol as the neutral 2.0× default, not the tightest "calm" 1.5×; the momentum rationale no longer claims "6-month" on a 39-day window; `correlation()` now returns nil (not a fake "uncorrelated" 0) for zero-variance series, with all 4 consumers (`averageCorrelation`, `ClusterCheck`, `CorrelationPrecheck`, `laneCorrelation`) excluding undefined pairs instead of counting phantom zeros; `WhatIfImpact.note` no longer attributes one class's before-fraction to a different class when the top flips.
- **Live-data staleness:** earnings proximity now caches only the RAW date and derives daysUntil/severity FRESH on every read (was frozen at fetch time forever, silently under-warning as reports approached); `isNewListing` now survives the quote-cache round trip (old-format caches gracefully decode-fail to "no cache", never mis-read); the first-ever live refresh now gets the same low-coverage bail-out protection as later ones.
- **Feature completion:** `StockSageAlertDecision.evaluate` — tested, side-aware stop-breach/target-hit logic that had ZERO production callers since 2026-06-22 — is now wired into `StockSageMonitor` via a new `checkIdeaAlerts` pass (crossing detection via per-symbol prior-price tracking, so it structurally can't spam repeat pushes), and BOTH strong-signal push paths now respect the same staleness gate price alerts always had (a weekend/holiday close can no longer fire a non-actionable push, nor poison the dedup map and suppress the later legitimate one).
- **Robustness:** `PartialLadder.levels` finiteness guard (mirroring the Pyramid fix); `Portfolio.add` rejects inf/NaN before persisting; `TrailingStop.recompute`/`Backtester.trailLevels` no longer abort entirely when ATR isn't computable on the first post-entry bars (skip-until-warm instead — verified the backtester's `Swift.max(stop, trail[pi])` consumer resolves the pre-warm sentinel to the fixed stop); `GapRisk.scenario` rejects a stop on the wrong side of entry; `LossLimit`'s streak now bounded by `now` like the $/R totals beside it; deterministic tie-breaks in `Allocation.slices` and `Currency.breakdown`; `.AE` → AED suffix mapping fixed (DFM holdings were permanently excluded from the FX-converted USD total via a nonexistent `AEUSD=X` pair).
**Result:** Reviewed every diff myself after the workflow (including the QuoteCache Codable back-compat path, the Monitor's crossing semantics against `AlertDecision.evaluate`'s actual guards, and the `earnings` computed-property SwiftUI observation chain), then ran an INDEPENDENT full build+test on the combined tree per the standing rule (agents' own green claims were each pre-combination): ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** — 1420 tests, 0 failures (~44 new regression tests). One review finding was already fixed en route (AllocationOptimizer sigma — refuted 2/3 by skeptics anyway); the `DrawdownScenario.drawdownPct` naming finding was resolved as documentation (explicit "0–1, NOT ×100" doc comment) rather than a risky rename.

## 2026-07-02 — Markets UI adversarial review (11 sections, 257 agents) + 7 critical UI fixes (owner /loop autonomous; Fable orchestrates, Sonnet verifies per owner directive)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsRiskAllocationSection.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageInput.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageLeverage.swift` + `StockSageInputTests.swift`, `StockSageLeverageTests.swift`.
**What & why:** With the engine fully audited, ran the same find→3-skeptic-verify pattern over the Markets UI layer (4,459-line MarketsView + 3 satellite files) — the layer where engine truths get distorted. First pass hit the session rate limit mid-verification; resumed after the 6am reset with verifiers on Sonnet 5 (owner's new model split). 81/82 findings survived skeptic verification (7 critical / 34 moderate / 40 minor). The 7 criticals, fixed by hand:
1. **Fake green P&L from a silently-zeroed cost basis** — `portfolio.add(..., costBasis: Double(newCost) ?? 0)`: a broker-pasted "1,234.56" (comma), locale decimal, or blank Cost/sh parsed to 0, rendering the entire position value as profit. Fixed with `StockSageInput` validation (new `nonNegativeAmount` — typed 0 legal, defaulted 0 never) + the Add button now disabled until both shares AND cost parse.
2. **Int(Double) crash loop in `numString`** — `String(Int(d))` traps past Int.max; a pathological share count (one typed "1e19") persisted to UserDefaults crashed every subsequent Portfolio render. Fixed with `%.0f`.
3+7. **Fabricated rebalance trades in BOTH risk-parity panels** (MarketsView + MarketsRiskAllocationSection) — holdings and targets were built from different filters, so `plan()`'s `norm[s] ?? 0` rendered "Sell $<everything>" for holdings merely dropped from risk-sizing (exclusion ≠ liquidate) and phantom "Buys" for FX-unconvertible names already owned. Fixed by intersecting both sets before `plan()` + surfacing FX-excluded names in a caveat.
4. **Stale parity targets silently mixed with live holdings** — targets freeze at "Balance by risk" time but the plan recomputed per-render from current positions; any add/remove produced trades from two different books. Fixed: plan suppressed with a "holdings changed — refresh" notice whenever the live symbol set differs from the sized-at-refresh set.
5. **Short-side ideas armed instantly-dead price alerts** — all six idea-card alert sites hardcoded `.above` target/`.below` stop (long-side). A sell/reduce idea's target is BELOW and stop ABOVE, so both alerts were already met at creation: fired a meaningless push immediately, disarmed, and the REAL stop-breach never notified — a silent risk-control failure. Fixed: direction derived from the level's position vs the current price at all three call sites, labels now show the computed ≥/≤.
6. **Long-side liquidation price on leveraged shorts** — `StockSageLeverage.assess` is long-only (entry·(1−1/L)); the detail sheet showed a leveraged sell/reduce idea a "wipe-out" price on the side where a short PROFITS. Fixed: engine gained a default-preserving `isShort` (short liq = entry·(1+1/L), ABOVE entry; `canLoseMoreThanAccount` always true for shorts), panel derives the side once and threads it to both leverage and gap lines.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1422 tests, 0 failures; 2 new engine test funcs). The 74 confirmed moderate/minor findings are next — delegated to a Sonnet fix workflow (MarketsView agents sequential — same file; satellites parallel). Full findings: session scratchpad ui_criticals.json/ui_mod_min.json + workflow wf_61cf8278-331 output.

## 2026-07-02 — briefing-ideas-1 UI findings: 4 moderate/minor bugs fixed in MarketsView (subagent fix pass)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** Fixed the 4 verified findings in the "briefing-ideas-1" section of the adversarial UI review. Engine edits NOT allowed for this group.
1. **Conviction tooltip stale when calibration active** (moderate) — `ideaCard` conviction meter always displayed "Estimated win-rate range ~35-58%, not a forecast." even when `store.convictionCalibration` was fitted and the card's own EV numbers used calibrated (potentially outside 35–58%) win probabilities, directly contradicting the adjacent header's "measured from N realized trades" label. Fix: help text is now conditional on `store.convictionCalibration`; calibrated → "win-rate mapped from N realized trades", uncalibrated → original prior-band text.
2. **Calibrated-state tooltip misattributed to "backtest outcomes"** (minor) — the `ideasHeader` calibrated-state `.help` said "calibrated from realized backtest outcomes" while `convictionCalibration` is actually `fit(fromJournal:) ?? backtestConvictionCalibration` — journal fit takes precedence. Fix: reworded to "your journal when it has enough closed trades, else the sample backtest (conservative, monotonic)."
3. **Net-cost floor literal "0.005R/day" duplicated in UI** (minor) — the `↓ floor` badge `.help` hardcoded `0.005R/day` duplicating `StockSageExpectedValue.minNetEVPerDayFloor`. Fix: now interpolated via `String(format: "%.3fR/day", StockSageExpectedValue.minNetEVPerDayFloor)`.
4. **Generated briefing never invalidated** (minor) — `briefing` (@State) was never reset when `store.symbols` changed, so stale LLM text naming removed symbols persisted indefinitely with no timestamp. Fix: (a) added `briefingGeneratedAt: Date?` state stamped in `generateBriefing()`; (b) a "Generated <relative time>" caption appears below the generated text (amber ⚠︎ tint when >4h old, mirroring the ideas-board staleness treatment); (c) `.onChange(of: store.symbols.map(\.symbol).sorted().joined())` resets both `briefing` and `briefingGeneratedAt` so a symbol-list change immediately falls back to the live deterministic summary.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — idea-detail-sheet UI findings: 8 bugs fixed in MarketsView (subagent fix pass)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** Fixed all verified findings in the "idea-detail-sheet" section. Engine edits NOT allowed for this group.
1. **"Copy plan" pasted GROSS R:R with no gate verdict** (moderate) — the Copy plan button used `StockSageRewardRisk.assess` (pure gross), never surfacing the net R:R or the pre-trade gate verdict the sheet already displays. A "Don't take this trade" setup could copy as a clean strong-looking broker plan. Fix: after `StockSageTradePlan.text`, compute `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate` and `StockSageTradeGate.evaluate` (same arguments as the sheet's gate block, including netRR and the risk-fraction floor) and append the net R:R line plus the gate verdict + fail/warn labels before writing to the clipboard.
2. **Sub-dollar %.2f in scale-out ladder rungs** (moderate) — `String(format: "%.2f (+%.1fR)", $0.price, ...)` collapsed sub-dollar rung prices to "0.10, 0.10, 0.10" or "0.00". Fix: `adaptivePrice($0.price)` for the rung price, matching every other price display in the sheet.
3. **Sub-dollar %.2f in "Sizes the LOSS: a stop-out at…" caption** (moderate) — same class. Fix: `adaptivePrice(stop)` for the stop price in that string.
4. **Sub-dollar %.2f in "Chandelier exit ~…" line** (moderate) — `ts.level` formatted `%.2f`. Fix: `adaptivePrice(ts.level)`.
5. **What-if pence notional mixing raw pence with major-unit book values** (moderate) — `sizedNotional` returned `ps.notional` raw (shares × raw entry price), while `whatIfHoldings` are already major-unit via `holdingValue()`. For a .L/.JO idea the proposed add was ~100× its real value, falsely firing the concentration warning. Fix: `StockSageCurrency.majorUnitValue(symbol: idea.symbol, rawValue: ps.notional)` on the sizer output before passing to `StockSageWhatIf`.
6. **Permanent spinner when multiTimeframe fetch is blocked** (minor) — the else-branch showed an indefinite `ProgressView` whenever `store.multiTimeframe[symbol]` was nil, including when `ToolPolicy.isExternalAllowed == false` or under `--qa`. Fix: guard spinner on `ToolPolicy.isExternalAllowed && !--qa`; show "Weekly timeframe unavailable." otherwise.
7. **Conviction tooltip stale once calibration fitted** (minor) — `convictionMeter.help` in the detail sheet always said "Estimated win-rate range ~35-58%" even when EV numbers on the same sheet used a calibrated win-prob. Fix: `.help` is now conditional on `store.convictionCalibration`; calibrated → "win-rate mapped from N realized trades", uncalibrated → prior-band text.
8. **"Adds diversification" over-affirmed on partial coverage** (minor) — `heldSeries` dropped portfolio positions with no spark, then `StockSageClusterCheck` affirmed diversification over the subset with a green checkmark, hiding the coverage gap. Fix: track `uncheckedCount`; when `!cc.isConcentrating && uncheckedCount > 0` suppress the green checkmark and append "(among N holdings with history — M had no series and were not checked)".
9. **Floor badge hardcoded "0.005R/day"** (minor) — two sites in `ideaDetailSheet` duplicated the literal from `StockSageExpectedValue.minNetEVPerDayFloor`. Fix: both use `String(format: "%.3fR/day", StockSageExpectedValue.minNetEVPerDayFloor)`.
10. **sizeMetricHelp claimed PRIOR win-prob** (minor) — ALREADY FIXED in a prior commit (line 3384 correctly reads "Size uses the calibrated win-prob when a journal or backtest calibration is fitted…"). SKIPPED.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — risk-alloc-corr UI findings: 6 moderate/minor bugs fixed in MarketsView (subagent fix pass)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** Fixed the 6 verified findings in the "risk-alloc-corr" section of the adversarial UI review. Engine edits NOT allowed for this group; all fixes are pure display/logic in MarketsView.swift.
1. **Contradictory current-weight figures** (moderate) — `parityRow` showed raw `t.currentWeight` (engine raw price×shares, no FX/pence conversion) while the rebalance trade lines displayed USD-converted weights, producing contradictory e.g. "61% → 33%" vs "Sell (25%→33%)" for the same symbol on the same card. Fix: parityRow now shows only `targetWeight` and vol (the trade plan, which IS USD-correct, carries the current→target movement per symbol).
2. **FX-concentration suppression** (moderate) — guard `cb.exposures.count > 1 || !cb.unpriced.isEmpty` hid the entire currency-exposure block (including the FX-risk warning) for a 100%-GBP book (count==1, unpriced empty), the exact worst case. Fix: guard now `cb.hasFXRisk || cb.exposures.count > 1 || !cb.unpriced.isEmpty`.
