3. **Cost-basis fallback valuation** (moderate) — `currentPrice(p.symbol) ?? p.costBasis` in rebalHoldings, allocationPanel holdings, and ccyHoldings silently substituted a possibly-years-old cost basis for unpriced holdings, distorting all allocation slices and rebalance trade sizes with no caveat. Fix: all three compactMap on `currentPrice` only (no fallback); unpriced holdings are excluded and already named by `unpricedHoldings` at the headline total.
4. **Multi-lot uniquingKeysWith drops targets** (moderate) — `uniquingKeysWith: { a, _ in a }` kept only the FIRST lot's target weight for same-symbol multi-lot books, roughly halving a holding's target and spuriously recommending "Sell half." Fix: `uniquingKeysWith: +` sums weights across lots. Separately, `ForEach(store.riskParity)` had duplicate IDs (symbol) for multi-lot books → undefined SwiftUI behavior; fix: aggregate parity targets per symbol into a deduplicated `aggregatedParity` array before the ForEach, combining weights and averaging volatility.
5. **A11Y labels truncate** (minor) — `Int(x * 100)` truncates (4.6% → "4 percent") while the visible text uses `%.0f` (rounds to "5%"). Fix: all three sites (`allocationGroup`, currency exposure row, `parityRow`) now use `Int((x * 100).rounded())`.
6. **Hardcoded 2% copy** (minor) — `"✓ Within 2% of target"` hardcoded the band value while `plan()` was called with its default. Fix: `let rebalBand: Double = 0.02` named constant, passed explicitly `band: rebalBand`, interpolated into the caption `"Within \(Int(rebalBand * 100))% of target"`.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — Week-horizon research roadmap item #2: execution-timing advisory (owner: "everything in markets... make the fastest in a week... top 3 things")
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExecutionTiming.swift` (new), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExecutionTimingTests.swift` (new).
**What & why:** RESEARCH_2026-07-02_week_horizon_velocity.md's top-3 roadmap item #2 (zero-turnover execution timing) is the one piece genuinely actionable as a code change this pass. First, audited the engine against the research's refuse-list (naive short-term reversal, standalone PEAD, crypto funding-timing, daily overnight/intraday round-trips) — confirmed CLEAN: the RSI oversold/overbought terms in `StockSageAdvisor.advise()` are trend-CONFIRMED pullback entries (oversold only gets credit in an intact 12-1 uptrend, explicitly WITHHELD in a downtrend — "knife, not a dip"), structurally the opposite of naive cross-sectional reversal; `StockSageEarnings` is a risk-flag module (gap-risk warning), not a PEAD-trading module; no funding-rate code exists at all. Nothing to remove.
Then implemented the session-timing note: `StockSageExecutionTiming.sessionNote(action:regime:)`, a pure function gated on `.bullTrend`/`.bearTrend` (StockSage's own trend-family score IS a past-return/momentum construction) and buy/sell-family actions — returns nil for `.range` (the RSI-bounce read is a structurally different, non-momentum signal the Lou-Polk-Skouras finding doesn't cover) and for `.hold`/`.avoid`. Wired into `buildIdeas` as a flag-only `extraNotes` append, the EXACT precedent pattern `StockSageReturnShape`/`StockSageVolStability`/`StockSageVolRegime`/`StockSageSectorRotation` already established — `score`/`conviction`/`stopPrice`/`targetPrice`/`suggestedWeight` completely untouched.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1427 tests, 0 failures; 5 new). Roadmap item #1 (turnover-aware cost gate) has no new code surface — StockSage doesn't implement any of the refuse-listed high-turnover strategies to gate, and NetEdge's existing cost model already prices round-trip friction per idea; the refuse-list itself now stands as documented, indexed policy in research/INDEX.md → RESEARCH_2026-07-02_week_horizon_velocity.md, checked (per CLAUDE.md's standing instruction) before any future signal work. Roadmap item #3 (IRRX reversal overlay) remains explicitly gated behind a dedicated net-of-cost simulation — not attempted.

## 2026-07-02 — Markets UI review follow-up: net-cost-floor + low-conviction flags threaded into TodayActionPlan (spawned background task, dispatched by owner)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift`.
**What & why:** Completes the one Markets UI review finding that was correctly SKIPPED by its fix-agent for needing an out-of-lane engine change: "Today-plan rows hide the engine's below-net-cost-floor de-rank flag (and low-conviction demotion)." Verified against current source: `StockSageExpectedValue.fastLane()` demotes (via `velocityRankKey`'s −500_000/−1000 penalties) but does NOT exclude below-net-cost-floor or low-conviction ideas from its ordering — so a row in `StockSageTodayPlan.rankedActions`'s top-3 output CAN legitimately be one of them, with no on-screen indication, while the main ideas/fast-lane boards already show both via `netCostFloorFlag`/an inline `minConvictionToRank` check.
- Added `StockSageExpectedValue.isLowConviction(_:)` — a named, testable mirror of the exact `idea.advice.conviction < minConvictionToRank` comparison every rank-key function already applies internally (previously only inlined, never a discoverable named function).
- `TodayActionPlan` gained two defaulted (`.clears`/`false`) fields — `netCostFloorFlag`/`isLowConviction` — computed in `rankedActions` via the SAME functions the main board uses, so the two surfaces can never disagree.
- `MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift`'s row rendering gained matching badges (mirroring `fastLaneRow`'s exact "below net-cost floor" style) plus the low-conviction badge, and the accessibility label was extended to include both — matching the UI review's own "a11y must never omit what's visible" finding class.
- `copyAllText` also appends both flags (⚠ below net-cost floor / ⚠ low conviction) per the file's own established "the copied plan can't disagree with the on-screen one" principle.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1431 tests, 0 failures; 5 new — `isLowConvictionMirrorsTheExactRankKeyThreshold` pins the boundary at conviction==0.40 not-low; the two `rankedActions` tests use hand-verified-via-standalone-Swift-snippet fixtures that isolate EACH flag independently: a conviction-0.40/1.4:1-R:R idea trips ONLY the floor flag (netVelocity≈0.0029R/day < 0.005), a conviction-0.20/3:1-R:R idea trips ONLY the conviction flag (netVelocity≈0.0465R/day, comfortably clear) — verified precisely before writing the fixtures, not guessed). Closes the last open item from the 2026-07-02 Markets UI adversarial review.

## 2026-07-02 — Week-horizon research roadmap #2 completion: overnight financing cost wired into short-side net EV (owner: "continue improving the markets tab on what u researched")
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageNetEdge.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageNetEdgeTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`.
**What & why:** The execution-timing roadmap item (#2 of RESEARCH_2026-07-02_week_horizon_velocity.md) explicitly called for "overnight borrow/margin costs charged into short-side and levered EV" — the earlier pass only shipped the display-only session-timing note, not this piece. Audited and confirmed a genuine gap: `StockSageNetEdge.allInCost` has always itemized a `financingCost` leg (rate·holdDays), but `evaluate()` — the function every net-EV/ranking number (`netEVR`, `netVelocity`, `netCostFloorFlag`, `clearsCostAfterFrictions`, the whole `rankByEV`/`velocityRankKey`/`bestOpportunity` chain) actually derives from — never accepted or incorporated it. `allInCost` itself had ZERO production callers (grepped), confirming this was dead-code-adjacent, unwired.
- `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate` gained default-preserving `annualFinancingRate: Double = 0, holdDays: Double = 0` params (0/0 → byte-identical to prior behavior for every existing caller), using the SAME `entry·rate·days/365` formula `allInCost` already itemizes.
- Added `StockSageNetEdge.defaultShortBorrowRate = 0.03` (3%/year) — a labeled, conservative retail-honest estimate, documented as a middle ground between the narrow stock-loan fee alone (too optimistic — ignores the margin-account requirement itself) and a full retail margin rate (broker-specific, higher).
- `StockSageExpectedValue.financingCostInputs(for:)` (new, private): 0/0 for buy-family (a cash long owns the shares outright), `defaultShortBorrowRate` + `expectedHoldDays(for:)` for sell/reduce (a genuine short-side plan per `StockSageAdvisor.stopTarget`'s stop-above/target-below construction — definitionally a margin transaction). Threaded into `netEVR`/`clearsCostAfterFrictions`, the two functions that drive every net-of-cost ranking/demotion signal.
- `StockSageCapitalAllocator.allocate` needed NO change — it already only funds buy-family ideas (`a.action == .buy || a.action == .strongBuy` guard), so financing is structurally always 0 there; confirmed by reading before touching anything.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1434 tests, 0 failures; 6 new). One numeric fixture caught a genuine hand-derivation error during verification: an early NetEdgeTests draft hardcoded `reward: 40` for `entry:100, stop:90, target:130` when `abs(130-100)` is actually 30 — the mismatch surfaced immediately as a test failure (not silently accepted), re-derived correctly via a standalone Swift snippet before fixing the fixture. Magnitude sanity-checked before implementing: ~9.9bps for a 12-day equity short (roughly doubles the existing 13bps round-trip cost — a real, meaningful effect) vs ~2.5bps for a 3-day crypto short (small relative to crypto's already-wide 70bps spread) — well-calibrated, not overwhelming. Closes roadmap item #2's financing-cost piece; the session-timing advisory (StockSageExecutionTiming) from the earlier pass covers the rest of that item.

## 2026-07-02 — Sub-dollar price formatting fix in clipboard copy-all text (subagent delta fix)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift`.
**What & why:** `StockSageTodayPlan.fmt` was a one-liner `String(format: "%.2f", v)` — for DOGE-class prices (~$0.104 entry, ~$0.099 stop) both values rounded to "0.10", producing a zero-distance plan in the copied clipboard text the user pastes into a broker. `MarketsTodayActionsCard.adaptivePrice` and `MarketsView.adaptivePrice` were already correct (%.4f for sub-dollar, %.6f for sub-cent); this fix makes `fmt` an exact mirror of that convention. Updated the pinned-%.2f test assertion to use the same adaptive logic, and added two regression tests: `subDollarPricesUseFourDecimalPlacesInCopyText` (DOGE-USD fixture, entry=0.1040 ≠ stop=0.0990) and `subCentPricesUseSixDecimalPlacesInCopyText` (SHIB-USD fixture). The `build()` path already called `fmt()` at lines 53-54 so it inherits the fix automatically.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — MarketsRiskAllocationSection: 4 delta fixes to match MarketsView.swift (subagent delta fix pass)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsRiskAllocationSection.swift`
**What & why:** Four independently-verified bugs in the satellite risk/allocation section that MarketsView.swift had already fixed but the satellite file had not received:
1. **FX-concentration guard** — added `cb.hasFXRisk` to the currency-exposure `if` guard at line 323, identical to MarketsView.swift line 1314. A 100%-one-currency (e.g. all .L / all .SR) book previously silently suppressed the FX concentration warning.
2. **Unpriced holdings cost-basis fallback** — removed `?? p.costBasis` / `?? pos.costBasis` fallback at all three sites (parityHoldings, allocationPanel holdings, ccyHoldings), mirroring MarketsView.swift's three `guard let price = currentPrice(...)` exclusions. Each `.map` → `.compactMap` with a guard so unpriced holdings are excluded rather than substituted with possibly years-old cost basis.
3. **Multi-lot rebalance targets first-wins** — changed `uniquingKeysWith: { a, _ in a }` to `uniquingKeysWith: +` in rebalTargets construction, mirroring MarketsView.swift line 1212. Two-lot same-symbol books previously got only the first lot's target weight fed to the plan, producing spurious "Sell ~half your AAPL" trades.
4. **Stale-parity suppression absent** — added the `liveSymbols != sizedSymbols` guard (mirroring MarketsView.swift lines 1162-1170) around the entire plan computation block. When holdings change after the last "Balance by risk" run, the section now shows the same "Holdings changed — tap Balance by risk again" warning instead of computing trades from a mismatched snapshot.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **

## 2026-07-02 — Consistency follow-up: financing cost threaded into the UI net-cost displays; StockSageTodayPlan confirmed structurally buy-only (owner: "continue never stop improving")
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageNetEdge.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageNetEdgeTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift`.
**What & why:** After wiring financing into `netEVR`/`netVelocity`/`netCostFloorFlag`, audited every OTHER site that computes a net-cost number for display and found 3 in `MarketsView.swift` that still called `StockSageNetEdge.evaluate`/`netRR` WITHOUT financing — the detail sheet's own inline net-cost line, and both places inside the "Copy plan" button (the net R:R line + the gate-verdict netRR) — meaning a short idea's own detail sheet could show a HIGHER net R:R than what was actually driving its rank on the board, exactly the "two contradictory numbers for the same idea" bug class the earlier UI review caught elsewhere.
- `StockSageExpectedValue.financingCostInputs(for:)` made non-private so the UI can reuse the exact same rate/hold-days logic rather than re-deriving it.
- `StockSageNetEdge.netRR` gained the same default-preserving `annualFinancingRate`/`holdDays` params `evaluate` already has.
- All 3 MarketsView.swift sites now thread `financingCostInputs(for: idea)` through; the detail-sheet line also grew a `"+ ~Nbps/yr short financing"` note when financing is non-zero, and its `.help` text now mentions the overnight cost.
- Attempted the SAME fix in `StockSageTodayPlan.swift`'s `build`/`rankedActions`, then discovered via a failing test that it was unreachable: `StockSageExpectedValue.velocityRankKey` explicitly guards `case .buyFamily = side(idea)` ("a short does not compound the same way... Fixes a short topping the Fast Lane while it is correctly barred from the best-opportunity card"), and `bestOpportunity` is buy-only too — so a short idea can NEVER reach `rankedActions`/`build`. Reverted that piece (kept it minimal rather than shipping dead code) and replaced the two now-false-premise tests with one that pins the actual, correct behavior (`rankedActionsNeverIncludesAShortIdeaSoFinancingIsStructurallyUnreachableHere`), so a future change can't silently start showing shorts there without financing.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1437 tests, 0 failures; net across this round: 3 new tests, after adding then correctly removing 2 that tested a false premise). Caught mid-flight by the test suite itself doing exactly its job — a wrong assumption about TodayPlan's reachability surfaced as a failing test instead of shipping quietly.

## 2026-07-02 — MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md: per-file knowledge map of the whole Markets tab (owner: "read all files so u can understand markets tab even more")
**Files:** `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` (new, ~218KB), `PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` (companion-docs link), `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.
**What & why:** Reading all ~71 Markets-tab files raw into one context is impossible, so a 10-domain full-read fan-out (workflow wf_4ea2429d-484; every file read IN FULL, every consumer claim grep-verified; Sonnet-max readers per the owner's orchestrator/implementer split) produced structured per-file maps — Purpose / Key symbols / Inputs / Consumers / Invariants / Gotchas — mechanically assembled (python, zero-fidelity-loss) into `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`: 78 module entries across core-scoring, sizing-allocation, portfolio-risk, exits-stops, ev-ranking, backtest-validation, data-feed, alerts-monitor, journal-plan-misc, and views (MarketsView mapped per MARK section). The gotcha entries capture exactly the traps this session's reviews kept finding: unit-scale mismatches (annualizedVolatility FRACTION vs returnOverPeriod PERCENT), nil-vs-0 sentinel semantics, deliberately-ablated flags (relativeStrengthEnabled), deliberately-UNWIRED modules, and threshold provenance. Spot-checked against firsthand ground truth before committing.
**Result:** Durable knowledge artifact that outlives any session's context window; linked from PROJECT_CONTEXT's companion-docs header. Maintenance rule stated in the file header: update the relevant entry when materially changing a file.

## 2026-07-02 — Ideas-card improvement wave: FASTMONEY #5 UI + execution-timing chip + Dynamic Type sweep (owner priority)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** Three owner-priority items, all UI-only in MarketsView.swift (no engine changes):
1. **FASTMONEY #5 momentum dot** — in `fastLaneRow`, when `idea.momentumQuality != nil`, show a 5pt colored dot + "hot"/"mixed"/"cold" label (thresholds ≥2/3 / ≥1/3 / else, matching the tri-signal 0/⅓/⅔/1 natural values). nil → nothing shown (honesty floor: never a fabricated neutral). `.help`: "Momentum read: ER trend + MACD histogram + 21-day return — a 1-day blip is not a 3-12d win. Descriptive, not predictive." `accessibilityLabel` extended with momentum quality word. `Circle().fill` not an Image, so the "don't touch icon-only Image fonts" convention is satisfied.
2. **Execution-timing chip on ideaCard** — after the confluence badge, when `StockSageExecutionTiming.sessionNote(action:regime:) != nil`, show a compact "⏱ near close" chip styled with DS.Palette.accent, full `sessionNote` as `.help`, and `accessibilityLabel("Execution timing: …")`. `StockSageExecutionTiming` already gates to trending buy/sell only (nil for range/hold/avoid), so the guard is trivially correct. Extended ideaCard's `.accessibilityLabel` closure to append ", enter near close for trend momentum edge" when the note fires.
3. **Dynamic Type sweep (ideas sections only)** — added 4 new `@ScaledMetric` tokens: `mvFont12=12`, `mvFont14=14`, `mvFont15=15`, `mvFont16=16`. Replaced ALL hardcoded text `.system(size: N)` fonts in `ideasHeader`, `ideaCard`, `bestOpportunityCard`, `capitalAllocationCard`, `summaryStat`, and `fastLaneRow`/`fastLaneBoardSection`. Icon-only `Image` lines kept as-is (existing convention). The `size: 11.5` non-integer in the "Find ideas" button mapped to `mvFont11` (nearest clean token). Other sections (journal, portfolio, risk) untouched — diff is reviewable.
**Result:** BUILD SUCCEEDED.

## 2026-07-02 — FASTMONEY_BACKLOG #5 data-plumbing: StockSageIdea.momentumQuality field + buildIdeas wiring
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`.
**What & why:** `StockSageExpectedValue.momentumQuality(for:closes:)` (0–1, already shipped + tested) needs RAW closes, but `StockSageIdea` only carried the downsampled spark — the exact reason the UI half of #5 was deferred (FASTMONEY_BACKLOG 2026-07-01 note). Added `momentumQuality: Double? = nil` as a trailing-default init param on `StockSageIdea` so every existing construction stays valid; nil means "unknown/not-computed", never defaults to a number (honesty floor). In `buildIdeas`, compute it once per idea from `history.closes` right where `dailyMove`/`realizedVol` are computed, gated on `history.closes.count > 20` (the minimum for `efficiencyRatio`, the loosest of the three internal signals; ≤ 20 bars → all signals nil → `momentumQuality()` returns the neutral sentinel 1.0 which is NOT a real quality score → store nil instead). Added 4 tests to `StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`: field defaults nil (trailing-default); explicit value is stored faithfully; nil at ≤ 20 closes with pinned signal assertions; non-nil at > 20 closes with quality ≥ 1/3 (at least the ER signal fired). `buildIdeas` end-to-end test omitted by design (network-coupled); field-level + existing `momentumQuality` function tests are the correct coverage.
**Result:** BUILD SUCCEEDED, ** TEST SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — Ideas-card wave 2: delta-verify of wave 1 + 3 minor fixes (owner priority: ideas card)
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** Two read-only Sonnet-max verifiers adversarially re-checked wave 1 (commit 5c88fb4). Verdict: structurally sound — the 1.0 unknown-sentinel is provably unreachable through the buildIdeas gate (at >20 bars ER always fires), the dot renders nothing on nil, the timing chip's gating traced correct through every action×regime combination, and a11y labels match visible content. Three MINOR findings, all fixed by hand:
1. **21-bar boundary untested** — the "justEnough" test used 22 bars while its comment claimed 21, leaving the true partial-signal state (ER fires, returnOverPeriod(21) and MACD do NOT) unpinned. Added `momentumQualityAtExactlyTwentyOneBarsUsesOnlyTheEfficiencyRatioSignal`: pins that at exactly 21 bars the quality is computed over a ONE-signal denominator (monotone-up → exactly 1.0 as a REAL measured value, not the sentinel), so a future indicator-threshold change can't silently alter the denominator. Comment corrected 21→22.
2. **Dot .help under-disclosed short histories** — copy amended to "short histories may use fewer than 3 signals" (a 21-34-bar name shows a dot computed from fewer signals than the tooltip implied).
3. **"Find ideas" button silently shrank 0.5pt** — the Dynamic Type sweep had rounded its 11.5pt into mvFont11; added a dedicated `mvFont11_5` @ScaledMetric token so the button keeps its exact pre-sweep visible size at default text setting while still scaling.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1444 tests, 0 failures; 1 new).

## 2026-07-02 — Ideas-card wave 4: 17 display-only improvements (4-lens triage — trader-workflow, honesty-clarity, badge-density, board-scan)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` only.
**What & why:** 17 triaged improvements from a 4-lens adversarial design critique (trader-workflow / honesty-clarity / badge-density / board-scan). All display-layer; no engine, no store changes.
- trader-workflow #1 (Vol-adj size): when `idea.volRegime?.sizingMultiplier < 0.85`, shows a "Vol-adj N.N%" metric after "Base size"; label is "Vol-adj" not "Effective" per honesty constraint; nil volRegime → nothing shown.
- trader-workflow #2 (floor badge rename): "↓ floor" → "costs > edge" — states the economic reason directly.
- trader-workflow #3 (rationale reorder): rationale bullets first, regime token last in the strip; regime context is after the most-actionable signal.
- trader-workflow #4 (stop distance %): appends "(N.N%)" parenthetically to the Stop metric value — visible risk distance without opening the detail sheet.
- trader-workflow #5 Part A (momentum dot on main card): momentum dot copied from fastLaneRow into ideaCard metrics row after R:R; same nil-guard and tooltip.
- trader-workflow #5 Part B (remove per-row "24/7 · volatile"): removed from fastLaneRow — section header already says "24/7"; kept in a11y label for VoiceOver users.
- honesty-clarity #1 (EV chip gross label): format string changed from "%+.2fR EV" to "%+.2fR EV (gross)"; tooltip updated to match; consistent with fast-lane row.
- honesty-clarity #3 (per-card staleness): `boardIsStale` dims card to 0.75 opacity and adds a clock badge in the top chip row — mirrors watchlist signalCard pattern.
- honesty-clarity #4 ("Base size" rename): "Size" → "Base size" in ideaCard AND bestOpportunityCard; tooltip unchanged.
- honesty-clarity #5 / badge-density #2 / board-scan #4 (near-close chip removal, implemented ONCE): removed the `⏱ near close` chip block (~8 lines); timing note stays in rationale and detail sheet; a11y label line also removed. StockSageExecutionTiming and buildIdeas wiring untouched.
- badge-density #1 (badge ordering): reordered HStack — risk warnings (earnings, floor) BEFORE opportunity signals (EV, confluence); action chip stays as identity anchor.
- badge-density #4 (near-close accent recolor): MOOT — subsumed by removal.
- badge-density #5 (momentum dot first in fastLaneRow): momentum dot moved immediately after velocity number, before earnings and floor badges; a11y label order updated to match.
- board-scan #1 (active-sort metric first): when `ideaSort == .velocity`, prepends a "Vel." metric as the first item in ideaCard's metrics HStack.
- board-scan #2 (fixed-width alignment): action badge gets `minWidth: 74`, EV chip gets `minWidth: 72, alignment: .trailing` and `.monospacedDigit()` — enables column-scanning across cards.
- board-scan #3 (sort-mode chip in summary strip): added a non-interactive "↕ N sort" summaryChip to ideasSummaryStrip, always visible as the board scrolls.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED **.

## 2026-07-02 — Ideas-card wave 5: delta-verify of wave 4 + 3 fixes (owner priority: ideas card)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`.
**What & why:** A Sonnet-max verifier ran 9 targeted probes on wave 4's diff (commit f62c6fd). Six clean — including the sharpest probes: `store.ideasIsStale` genuinely exists (StockSageStore.swift, >4h), the Vol-adj formula is exactly `suggestedWeight × sizingMultiplier`, the chip removal left zero dead code while the buildIdeas rationale wiring survived, and the retained "24/7 volatile" a11y text on fast-lane rows was judged a deliberate, defensible VoiceOver choice (row-by-row navigation loses the section-header context sighted users get). Three findings, fixed by hand:
1. **Staleness never spoken to VoiceOver** (moderate) — the explicit `.accessibilityLabel` on the card's `.combine` element REPLACES synthesized child labels, swallowing the clock badge's own label; the opacity dim is invisible to VoiceOver too. Fixed: `boardIsStale` now appends ", board data is over 4 hours old" inside the outer label closure.
2. **No zero-price guard on the stop-distance %** (minor) — `abs(price−stop)/price` could render "(inf%)"/"(nan%)" on a malformed zero price. Fixed with the same `price > 0` pattern `rewardRisk()` already uses; the bare stop price still displays when unguardable.
3. **Vel. metric transparency** (minor/informational) — the card shows GROSS EV/day while the board orders by net-adjusted log-growth (documented, intentional, consistent with the fast lane). Added a `.help` tooltip disclosing the divergence so two cards with similar gross Vel. ranking apart is explicable.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1444 tests, 0 failures).

## 2026-07-02 — Ideas-card wave 6: visual QA of waves 1–5 in the live app (owner priority: ideas card)
**Files:** none (verification only).
**What & why:** Switched verification modality from code review to PIXELS: built and launched the app (driver.sh), connected the live feed (210 names, 1 fetch-miss honestly surfaced as "191 priced · 1 couldn't be fetched (ROG.SW) — ranking covers only what loaded"), and screenshot-verified the ideas board end-to-end via computer-use. CONFIRMED RENDERING: the reward:risk sort-trace chip in the summary strip; "+0.51R EV (gross)" labeling; "Stop 409.17 (1.9%)" distance; "Base size" relabel; "● hot" momentum dots on both the main card and every fast-lane row (positioned first-after-velocity per the badge-order spec); rationale-first strip with the regime token demoted to last; fast-lane gross labels + concentration warning; Today's-plan ranked rows with gate CAUTION badges; cancellable ideas-refresh progress ("Loading 210/210", Cancel button). Conditionals behaved correctly by ABSENCE too: no Vol-adj metric (multiplier ≥ 0.85 on the visible cards) and no staleness dim/clock on a freshly-refreshed board.
**Result:** ✅ Visual QA pass — no layout regressions from the minWidth frames, no badge sprawl on real data. App instance stopped after capture.

## 2026-07-02 — Wave-9 adversarial fix batch: 13 findings (A–M) across StockSageTradePlan + MarketsView + tests
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTradePlan.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTradePlanTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:** Triaged and implemented 13 adversarially-verified findings from the wave-8 sheet restructure. Three BLOCKERs + seven SHOULD-FIXes + three NITs:
- **A** (BLOCKER): Added `nonisolated private static func adaptivePrice` to `StockSageTradePlan` replicating `MarketsView.adaptivePrice` exactly (≥$1→2dp, ≥$0.01→4dp, sub-cent→6dp). Used for rung prices (previously `%.2f` — broke sub-dollar names) and chandelier level (same issue). rMultiple keeps `%.1f`. Stop/Target in plan text also switched to adaptivePrice for consistency.
- **B** (BLOCKER): Action line relabeled from `"conviction X%"` to `"signal strength X/100 · regime — rules-based score, not a win probability"` matching the sheet's wave-8 honesty relabel and making the pasted plan self-contained. Existing test `contains("Action: Buy")` still satisfied.
- **C** (BLOCKER): Regime size `.help` (~4428) and `sizeMetricHelp` (~3500) both rewritten to state the correct allocator pipeline order: vol-targeting shrink → per-symbol vol-regime brake → correlation de-weighting → heat cap (the vol-targeting step was omitted).
- **D** (SHOULD-FIX): `positionSizerPanel(idea)` call added immediately after the buy-guard's closing brace for Sell/Reduce ideas (never renders twice; sizer's own stopPrice guard suppresses for Hold/Avoid).
- **E** (SHOULD-FIX): `hasExitPlanContent` and `hasContextContent` bools computed from the real child guards; Divider+header for both sections now gated on them (no more orphaned floating labels for Hold/Avoid with zero content). Also applied fix I (.accessibilityAddTraits(.isHeader)) to both new conditional headers.
- **F** (SHOULD-FIX): Ladder bell `.help` and `.accessibilityLabel` now branch on `alreadyArmed` (armed → "Price alert already armed at PRICE"; not armed → original text).
- **G** (NIT): Duplicate "not a win probability" disclaimer removed from value label (kept in caption below). Value label is now clean "Signal strength X/100 · regime"; caption is "How many rules agree — NOT a win probability."
- **H** (SHOULD-FIX): EV line changed from `"Est. EV %+.2fR per trade (~..."` to `"Est. EV %+.2fR per trade (gross) (~..."` matching the idea-card header.
- **I** (SHOULD-FIX): `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isHeader)` added to "Exit plan" and "Context" header Text views (done as part of E's conditional header logic; "Why" text left alone — it sits inside a .combine element).
- **J** (NIT): Both branches of sheet conviction-meter `.help` changed from `"Conviction —"` to `"Signal strength —"` (sheet only; idea-card tooltip at ~3101 untouched).
- **K** (NIT): `store.addPriceAlert(symbol: idea.symbol.uppercased(), ...)` → `store.addPriceAlert(symbol: idea.symbol, ...)` (store normalizes internally).
- **L** (NIT): Comment added above `alreadyArmed` predicate noting the exact-Double-equality on rung.price is intentional (matches store's dedup; refreshed quote regenerates rungs).
- **M** (TESTS): Added 3 new tests to `StockSageTradePlanTests.swift` (wave-9 section): `actionLineUsesSignalStrengthLabel`, `ladderPlanTextAdaptivePriceAndOrdering`, `chandelierPlanTextAdaptiveSubDollar`. Fixtures hand-verified via `derive_wave9_fixture.swift` (scratchpad) before writing assertions. Caught one false assertion (`contains("~0.00")` fails because `"~0.006200"` itself contains that substring) and corrected it to `!contains("~0.01")` (the actual 2dp-rounding artifact). All existing tests untouched and green.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (all tests pass, 3 new tests green). No test expectation from the pre-wave-9 suite required updating (the `contains("Action: Buy")` assertion is still satisfied by the new Action line format).

## 2026-07-02 — Wave-11: 6 display/label-only improvements to ideaDetailSheet (wave11_accepted.md)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTradeGate.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageRewardRisk.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:** Implemented all 6 accepted items from the wave-10 4-lens Fable-xhigh critique:
- **Item 1 (Pinned CTA bar):** Moved the CTA HStack (Log / Copy plan / Backtest 5 years) + caveat OUT of the scrolled VStack into `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)`. Opaque bezel-matched background + top hairline divider. CONDITIONAL verdict chip (buy-family only: "Clear to trade" / "Proceed with caution" / "Don't take this trade") reusing the gate computed at the same inputs as the in-sheet gate section — so commitment never detaches from the verdict. Hold/Avoid show buttons only (no fabricated verdict). Chip carries `.accessibilityLabel("Pre-trade gate: …")` + `.layoutPriority(1)`/`.lineLimit(1)` so the verdict never truncates at minWidth 440. (`.safeAreaInset` already insets the ScrollView content on macOS 26.5, so the bottom breathing space is a small `DS.Space.sm` gap, NOT a 72px spacer — the earlier 72px spacer double-gapped and was removed in the correction pass below.)
- **Item 2 (Backtest auto-scroll):** Wrapped ScrollView content in ScrollViewReader; added `Color.clear.frame(height: 0).id("backtestAnchor")` before the backtest panel. `.onChange(of: store.backtestSymbol)` guarded to `== idea.symbol` (no yank from other-idea backtests); scrolls to anchor with `.easeOut(duration: 0.35)`.
- **Item 3 (Escape):** `.keyboardShortcut(.cancelAction)` added to the existing X close Button. `.help` updated to "Close (Esc)". No other cancelAction in the sheet.
- **Item 4 (Copy-plan unification — honesty fix):** Extracted the sheet's Copy-plan text closure into `private func fullPlanText(for:)`. Both the pinned-bar Copy-plan button and the card context-menu `copyIdeaPlan` now call this helper. `copyIdeaPlan`'s hand-rolled one-liner (missing caveat / gate verdict / net R:R / risk flags / "not a win probability" disclaimer) is deleted. The card export is now byte-identical to the sheet export.
- **Item 5 (Calibration chip):** `calibrationChip` label changed from bare "assumed"/"measured · n=X" to "win% assumed"/"win% measured · n=X". Tooltip updated from "EV win-rates" to "Win-rate". Propagates to all 5 call sites (sheet EV, best-opp, deploy, money-velocity, ideaDetailSheet).
- **Item 6 (Net/gross R:R labeling):** (a) Added `rrIsNet: Bool = false` param to `StockSageTradeGate.evaluate` — when true, check label reads "Net reward:risk (after est. costs) X.X:1" instead of "Reward:risk X.X:1". Default false = byte-identical for all other callers. Passed `rrIsNet: resolvedNetRR != nil` from the sheet gate and the pinned bar chip gate — the `?? gross` fallback is NEVER mislabeled "net". `fullPlanText(for:)` follows the same rule. (b) Added " gross" suffix to `RewardRisk.note` → "R:R 2.0 gross — Fair; needs a >33.3% win-rate just to break even."
- **riskFlags hoist:** Moved `let riskFlags = StockSageRiskFlags.flags(...)` to function level in `ideaDetailSheet` (above the ScrollView) so the Copy-plan closure and the pinned bar's chip gate both see it.
**Correction pass (folded in before merge — from the wave-11 3-lens Fable-xhigh delta-verify + the Ideas-board audit):** All display/label/comment/test-only, no ranking/engine/numeric/verdict change.
- **Layout:** removed the 72px bottom spacer (`.safeAreaInset` already insets on macOS 26.5 → it double-gapped ~96px) → `DS.Space.sm`; swapped the reader nesting to the house pattern (`ScrollViewReader { proxy in ScrollView { … } }` — the ScrollView is now the reader's CONTENT, matching ContentView/CodeView/CommandPalette) so the backtest auto-scroll can't silently no-op; `onChange` → two-param form; negative `.padding(.top, -DS.Space.sm)` on the zero-height backtest anchor to cancel a phantom VStack gap.
- **Copy-plan honesty (F15/F28):** `fullPlanText(for:)` now computes the gate with the SAME condition as the on-screen gate/chip — a stop-less buy's "Don't take this trade" verdict now appears in the exported plan (previously omitted), and sell/reduce no longer get a gate line the screen never shows. `StockSageTradePlan.text`'s R:R line labeled `gross` to match `RewardRisk.note` (removes the in-artifact contradiction of an unlabeled gross break-even directly above a labeled net one). "est." added to the copied net-cost line.
- **Cross-surface net/gross (F09):** threaded `rrIsNet: resolvedNetRR != nil` through `StockSageTodayPlan.build()` and `rankedActions()` so the Today-actions card stops labeling a net figure plain "Reward:risk" while the sheet says "Net reward:risk (after est. costs)". Display-only; verdicts unchanged.
- **Test coverage (F16/F18 — the gap that let the above slip):** +3 `StockSageTradeGateTests` (rrIsNet:true label, default-path byte-identity, net-negative rr → blocked + "costs exceed the reward"), +2 `StockSageRewardRiskTests` (note contains "gross" + breakeven decimal), and `StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests` updated to pass matching `rrIsNet`. All fixtures HAND-DERIVED via `derive_wave11f.swift`.
- **Docs:** MARKETS_TAB_MAP consumer/line refs refreshed; `research/INDEX.md` corrected to note `candidateSelectorEnabled` shipped ACTIVATED (owner, 2026-06-27).
**HELD for owner (from the audit, NOT applied — display/behavior judgment):** F01/F02 identity-calibration "measured vs assumed" seam (CRITICAL, owner-ratified default, test-locked); F03 weekly gross/net headline; F08 Conviction↔Signal-strength term; F10 decimal-comma locale; F04 sizer parse unification; F12/F13 perf; plus assorted engine-file label/hygiene items. See `AUDIT_2026-07-02_ideas_board.md`.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (1,452 tests, 0 failures — +5 new label-pinning tests). Independently re-verified (fresh build + full suite) after the correction pass.

## 2026-07-02 — Audit defects F11/F31/F32/F27/F36/F37 fixed (6 findings from AUDIT_2026-07-02_ideas_board.md)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTradeGate.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTradeGateTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageEarningsTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:**
- **F11 (Store — retry-ability):** `refreshLaneCorrelation` previously recorded `laneCorrelationFingerprint` even on a nil (failed) fetch, permanently blocking retries. Fixed: fingerprint recorded ONLY when result is non-nil, mirroring `refreshTrailingStop`/`refreshLiquidity` cache-on-success. SKIPPED unit test (function is @MainActor async + requires network; no pure decision logic to isolate; skip noted in test file).
- **F31 (View — cancellation):** `.task` in `ideaDetailSheet` inserted into `mtfFetchCompleted` even when the sheet was dismissed mid-fetch (Task cancelled but execution continued past the await). Fixed: `guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }` before the insert, so a cancelled fetch does not mark the symbol done. No store-caching change.
- **F32 (Store — stale earnings expiry):** `deriveEarningsProximity` now evicts any cached date more than 86400s in the past (`.compactMapValues { date in guard date.timeIntervalSince(now) >= -86_400 else { return nil } … }`). The `.imminent` badge and −2000 rank demotion now clear automatically mid-session after a report date passes. Numeric/ranking logic for fresh dates unchanged. +5 tests in StockSageEarningsTests.
- **F27 (Label honesty — financing note):** Two `financingNote` constructions in MarketsView (`fullPlanText` + sheet evidence line) changed from `finRate > 0` to `finRate > 0 && finDays > 0`. FX/index `.sell` ideas have 0 hold days (expectedHoldDays returns nil → 0 fallback), so $0 financing is modeled — the old condition falsely claimed "~300bps/yr short financing was included". Numeric output identical. +3 tests in StockSageExpectedValueTests.
- **F36 (TradeGate label bands):** Correlation check label split into three honest bands: >=0.8 warn ('Highly correlated…'), 0.5–<0.8 pass ('Moderate correlation X.XX — partial overlap with your book'), <0.5 pass ('Low correlation (X.XX)…adds diversification'). Gate LEVEL unchanged — both moderate and low still produce .pass. +4 tests in StockSageTradeGateTests.
- **F37 (Advisor momentum label):** Threshold for '6-month momentum' label raised from `momPeriod >= 100` to `momPeriod >= 120`. 100 bars ≈ 4.6 months; only momPeriod >= 120 (~5.5 months) earns '6-month'. Shorter windows say 'momentum (N-day window)'. Numeric signals unchanged. Existing test `shortHistoryMomentumRationaleDoesNotClaimSixMonths` (40 bars) still passes. +4 tests in StockSageAdvisorTests.
**Updated test pins:** None — no existing test asserted the old (incorrect) label strings for F36/F37; the affected tests only checked decision levels (unchanged) or used fixture sizes outside the 100–119 bar range affected; the existing `shortHistoryMomentumRationaleDoesNotClaimSixMonths` (40-bar fixture) still passes under the new >=120 threshold.
**All fixture derivations are preserved as inline comments in the test files themselves** (the session scratchpad is ephemeral and not checked in).
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (+16 new tests: 5 F32, 4 F36, 4 F37, 3 F27; all passed on first run).

## 2026-07-02 — WAVE 12: display-only polish + parity pass (11 items, 10 DONE 1 N/A)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsTodayActionsCard.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageGlossary.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:** Display-only polish + parity pass from the 2026-07-02 ideas-board audit (F21/F22/F25/F26/F29/F30 + ergonomics#4/#6 + hierarchy#5 + trust#4/#5 + coherence#5/#6/sizing-parity). HARD RULE: zero ranking/numeric/decision change; no honesty caveat weakened.
- **Item 1 (ergonomics#4 — planCopied feedback):** Added `@State private var planCopied = false` + `.onChange(of: selectedIdea)` reset. Pinned-bar "Copy plan" button now shows icon→checkmark + label→"Copied" for 2s, then reverts. `frame(minWidth:58)` on label prevents capsule jump. Mirrors ideasCopied pattern.
- **Item 2 (ergonomics#6 — Arm all 3):** Added "Arm all 3" text button on the scale-out ladder header row. `allArmed` predicate (allSatisfy: isArmed + symbol + target + direction). Disabled + .help + .accessibilityLabel for both armed/unarmed states. Calls store.addPriceAlert with same direction rule as per-rung bells.
- **Item 3 (hierarchy#5 — section headers):** 'Exit plan' and 'Context' headers restyled to size 12 semibold white (matched 'Why'); 'Evidence' header + Divider added above evidence pile (net-cost/EV/velocity/floor/backtest, guarded on stop+target non-nil); regime-size caveat relocated from top of Exit plan to directly under plan-numerics row (explains Regime size metric there, keeping stale-regime color branch); hasExitPlanContent simplified to ladder/chandelier-only.
- **Item 4 (trust#4 — caution badge explanation):** gateBadge in MarketsTodayActionsCard gains .help joining warn/fail labels with " · " separator. CAUTION rows render first warn label as small secondary Text line (lineLimit:1, '+N more' suffix when several warns).
- **Item 5 (trust#5 — net velocity in sheet):** Sheet velocity evidence line: computes netVelocity; if non-nil shows "gross - net after frictions" form; nil → "gross" labeled only. Never fabricates a net.
- **Item 6 (F29/F30 — gross-tag parity):** Added '(gross)' to: moneyVelocityCard "Best now" EV stat, bestOpportunityCard Est. EV, bestOpportunityCTA evText, Today card row velocity, TodayPlan copyAllText velocity line (→ "R/day gross"), sheet velocity line (covered by item 5). Fixed ideaCard EV chip .help misdirection (was "Net EV and velocity are shown in the fast lane" — fast lane shows GROSS; rewrote to point at detail sheet's Evidence net-cost line).
- **Item 7 (coherence#6 — sizing parity):** Added conditional 'Vol-adj' metric to sheet's plan-numerics row. Identical `<0.85` gate and `suggestedWeight * sizingMultiplier * 100` formula to ideaCard. Order: Base → Regime → Vol-adj. .help frames as "Deploy plan is authoritative".
- **Item 8 (F22 — band constant):** Added `nonisolated static let assumedWinBandLabel = "35–58%"` to StockSageExpectedValue (comment ties it to 0.35 + 0.23 constants). Interpolated at all 6 display sites (MarketsView ×4, Glossary ×1, calibrationChip ×1). +1 test `assumedWinBandLabelMatchesBandEndpoints` in StockSageExpectedValueTests (asserts label == "\(lowPct)–\(highPct)%" derived from the numeric endpoints).
- **Item 9 (F25 — crypto predicate):** Replaced 3 inline `hasSuffix("-USD")` checks in MarketsView with `StockSageAllocation.assetClass(sym) == "Crypto"` (byte-identical behavior; assetClass uses hasSuffix("-USD") internally).
- **Item 10 (F26 — twin copy labels):** Unified "Copy plan" → "Copy today's plan" in BestOpportunityActionCard; aligned .help text. Renamed the button label only — the callback was already unified in wave-11 (both paths already called the same fullPlanText-based callback).
- **Item 11 (F21 — label reword):** Fixed "Signal strength — a rules-based signal strength, not a probability…" → "Signal strength — a rules-based score, not a probability…" in uncalibrated branch of ideaDetailSheet conviction .help.
**Updated test pins:** `StockSageExpectedValueTests.assumedWinBandLabelMatchesBandEndpoints` (new). `StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.copyTextParsesWithSymbolVelocityAndStop` — existing `text.contains("R/day")` still passes with "R/day gross" (no pin change needed).
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full suite, 0 failures, +1 new band-constant test).

## 2026-07-02 — WAVE 12 RESIDUAL FIXES: 9 small display/copy/test/doc fixes (adversarial verify follow-up)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageExpectedValue.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageTodayPlan.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageTodayPlanRankedTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_LOG.md`.
**What & why:** Residual fixes from the wave-12 adversarial verify pass. Zero ranking/numeric/decision change; all display, copy, test, or doc only.
- **Fix 1 (OVERFLOW):** `ideaMetric` value Text gains `.lineLimit(1)` + `.minimumScaleFactor(0.75)` so 6-metric crypto rows scale instead of truncating; label Text gets `.lineLimit(1)`; plan-numerics HStack spacing 20→14 (that row only).
- **Fix 2 (EVIDENCE GATE):** Extended Evidence header gate from `stopPrice != nil && targetPrice != nil` to also fire when `store.backtestSymbol == idea.symbol` — prevents stop-less idea's backtest rendering header-less between Why and Context.
- **Fix 3 (SEPARATOR):** Sheet velocity line changed `gross - net` separator to ` · ` (house convention) so a negative net value no longer reads as subtraction (`gross - -0.008R/day net`).
- **Fix 4a (EXPORT GROSS TAG — playbook):** `StockSageExpectedValue.playbook()` best-bet line: `est. EV +X.XXR.` → `est. EV +X.XXR (gross).` — copy path now matches the moneyVelocityCard tagged stat.
- **Fix 4b (EXPORT GROSS TAG — build):** `StockSageTodayPlan.build()` best-bet line: `est. EV %+.2fR` → `est. EV %+.2fR (gross)` — both Copy-today's-plan buttons now match the CTA's evText.
- **Fix 5 (DEPLOY-CAPITAL PARITY):** Deploy-capital card's per-position EV metric display and its copy-text line: `%+.2fR` → `%+.2fR (gross)`.
- **Fix 6 (HELP DUPLICATION):** CALIBRATED `.help` branch (ideaDetailSheet conviction meter + ideaCard conviction tooltip): `rules-based signal strength` → `a rules-based score` (same reword as wave-12 item 11 applied to uncalibrated branch; now consistent).

## 2026-07-02 — Audit hygiene batch: 5 LOW-severity fixes F33/F34/F35/F38/F39
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageAdvisor.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageConvictionCalibration.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageQuoteService.swift`, `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageEarnings.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSagePortfolioAnalyticsTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageAdvisorTests.swift`, `Salehman AITests/StockSageConvictionCalibrationTests.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:** Five LOW-severity hygiene fixes from AUDIT_2026-07-02_ideas_board.md (F33–F39 subset).
- **F33 (PortfolioAnalytics.correlation n<2):** Changed `guard n >= 2 else { return 0 }` to `guard n >= 2 else { return nil }` — consistent "undefined" convention for n<2, same as the zero-variance case. Caller verification: all 3 call sites pre-guard to n≥2 (ClusterCheck guard≥2, Precheck minOverlap=5, laneCorrelation filter{≥2}), so live behavior is byte-identical. Added test `correlationIsNilNotZeroForInsufficientOverlap` pinning nil for n=0, n=1, and confirming n=2 returns non-nil.
- **F34 (Advisor score→action boundary asymmetry):** Added a 14-line comment at the score→action mapping explaining the deliberate long-side bias (+0.5→.strongBuy inclusive, −0.5→.reduce not .sell; shorts carry financing cost + unlimited-loss asymmetry requiring a stronger signal). Added `actionForScore(_:trending:)` (nonisolated static) and two tests `scoreBoundaryPlusFiftyIsStrongBuy` / `scoreBoundaryMinusFiftyIsReduceNotSell` pinning both boundaries. **Orchestrator correction before merge:** the helper as first written was an UNCALLED MIRROR of advise()'s inline switch — the tests would have pinned a copy that could drift from the live path. Fixed extract-and-call: advise() now calls `Self.actionForScore(score, trending:)` (single source of truth; the inline switch is gone), so the boundary pins cover the real path. Behavior byte-identical (same switch body). No numeric change.
- **F35 (ConvictionCalibration always-true guard + dead width):** Converted `if train.count >= minTrainSamples` (always true by the entry guard) to `assert(...)` documenting the invariant. Removed dead `let width = …` / `_ = width` pair. Wrapped the body in `do { }`. Added test `selectCalibrationInvariantHoldsAtMinimumSampleCount` (n=44, minimum passing boundary) confirming the assert never fires. Zero behavior change.
- **F38 (QuoteService path encoding):** Changed `addingPercentEncoding(withAllowedCharacters: .urlPathAllowed)` to `.urlHostAllowed` in both `fetchOne` and `fetchHistory` — stricter set prevents a symbol containing '/' from silently rewriting the URL path segment. Verified: no curated symbol contains '/' (Berkshire is `BRK-B`, not `BRK/B`). Live behavior byte-identical. Unified with StockSageEarnings which already used `.urlHostAllowed`.
- **F39 (UA constant deduplication):** Promoted `StockSageQuoteService.ua` from `private` to `nonisolated` (internal). Changed `StockSageEarnings.ua` to `StockSageQuoteService.ua` reference instead of a byte-identical literal. MediaSearch and WebTools/MediaTranscribe have their own copies outside the Markets fence — intentionally left untouched. One change site for any future Yahoo UA update.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (full suite, 0 failures).

## 2026-07-02 — Skill-library fix pass: 3 review findings the parked fixer never applied (hand-fixed)
**Files:** `skills/debugging-guide/SKILL.md`, `skills/visual-qa/SKILL.md`, `skills/testing-discipline/SKILL.md`.
**What & why:** The skill-library workflow's reviewer found 3 defects but the fixer agent died at the usage limit; handoff queued them for hand-fixing.
- **debugging-guide:** the failed-test extraction pipeline `grep -E "Test case '.*' failed" | sed -E "s/.*'([^']+)'.*/\1/"` was wrong — greedy `.*` makes sed capture the LAST quoted segment, which on swift-testing xcodebuild lines (`Test case 'X/y()' failed on 'My Mac…'`) is the run DESTINATION, not the test id. Replaced with anchored extraction `grep -hoE "Test [Cc]ase '[^']+' failed" | sed -E "s/Test [Cc]ase '([^']+)' failed/\1/"` — takes the quoted segment immediately before " failed", covers both swift-testing and XCTest capitalizations, `-h` drops filename prefixes from the multi-file glob. Verified by measurement on a synthetic two-format log: old printed `My Mac - Salehman AI (12345)`; new prints both real test identifiers.
- **visual-qa:** claimed `calibrationChip` has "5 call sites (sheet EV, best-opp, deploy, velocity card, board)" — live count is 4 (best-opp ~3360, deploy capital ~3452, money velocity ~3534, sheet EV ~4677; definition ~3507). "Board" was phantom. Corrected count, list, and the definition line ref (~3505→~3507).
- **testing-discipline:** the NetEdge reward-40 incident was dated 2026-07-01; it actually happened in the 2026-07-02 "Week-horizon research roadmap #2" entry (verified against this log). Corrected to 2026-07-02 with the entry name.
**Result:** Markdown-only, no Swift source touched — no build impact possible. Each fix verified against ground truth before editing (synthetic-log measurement / live grep of MarketsView.swift / this log). Merged after CI green on the fix branch.

## 2026-07-02 — UX wave: Dynamic-Type font-token sweep (F48) + ideaMetric/summaryStat merge (F24), adversarial-verify fix pass
**Files:** `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift`, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`.
**What & why:** Display-only accessibility pass on MarketsView (worked in an isolated worktree, branch `ux-wave`, concurrently with another agent editing the same file on a different branch for honesty-label copy — zero string/copy overlap by construction). Two changes bundled in this wave:
- **F48 (Dynamic-Type token sweep):** Replaced hardcoded `.font(.system(size: N))` literals throughout MarketsView's chrome/alerts/portfolio/risk sections with the existing `mvFontN` `@ScaledMetric(relativeTo: .caption2)` tokens, and added 5 new tokens the sweep needed: `mvFont12_5`, `mvFont17`, `mvFont18`, `mvFont20`, `mvFont22` (each defaults to its literal numeric value, so rendered size at the default Dynamic-Type setting is byte-identical to the pre-sweep hardcoded literal — the only behavior change is that these labels now scale with the system's Dynamic-Type/caption2 preference, which is the intended a11y gain, not a regression). `mvFont11_5` and `mvFont12_5` keep their own unrounded tokens (not folded into mvFont11/12/13) so the "Find ideas" button and `ideaMetric`'s value text keep their exact pre-sweep default size.
- **F24 (ideaMetric/summaryStat merge):** `summaryStat` (a near-duplicate of `ideaMetric` with no accessibility grouping) was merged into `ideaMetric`, which gained an optional trailing `sub`/`subColor` pair: `ideaMetric(_:_:color:sub:subColor:)`. The `sub`-path reproduces `summaryStat`'s rendering byte-for-byte (uppercased label, bold rounded value, colored sub-line); all 3 former `summaryStat` call sites now get `ideaMetric`'s combined accessibility label. `summaryStat` itself is deleted.
**Adversarial-verify fixes (this pass, MUST-FIX):**
1. **Build break in `BestOpportunityActionCard`:** the F48 token sweep touched that struct's font literals too, but `BestOpportunityActionCard` is a standalone `View` struct (not a `MarketsView` method) — it cannot see `MarketsView`'s `private @ScaledMetric` properties, so the sweep left 8 `cannot find 'mvFontN' in scope` compile errors (mvFont10/11/12/13/15). Fixed by following the struct's own established pattern (it already carried `var mvFont9: CGFloat = 9` injected from the `bestOpportunityCTA` call site): added `var mvFont10/11/12/13/15: CGFloat = <literal default>` parameters, injected from the same call site (`BestOpportunityActionCard(... mvFont9: mvFont9, mvFont10: mvFont10, mvFont11: mvFont11, mvFont12: mvFont12, mvFont13: mvFont13, mvFont15: mvFont15)`). Default values match the pre-sweep literals exactly, and the values are now the parent's live `@ScaledMetric` readings, so default-Dynamic-Type rendered size is unchanged and the card now scales with the rest of the file.
2. **This log entry** — backfilling the missing wave entry per the standing owner directive (log every change, no exceptions).
3. **MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md "shared helpers" entry** — updated to document `ideaMetric(_:_:color:sub:subColor:)` (was the pre-F24 `ideaMetric(_:_:color:)`) and the summaryStat merge/deletion; the Gotchas line's `@ScaledMetric` token range corrected from `mvFont7–mvFont13` to `mvFont7–mvFont22` (plus the unrounded `mvFont11_5`/`mvFont12_5`), and a note added that `BestOpportunityActionCard` takes plain injected `mvFontN` parameters rather than its own `@ScaledMetric` properties.
**Result:** ✅ ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, ✅ ** TEST SUCCEEDED ** (`-only-testing:'Salehman AITests'`, isolated derived-data at `ux-dd`). Zero ranking/numeric/decision/copy change; scope held to `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` + `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` per the fence (no honesty-label strings touched, consistent with the concurrent branch's ownership of that copy).
**Landing (post-calc compose):** squash-merged onto post-calc main (`f916770`); the 3 expected MarketsView conflicts hand-composed by the orchestrator — Est./week weekly line = merged `ideaMetric` helper + calc's "gross, if you run top 3" sub + F03/F44 `.help`; strategy-backtest calibration status = calc's method-keyed assumed/fitted/measured text + `mvFont11` token on the icon; EV-tag comment = calc's F01/F02 method-keyed prose with the ephemeral `hc#1` prefix dropped per F49. Post-compose checks: zero conflict markers, `summaryStat` only in comments, sole raw font literal is the documented decorative size-4 bullet. Full gate re-run green on the composed tree (`** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` / `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 0 failed cases).

It is deliberately EXECUTION-BLOCKED by its own §0.5: the verified manifest `research/UNIVERSE_VERIFIED_2026-07-03.md` and the fetch-timing measurement do NOT exist yet — the verification agent was throttled by Yahoo v8 (HTTP 429) mid-run and, per gated-scope, REFUSED to guess the verified list, the concurrency, or the watchdog value. Sizing rules are pinned as formulas (concurrency = highest measured c with rate429<1% AND p90≤1.25×p90(6); watchdog = max(120, ceil(ceil(1024/C)×p90×1.5 /30)×30)s, >600s ⇒ STOP-for-owner) to be filled from a real measurement, not a guess. DELIBERATE: did NOT retry-hammer Yahoo — the app's own StockSageQuoteService fetches the same endpoint, so aggressive verification risks throttling the live app. RESUME PATH: retry verification at a gentle rate (~1 req/2s + backoff) once the throttle clears, OR enable the installed EODHD plugin (owner token) as an independent verification source; then the plan's §0.5 precondition is met and implementation runs.

## 2026-07-03 — Test hardening: pin returnShape's `>= 30` count boundary AT the minimum (F05 off-by-one companion)
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageReturnShapeTests.swift`.
**What & why:** `StockSageReturnShape.returnShape` guards `returns.count >= 30` (StockSageReturnShape.swift:49). The suite already pinned nil BELOW the minimum (`tooFewReturnsReturnsNil`, 29 returns) but nothing pinned non-nil AT exactly 30, so an off-by-one to `>= 31`/`> 30` would have passed silently — the exact case `testing-discipline`'s F05 pattern exists to catch ("also test non-nil AT the minimum"). Added `returnShapeCountBoundaryStraddlesThirty`: 29 vs 30 NON-FLAT returns (`rets[0] = -0.03` → sd 0.00574 > 0) so the second `guard sd > 0` (line 57) cannot be what flips nil — isolating the COUNT guard. Fixtures derived independently in `scratchpad/derive_returnshape_boundary.swift` (printed 29→nil · 30→non-nil · sd>0), never by calling the engine under test (gate 3). Found by a coverage audit of the honesty-floor nil-guards prompted by the loaded `stocksage-mental-model` + `testing-discipline` skills — the other guards (volStability 146/147, volRegime 273, momentumQuality 20/21, the 50:1 rewardR cap at ExpectedValueTests:371) already had straddles; returnShape's at-minimum companion was the only gap.
**Result:** ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (0 failures); named case verified in the log: `StockSageReturnShapeTests/returnShapeCountBoundaryStraddlesThirty() passed`. Test-only, no production code touched.

## 2026-07-03 — Frog-in-the-pan momentum-quality filter: net-of-cost REAL-DATA ablation → NULL (docs-only)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_frog_in_the_pan_ablation.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md` (dated line under Detail files).
**What & why:** Autonomous ablation of the Da-Gurun-Warachka (2014, RFS) information-discreteness ("frog in the pan") momentum-quality filter — the top-ranked un-tried candidate from the 2026-07-03 new-edge design pass. ID = sign(formRet)×(%neg−%pos) over the 126-bar formation window; low-ID (continuous) vs unconditional momentum on a frozen 24-name/6-sector + global/Saudi panel, 5y Yahoo daily, no look-ahead, block-level significance + horizon sweep (21/42/63/126d), net-of-cost via the real `StockSageNetEdge.defaultCosts`, DSR from the COMPILED REAL `StockSageDeflatedSharpe` (trials=20). VERDICT NULL: the FIP direction is present (jumpy/high-ID winners near-flat forward vs continuous) but never block-significant (all p>0.22) and NO config clears DSR>0.95 (best absolute 0.684 = bull beta; best incremental 0.094). The hypothesised mechanism (turnover reduction) doesn't bite at 1–6mo holds — per-trade cost 13–30bp ≪ 120–820bp holding return. Engine UNCHANGED; no ID filter added. Reproduces the standing prior. Method fidelity per `ablation-harness`; recorded per `research-memory` (a null is a deliverable). Data via Yahoo v8 (concurrency 1, ~2s, no 429 encountered).
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). Detail file + INDEX line; a documented NULL closes the FIP-filter question.

## 2026-07-03 — StockSageHistoryCache SHIPPED: disk history cache unblocks offline net-cost validation (autonomous 12h window, Claude #4)
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageHistoryCache.swift` (new), `Salehman AITests/StockSageHistoryCacheTests.swift` (new, 6 cases), `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStore.swift` (one detached save call after the histories fetch), `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md` (new entry), `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-history-cache-design.md` (design, committed a9ff92b).
**What & why:** Superpowers flow (brainstorm→spec→implement) on the O8 proposal. O6 found the app persists last-good *quotes* but NEVER price histories (`fetchHistories` is network-only, its result discarded after `buildIdeas`) → `StockSageNetCostSim` had 0 usable panels offline → the DSR>0.95 edge campaign was hostage to the Yahoo throttle. Fix: a Codable disk cache mirroring `StockSageQuoteCache` — full OHLCV per symbol, JSON, Application Support, trimmed to last 252 bars + universe-evicted, `savedAt` + a timezone-free `isStale` gate, `schemaVersion` guard (mismatch→nil→clean re-fetch), and a `panel(from:industryOf:)` builder that aligns closes on the SHARED date-axis intersection (no look-ahead) for the sim. The write path is ONE `Task.detached` best-effort save right after the successful bulk fetch in `refreshIdeas` — ZERO new network (persists bytes already downloaded and otherwise discarded), off the main actor so the ~19MB encode never blocks `buildIdeas`. **Owner-decisions taken (documented in the spec, each a 1-line flip):** full OHLCV / JSON / T=5 / 1y window / **sim-only consumption — NO offline board**, so nothing user-visible changes ⇒ no visual-QA gate (safe to land with owner away). No engine-math / EV / ranking change; held gates (F01/F02, F03, RANKING #10, cost-table) untouched.
**Result:** ✅ Typecheck clean · ✅ `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` (0 failures). All 6 new cases verified passed in the log incl. `cachedHistoriesEnableTheNetCostSim` — the concrete O6 unblock (a cache built from real-shaped candles → panel → `simulate` returns non-nil with ≥4 rebalances, vs 0 panels before). Fixtures hand-derived (trim 400→last-252 = 149…400; F05 staleness straddle at day 7/8; schema-version + malformed-JSON → nil). Reversible (delete the file + the one save call). NEXT: with histories now persisted, an offline net-cost edge-validation run becomes possible once a live refresh populates the cache (no longer throttle-blocked for the *validation* step).

## 2026-07-03 — Low-beta / low-IVOL LONG-tilt (defensive/BAB anomaly): net-of-cost REAL-DATA ablation → NULL (docs-only)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_low_beta_ablation.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md` (dated line under Detail files).
**What & why:** Second autonomous ablation of the 2026-07-03 new-edge candidate set (candidate #2). Tested the Frazzini-Pedersen "Betting Against Beta" (2014) / Baker-Bradley-Wurgler (2011) defensive anomaly as a cross-sectional LONG-ONLY ranking tilt (prefer bottom-tercile realized-beta, and separately low idiosyncratic-vol, vs unconditional equal-weight long) on 18 US large-caps + ^GSPC (Saudi/global dotted names excluded from the ^GSPC-beta cross-section — async-trading stale-price bias would fake-fill the low-beta cohort). 5y Yahoo daily, no look-ahead, block-level paired-t + horizon sweep (21/42/63/126d), net 13bps, DSR from the COMPILED REAL `StockSageDeflatedSharpe` (trials=24, selection-deflated). VERDICT NULL — and worse: NO config clears DSR>0.95 (best 0.227) and the incremental LOW−BASE return is significantly NEGATIVE at 7/8 horizons (−131 to −1156 bp/block, t −2.0 to −3.0). ALPHA-vs-RISK: not alpha (negative increment) and not a clean risk win (LOW block-Sharpe below BASE everywhere; drawdown often worse from 6-name concentration) — and the only real benefit, variance/drawdown reduction, is ALREADY owned by the engine's vol-targeting (cryptoRiskScaler + vol-regime brake) at the sizing layer. Consistent with BAB's structure (alpha in the short-high-beta leg + leverage; the unlevered long leg thin/regime-dependent). NOT a disproof of the academic anomaly (single 2021–26 high-beta-led bull; long leg only). Engine UNCHANGED; no low-beta tilt added. Method per `ablation-harness`; recorded per `research-memory`.
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). Detail file + INDEX line; a documented NULL closes the low-beta/BAB long-tilt candidate #2 as refused.

## 2026-07-03 — Two new-edge ablations → both NULL (docs-only): residual momentum + downside-beta asymmetry
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_residual_momentum_ablation.md` (new), `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_downside_beta_ablation.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md` (2 dated lines).
**What & why:** Continuous autonomous ablation pipeline (owner directive "never stop 12h"), candidates #5 and #1 of the vetted queue, both on the cached 18-US+^GSPC panel (reused, ZERO new Yahoo). (1) RESIDUAL MOMENTUM (Blitz-Huij-Martens 2011): NULL — no config clears DSR>0.95 (best 0.694), incremental over plain 12-1 momentum NEGATIVE at all horizons (−37 to −580 bp/block, all |t|<1), and the turnover-reduction hypothesis is FALSIFIED (residual momentum turned over ~2× FASTER — standardizing by residual vol amplifies noise). Drawdown modestly lower at 3/4 horizons (paper's vol-claim, directional) but never nets out. (2) DOWNSIDE-BETA / SEMI-BETA (Ang-Chen-Xing 2006): NULL — LOW_DB collapses into the symmetric low-beta null (indistinguishable, p=0.54–0.73); LOW_ASYM (β⁻−β⁺) directionally distinct but underpowered (p=0.11–0.16) and fails DSR (best 0.517); headline caveat = the 5y window is a sustained bull with no bear regime, the exact condition the premium needs. Both: real DSR compiled from source (bit-identical low-beta cross-check confirms panel fidelity), block significance, McLean-Pontiff noted, honest "did NOT establish". Engine UNCHANGED. Method per `ablation-harness`; recorded per `research-memory` (nulls are deliverables).
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). 2 detail files + 2 INDEX lines; two documented NULLs close candidates #5 (residual momentum) and #1 (downside beta) of the new-edge queue. Running tally this session: 4 indexed NULLs (frog, low-beta, residual-mom, downside-beta).

**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_intermediate_momentum_ablation.md` (new), `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_momentum_crash_conditioning_ablation.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md` (2 dated lines).

(1) INTERMEDIATE / "GAP" MOMENTUM (Novy-Marx 2012): NULL — the market-neutral INTERM−RECENT spread is negative at every horizon (−0.02 to −3.41 %/block), never significant (all p≥0.16), DSR=0.000 everywhere (overlapping cross-check confirms the sign); same momentum factor at an older lag, not a distinct incremental edge over the engine's shipped recent-momentum term. (2) MOMENTUM-CRASH-STATE CONDITIONING (Daniel-Moskowitz 2016): INSUFFICIENT SAMPLE — the crash-state (24mo mkt<0 AND 1mo vol<2y-median) fired EXACTLY ONCE (fall 2023) in the 5y bull panel, so efficacy is untestable (no power); correctly refused to declare a null. But a VALUABLE MECHANISM finding survives: the overlay is NOT redundant with the engine's vol controls — varianceScalar measured 1.0 (no-op) precisely in the crash-state, so it targets an unaddressed axis (vol-trajectory vs vol-level). Needs a multi-regime panel (2008/2020) to test — recorded as residual/open, do NOT add without that data or owner sign-off. Both: real DSR compiled from source, block significance, honest limitations. Engine UNCHANGED. Method per `ablation-harness`; recorded per `research-memory`.
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). 2 detail files + 2 INDEX lines. Session tally: 5 indexed NULLs (frog, low-beta, residual-mom, downside-beta, gap-mom) + 1 documented INSUFFICIENT-SAMPLE (momentum-crash, mechanism non-redundant). New-edge campaign base rate holds — no proven edge; engine value stays risk-discipline.

## 2026-07-03 — Trend-acceleration ablation → NULL + momentum-crash multi-regime 15y re-run → REFUSE (docs-only)

Notable: accel/MACD correlation r=0.089 (near-zero) → accel is statistically DISTINCT from the shipped MACD term but adds no net incremental edge (a distinct-but-not-additive signal, not a hidden duplicate). (2) MOMENTUM-CRASH OVERLAY multi-regime re-run (the documented next step for the prior insufficient-sample result): built a 25-US-large-cap+^GSPC 15y panel (2011→2026, gentle Yahoo, no 429) and re-ran the Daniel-Moskowitz crash-state overlay. Upgraded insufficient-sample → REFUSE/lean-refute: even 15y fires the state only TWICE (both 2023 — the "2y-negative AND calm-vol" definition structurally can't fire in fast-vol crashes like 2020 or melt-up-preceded declines like 2022), and where it fires the overlay is net-NEGATIVE and sign-stable (best DSR 0.0007, wrong side; both episodes were momentum continuations, flattening hurt). The non-redundancy mechanism (varianceScalar=1.0 in-state) REPLICATED. A real efficacy test still needs a *fired* slow-bleed-into-second-leg crash (2000-03/2008) — pre-2011 data, owner-scoped. (3) Queue #10 vol-managed momentum CLOSED BY ANALYSIS (not run): it IS the shipped `varianceScalar` (targetVol 0.20 attenuation on the trend family) — nothing new to ablate. Engine UNCHANGED throughout. Method per `ablation-harness`; recorded per `research-memory`.
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). 1 new detail file + 1 detail-file UPDATE + 2 INDEX changes. Session tally: 8 indexed NULLs (frog, low-beta, residual-mom, downside-beta, gap-mom, 52wh, MAX, trend-accel) + momentum-crash upgraded to REFUSE via multi-regime re-run. The vetted price-only new-edge queue is now COMPREHENSIVELY SWEPT (9 candidates run/refused, #3 seasonality deferred needing 10y+ same-month history, #6 industry-momentum runnable on the new 25-name panel). Campaign conclusion holds: NO proven edge; engine value is risk-discipline. The one non-redundant mechanism surfaced (crash-state vol-trajectory) is net-negative where testable.

The engine's value is risk-discipline, not alpha. One non-redundant mechanism surfaced (momentum-crash vol-trajectory conditioning) but it is net-negative where testable and needs a fired 2000-03/2008 crash to test properly. ## 2026-07-03 — Defensive anomalies multi-regime DRAWDOWN re-run → nuanced NULL confirmed (docs-only)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_low_beta_ablation.md` (UPDATE section), `research/INDEX.md` (low-beta + downside-beta entries each get a dated UPDATE clause).
**What & why:** Followed the documented residual BOTH the low-beta and downside-beta ablations explicitly named — a re-run on a window containing a genuine drawdown regime (their 5y panel was all-bull, the wrong regime for a DEFENSIVE signal). Re-ran symmetric-beta/IVOL/downside-beta/asymmetry LOW-vs-BASE on the 25-name/15y multi-regime panel (2011-2026, incl. COVID-2020, the 2022 bear, 2018Q4; reused, no fetch), real DSR, drawdown-period decomposition. RESULT: full-period NULL CONFIRMED (no config clears DSR>0.95; LOW−BASE still negative in bulls). Informative new decomposition: the defensive tilt shows REAL protection in the FAST 2020 COVID crash (+87 to +419bp/blk) and 2018Q4, but is ABSENT/negative in the SLOW rate-hike 2022 bear — so "defensive" is regime-selective even within drawdowns; and even where protection is real, giving up bull return still sinks the full-period net DSR (best drawdown-only 0.346, n=5–18 thin). asymmetry (β⁻−β⁺) is the noisier, less protective leg. Not a positive; closes the drawdown-regime residual with a nuanced (not flat) null. Real DSR compiled from source, block significance, honest thin-n/survivorship caveats. Engine UNCHANGED.
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). 1 detail-file UPDATE + 2 INDEX UPDATE clauses. The defensive-anomaly thread is now fully closed (5y-bull + 15y-multi-regime both tested). Campaign conclusion unchanged: no price-only single signal clears the net-of-cost bar; engine value is risk-discipline.

## 2026-07-03 — Momentum-crash overlay 2008-inclusive re-run → REFUTE (residual resolved AGAINST the overlay, docs-only)
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-03_momentum_crash_conditioning_ablation.md` (2008 UPDATE section), `research/INDEX.md` (momentum-crash entry UPDATE clause).
**What & why:** Ran the definitive test the momentum-crash entry named — the overlay on a 20-name+^GSPC panel back to 2004 (5660 bars) so it CONTAINS the canonical Daniel-Moskowitz 2008-09 momentum crash. This was the ONE non-redundant candidate from the 11-ablation sweep; the 5y/15y runs couldn't test its efficacy (crash-state never fired in a real crash). RESULT: NULL upgraded to REFUTE with an airtight structural diagnostic — the crash-state fired 0 bars during Sep-2008→May-2009 because its "calm-vol" clause (1mo vol < 2y median) is FALSE through every high-vol crash (2008 vol 22-82% ≫ median); first firing 2009-06, 3mo AFTER the reversal. So the overlay is MIS-TIMED BY CONSTRUCTION — it can only act in calm recovery/continuation, where flattening is now BLOCK-SIGNIFICANTLY net-negative (21d −11.3bp/blk p=0.005, 42d p=0.013; best DSR 0.132≪0.95). Not for lack of power this time — the definition is structurally wrong for its target. Non-redundancy mechanism replicated (varianceScalar ~no-op in-state) but efficacy is negative → do NOT wire. Caveat: 20y survivorship excludes 2008-collapsed names (the overlay's target), so this liquid-survivor panel is conservative; only a CRSP-grade delisting-inclusive panel could revisit (owner/Fable-scoped). Real DSR compiled from source, block significance, honest caveats. Engine UNCHANGED.
**Result:** Docs-only (no Swift). 1 detail-file UPDATE + 1 INDEX UPDATE clause. **The momentum-crash thread — the campaign's last open lead — is now definitively closed (5y insufficient → 15y REFUSE → 20y/2008 REFUTE).** The entire single-signal new-edge campaign is comprehensively concluded: no price-only signal clears the net-of-cost bar; the one non-redundant mechanism is mis-timed-by-construction and net-negative. Engine value is risk-discipline; no proven alpha. Every genuine documented residual is now resolved — further progress is strictly owner/data-scoped (CRSP-grade panels, or the shipped-engine baseline measurement in another session's lane).

## 2026-07-07 — Money-math multi-model verification wave: 3-way blind derivation + invariant sweeps + reference docs (+ UX-drift finding)
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageMoneyInvariantSweepTests.swift` (NEW), `KELLY_INVARIANTS.md` (NEW), `NETEDGE_REFERENCE.md` (NEW), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen).
**What & why:** Executed the `ORCHESTRATION_PROMPT_2026-07-07.md` money-math wave. Since the Claude Code agent harness only spawns Claude models, the plan's DeepSeek/Gemini roles were run via the user's OWN paid API keys (extracted from VS Code's local secret store into the app Keychain, `SA.Salehman-AI` service, `deepseek-api-key`/`gemini-api-key`; a text-in/out relay at `scratchpad/bridge.sh` — models cannot drive tools, so all edits/gates stayed on the orchestrator per the Grok-relay discipline: never paste external code, always verify).
- **Verification (3 independent blind derivations):** Opus 4.8 + Fable 5 (Workflow `wf_616ad353-164`) + Gemini all derived the 8 core money formulas from first principles WITHOUT reading the code, then an Opus pass compared code vs both derivation sets. **Verdict: ZERO real bugs.** All 4 code-vs-derivation divergences classified INTENTIONAL/COSMETIC, each with a ratifying doc-comment + pinning test (the 50:1 rewardR cap on netReward; the −1R clean-stop EV contract with gap-honesty handled separately in `simulateExit`; raw-kurtosis DSR convention; sizer's no-notional-cap "size by the loss" design). 7 of 8 safety invariants already test-pinned; the 8th (`sizingBias ∈ [0.40,1.25]`) only partially (crisis 0.25 pinned, band bounds NOT — follow-up below). Three-way derivations agree on every closed form and break-even (`p*=1/(R+1)`; `f*=0.325`, half `0.1625`; half-Kelly≈75% growth is an approximation exact only as `f*→0`).
- **DeepSeek V4 Pro** proposed three parametric invariant tests. Its draft had the expected external-model defects (XCTest `#expect(…,accuracy:)` which doesn't exist in Swift Testing; wrong type names `PositionSizer`/`fullFraction`; and a units-wrong `netRR=2−costBps·0.002`, real value at 13bps is 1.923977 not 1.974). - **Gemini** authored `KELLY_INVARIANTS.md` + `NETEDGE_REFERENCE.md` (formulas verified against code + the hand-derivations; added the dollar-space + 50:1-cap notes so NETEDGE matches the actual `evaluate` implementation, not just the idealized R-space form).
