## 2026-07-07 — Test hardening (overnight loop): pin the sizingBias band clamps at their exact bounds
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageRegimeTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen).
**What & why:** The 2026-07-07 money-math verification flagged invariant #8 (`sizingBias ∈ [0.40, 1.25]`) as only PARTIAL — crisis 0.25 was pinned but the band edges were not (the existing regime tests only assert loose `> 1.0` / `< 0.75`). Added `sizingBiasBandClampsAtExactBounds`: derived the reachability (`scratchpad/derive_regime_band.swift`) — max-bear non-crisis score −0.90 → raw `0.75−0.45 = 0.30` CLAMPED UP to **0.40** (genuine straddle: raw 0.30 sits below the floor, so the clamp is load-bearing), and max-bull non-crisis score +0.85 → **1.175**, which is the max REACHABLE bias and sits below the 1.25 upper clamp (the cap is deliberately slack, never binds). Test pins both exact values + the `.trendingBear`/`.trendingBull` states from constructed 250-bar series. Non-vacuous: the 0.40 assertion fails (0.10 ≫ 1e-9) if the lower clamp were removed.
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only. Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; new case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 test, engine unchanged. Invariant #8 now fully pinned (crisis 0.25 + lower-clamp 0.40 + reachable-max 1.175 all locked).

Derived in `scratchpad/derive_gate_decision.swift` from the SPEC (not the code): clearIdea A entry100/stop95/target120 → netRR **3.873294**, clearIdea B entry100/stop97/target115 → netRR **4.750799** (US no-suffix 13bps: `(min(grossRR,50)·grossRisk − 13/10000·entry)/(grossRisk + …)`), both ≥2 ⇒ positive-skew PASS; riskFraction 0.01 ≤ 0.02 cap PASS; hasStop PASS; corr/earnings nil ⇒ no warn/fail ⇒ `.clear` by the documented rollup (`fail?blocked : warn?caution : clear`).

**Result:** +3 assertions, engine unchanged. The gate test now pins the decision VALUE and can no longer pass on an empty plan list.

## 2026-07-07 — Robustness investigation (overnight loop): two catalog NaN/finite findings are FALSE POSITIVES; pinned the winProb clamp
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageMoneyInvariantSweepTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine change.
**What & why:** The 2026-07-07 money-math catalog flagged two "UNGUARDED" robustness risks. Investigated both empirically (`scratchpad/test_nan_clamp.swift`, `derive_priorwinprob_band.swift`) — BOTH are false positives:
- **`StockSageExpectedValue.priorWinProb` NaN — already safe.** The claim was `max(0, min(1, .nan))` doesn't clamp. Empirically it DOES: Swift's `min`/`max` return the NON-NaN operand, and `conviction` is the SECOND arg to `min(1, conviction)`, so `min(1, NaN) = 1.0 → max(0,1)=1 → 0.58` (finite). Output is finite AND in [0.35, 0.58] for EVERY input class (0/0.5/1/−5/5/+Inf/−Inf/NaN → 0.35/0.465/0.58/0.35/0.58/0.58/0.35/0.58). A NaN conviction thus can't escape as a non-finite win-prob, so evR and the rank comparator stay finite. No guard needed.
- **`StockSageNetEdge.evaluate` non-finite — unreachable.** Every caller (CapitalAllocator:68, ExpectedValue:224/264, TodayPlan:25/107, MarketsView:4422/4444/4561/4719) passes `idea.price` + the idea's Advisor-derived ATR stop/target — never a user-editable/parsed field. `PositionSizer.size` guards `.isFinite` precisely because IT takes the user's account/risk inputs; `evaluate` doesn't take any. No `.isFinite` guard needed (ponytail YAGNI — no dead guard for an unreachable input).
- **What I DID add (regression lock, not a dead guard):** `priorWinProbStaysInBandForAnyInputIncludingNaN` pins the load-bearing behavior — the clamp keeps output finite and in the assumed [0.35, 0.58] band for NaN/±Inf/out-of-range. Guards against a future reorder to `min(conviction, 1)` (which WOULD leak NaN, per Swift's arg-order NaN semantics). Hand-derived exacts, `.isFinite` + band checks on the extreme inputs.
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only, documents a null investigation (fact-discipline: a null is a win, index it). Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 invariant test; both catalog robustness findings closed as false-positive/unreachable (do not re-file). Engine unchanged.

## 2026-07-07 — Test hardening (overnight loop): pin cVaR95 (expected shortfall) — was UNTESTED
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSagePortfolioAnalyticsTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine change.
**What & why:** Spot-check found `StockSagePortfolioAnalytics.cVaR95` (1-day conditional VaR / expected shortfall — "if you lose, HOW bad?", added earlier this session) had ZERO test references anywhere in the suite, despite two branches: the normal tail-mean `max(0, −mean(tail)·100)` and the `guard !tail.isEmpty else { return var95 }` zero-tail fallback. Added `conditionalVaRAveragesTheTailAndFallsBackToVaRWhenTailIsEmpty` pinning BOTH (single-holding ⇒ port == dailyReturns(closes); hand-derived in `scratchpad/derive_cvar.swift`):
- NORMAL: 21 closes → returns [−0.10, +0.01…+0.19]; 5th-pctile cutoff = +0.01 so the lone sub-cutoff return is the −0.10 crash ⇒ tail=[−0.10] ⇒ **cVaR95 = 10.0** while **var95 = 0.0** — the inequality PROVES the tail-mean branch fired (a fallback would make them equal).
- FALLBACK: 6 closes (compute needs minLen ≥ 5) → 5th-pctile idx = round(4·0.05) = 0 = the min, nothing strictly below ⇒ empty tail ⇒ **cVaR95 == var95 == 10.0**.
- STOP-on-mismatch honored: my first fallback fixture ([100,90,95], 2 returns) FAILED the gate because `compute` requires minLen ≥ 5 → returned nil → force-unwrap failed. Per testing-discipline I did NOT edit the assertion — I re-derived, found the min-observations guard, and fixed the FIXTURE (6 closes). The engine was right.
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only. Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; new case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 test; the previously-untested expected-shortfall metric now has both branches pinned with hand-derived values.

## 2026-07-07 — Test hardening (overnight loop): pin netExpectedWeeklyR — was UNTESTED
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageExpectedValueTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine change.
**What & why:** Hunt for untested public surface found `StockSageExpectedValue.netExpectedWeeklyR` (net-of-cost companion to `expectedWeeklyR`, added earlier this session, brief §1.A #4) had ZERO test references. Added `netExpectedWeeklyRSumsNetVelocitiesBelowTheGrossRollup`: a single US-equity idea ⇒ fastLane=[idea], concentration nil ⇒ factor 1.0, so netExpectedWeeklyR = netVelocity·5. Hand-derived in `scratchpad/derive_netweekly.swift` from the SPEC (US 13bps = spread 8 + slippage 5, no financing on a long): cost 0.13, netReward 29.87, netRisk 10.13, p=priorWinProb(0.9)=0.557 ⇒ netEVR=(0.557·29.87 − 0.443·10.13)/10 = **1.215**, netVelocity 1.215/12 = 0.10125 ⇒ **×5 = 0.50625**. Test pins net = 0.50625 AND net < gross (0.5116667) — the gap IS the round-trip cost, which PROVES the net path is a real cost-deducted number, not a relabel of gross.
- Process note: an intermittent runner crash ("Early unexpected exit… signal term before establishing connection") appeared mid-development. Rather than assume my force-unwrap hit nil, I PROBED (wrote the actual optionals to a temp file): net=0.50625, gross=0.5116667, netEVR=1.215, hold=12 — all non-nil and matching the hand-derivation exactly. So the crash was FLAKY (parallel-runner bootstrap), not my assertions; the restored test passes clean. No assertion was edited to match the impl (it already matched).
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only. Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; new case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 test; the net-of-cost weekly-R rollup now has a hand-derived value pin proving it sits below the gross rollup by exactly the cost.

## 2026-07-07 — Test hardening (overnight loop): pin ReturnShape dual-threshold's SECOND clause (the 2% downside floor)
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageReturnShapeTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine change.
**What & why:** §1.A #9 made `isLeftTailed = skewness < −0.5 && downside95 > 0.02` (dual). The existing tests covered (skew≈0 → false) and (deep skew + large downside → true), but NEITHER pinned the discriminating case that makes the `&& downside95 > 0.02` clause load-bearing: strong negative skew WITH a tiny downside. Added `strongNegativeSkewWithTinyDownsideIsNotLeftTailed`: 37×+0.5% + 3×−1.5% returns → skew ≈ **−3.227** (≪ −0.5, clause SATISFIED) but downside95 ≈ **0.015** (≤ 0.02, clause FAILS) ⇒ isLeftTailed must be FALSE. Hand-derived in `scratchpad/derive_leftrail.swift` (returnShape's exact population-variance skew + percentile). Load-bearing: a regression to the old skew-only threshold would make isLeftTailed TRUE and fail this test — it is the ONLY test pinning the 2% downside floor.
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only. Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; new case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 test; the ReturnShape dual threshold is now pinned on BOTH clauses, closing the regression window on the 2% downside floor.

**Known imperfection (documented, queued):** the pinned-bar verdict chip truncates "Do NOT trade" → "Do NOT t…" at the sheet's natural ~450pt width — wave-2's D5 measured the 45pt slot vs an 80pt label ideal and kept mvFont9 to minimize it; the sheet does NOT grow with the window, so the truncation is standard for blocked verdicts. Fix queued in the follow-up wiring wave: unify the chip's blocked label to "Blocked" (the Today family's ratified vocabulary, fits the slot; full verdict stays in the gate section + .help + VoiceOver label).
**Result:** UX Wave 2 is no longer held — merged, gated, QA'd. The 2026-07-06 phantom-entry feature set (dangerSoft sweep, sheet nav, chip unification) is now genuinely in the tree via this wave; remaining from that set: the fastLane e/l UI call-site wiring + `IdeaSort.momentumWeighted` + earningsWarningRow DRY (next wave).

## 2026-07-07 — Test hardening (overnight loop): pin VolStability skip-invalid-window + min-valid-window floor (§1.A #8)
**Files:** `Salehman AITests/StockSageVolStabilityTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine change.
**What & why:** §1.A #8 changed a single invalid rolling-vol window from `return nil` (abort the whole read) to `continue` (SKIP it), and added a min-valid-window floor `series.count >= max(5, Int(126·0.6)) = 75`. The existing VolStability tests all use ALL-VALID series, so NEITHER behavior was pinned. Added `invalidWindowsAreSkippedAndTheMinValidWindowFloorApplies` (throws, `try #require`) exercising both from the proven `flatVolCloses(0.01, 200)` base (201 closes ⇒ 126 rolling windows):
- SKIP: overwrite EXACTLY 21 closes [90...110] with a constant ⇒ precisely ONE window (anchor i=110, slice [90...110]) is fully flat ⇒ vol 0 ⇒ invalid. The OLD abort-on-invalid returns nil; the new skip keeps the other 125 valid windows ⇒ non-nil. `try #require` IS the discriminator (fails under the old behavior).
- MIN-FLOOR: overwrite [80...200] (121 closes) ⇒ >51 windows invalid ⇒ <75 valid ⇒ the line-106 floor returns nil (spotty sample honestly refused, not a fabricated CoV).
- Process: first attempt failed to COMPILE (`try #require` in a non-throwing func — 17 cascade failures); marked the func `throws`, re-gated green. Window-count derivation (1 invalid for a 21-bar block; the anchor slice is closes[i-20...i]) held exactly — non-nil/nil branches both fired as intended.
**Gate check:** No owner gate, no UI, no engine change — additive test only. Full suite `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; new case grepped `passed`.
**Result:** +1 test; VolStability's §1.A #8 skip + 75-valid-window floor now pinned. This closes the last untested §1.A behavior found this session.

## 2026-07-09 — DSR seal #8 honesty fix: the aggregate strategy panel could show a green "PASS" seal at <100 trades
**Files:** `Salehman AI/StockSage/StockSageStrategyBacktest.swift` (+1 computed prop `deflatedSharpeShowsPass` + doc), `Salehman AI/Views/MarketsView.swift` (strategy-backtest DSR row gated on it), `Salehman AITests/StockSageStrategyBacktestTests.swift` (+1 @Test), this log, `MARKETS_TAB_MAP.md`, `SOURCE_BUNDLE.md` (regen). NO engine/ranking/calibration/cost change.
**What & why (ideas-card full-review finding, fresh-eyes pass):** The aggregate strategy-backtest panel rendered its green DSR `checkmark.seal.fill` "PASS" gated ONLY on `deflatedSharpe.passes` (`dsr > 0.95`, which populates at n≥4). `isSignificant` is a pure min-trade floor (`totalTrades >= 100`), so at 4–99 trades with a high small-sample Sharpe the DSR could clear 0.95 and light a green PASS seal **directly beside this panel's own "Only N trades — isn't statistically meaningful yet" verdict** — the exact #8 violation the struct documents at its own `passesHonestSignificance` doc-comment ("a PASS glyph can never sit next to a 'not meaningful yet' verdict"). This is the identical bug class round-g FIX-4 closed for the *per-symbol PSR* one panel over; the *aggregate DSR seal* was left ungated. Fix: new honest predicate `deflatedSharpeShowsPass = (deflatedSharpe?.passes ?? false) && isSignificant`; the DSR row's seal glyph/word/color gate on it, and the word reads "UNPROVEN (sample too small — <100 trades)" when not significant. The DSR NUMBER still always shows (no information withheld) — mirrors round-g's "PSR number always shown, only the glyph/word gated."
**Verification (final tree):** `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **` + `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`, 1768 passed / 0 failed. New case `deflatedSharpeSealRequiresMeaningfulSample` passed by name (4.09s). **Falsifiability-probed:** dropping `&& isSignificant` from the predicate made the test FAIL at class level (`deflatedSharpeSealRequiresMeaningfulSample() failed`), then restored + re-gated green — the straddle (`deflatedSharpe.passes == true` while `deflatedSharpeShowsPass == false` at 50 trades) is load-bearing, not vacuous (IL-23/WHIPPYX class avoided). Fixture booleans are the spec itself (0.99>0.95, 50<100) — no derive script needed.
**Visual QA:** PIXEL-NEUTRAL on the live board — live `totalTrades` ≈ 585 (≥100) ⇒ `isSignificant` true ⇒ the DSR row takes the byte-identical `dpass = passes` / same "PASS/UNPROVEN honest bar" strings it always did. The changed `<100 trades` branch is unreachable with the live ~585-trade backtest (same inherent un-exerciseability as round-g's sibling per-symbol PSR tiny-n corner). No layout/frame/chip change; format-string arg counts verified matched + compiled. App launched + Markets board confirmed rendering; the strategy-backtest panel populates only after a full 24-symbol backtest run (calibration not yet run during the QA scan), so the fixed branch was verified by the falsifiability-probed test rather than pixels.
**Gate check:** No owner gate crossed (display-honesty only; not RANKING #10 / F08 / F03-44 / calibration / cost). This STRENGTHENS the honesty floor (adds a significance guard to a PASS glyph) — the opposite of a weakening. Honesty floor intact: nil stays nil, DSR number still shown, estimate labels untouched.
**Result:** The aggregate DSR PASS seal can no longer sit beside the panel's "not meaningful yet" verdict. Both PASS glyphs in the strategy backtest (HLZ via `passesHonestSignificance`, DSR via `deflatedSharpeShowsPass`) now gate on the ≥100-trade significance floor.

**What & why:** the 2026-07-11 intraday-curve measurement (method pinned pre-result) returned REVISE-ADVISORY under its own decision rule: the close's depth claim PASSED (20.0% of session volume, 3.7× midday) but its range test FAILED (9.7 vs 8.1bp median). ## 2026-07-11 — Research: news-sentiment survey (survey-grade, 3-vote) → REFUSED at survey stage; the last accessible new data class closes
**Files:** `RESEARCH_2026-07-11_news_sentiment_survey.md` (new), `research/INDEX.md` (+1 line), this log. Docs-only; nothing wired, no ablation run (deliberately).
**What & why:** the one genuinely new, on-tier data class (EODHD daily news sentiment). 30-agent workflow (4 finder angles → 3-vote adversarial verification, primary PDFs read; wf_0c79324b-c47). Gross signal confirmed real (SESTM SR 4.29 EW, LLM 34bps/day) but REFUSED for retail on this data by two structural disqualifiers: (D1) signal-side survivorship unfixable — delisted names carry ≈0 sentiment (TUPBQ/BBBYQ 0 rows), no reconstruction → an ablation would be survivorship-lite untestably; (D2) daily aggregated score ≈ f(recent returns, attention, stale reprints) per the Tetlock-2007 endogeneity horse-race (nets to ≈0 over lagged price controls) → not incremental to the price signals already measured null, so an ablation re-derives known nulls. Plus a cost wall at the ratified 13bps tier, liquidity scoping into the uncovered/expensive small-cap corner, and −81% real-time decay. Per gated-scope, a data-blocked test is refused not shipped — so REFUSE-and-index rather than the usual prereg→null.
**Honesty note:** the workflow hit the Fable-5 weekly usage limit mid-Verify (20/32 agents completed — all 4 finders + the load-bearing academic-core votes; the synthesis agent + duplicate votes errored). Synthesis completed directly on Opus 4.8 from the durable journal.jsonl; no claim rests on an un-returned agent; verification tally 0 REFUTED.
**Result:** news sentiment joins price/quality/insider/composite as a closed class; the new-data-class bar is now explicit (incremental-to-price AND survivorship-free coverage). The measured-edge search has no remaining accessible lane; steady state stands.
